94	/-! ## §1. The identity component of a group scheme — abstract substrate
95	
96	The identity component is an abstract feature of `k`-group schemes locally of
97	finite type. We package the definition + open-and-closed-subgroup-scheme
98	structure in the `GroupScheme` namespace so they are reusable outside the
99	Picard context.
100	
101	In this project, a "`k`-group scheme" is encoded as an
102	`Over (Spec (.of k))`-object `G` carrying a `[GrpObj G]` instance (the
103	group-object structure in the over-category). The locally-of-finite-type
104	hypothesis is the `[LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]` instance on the structural
105	morphism to the base.
106	
107	Blueprint references: `def:identity_component_group_scheme` +
108	`thm:identity_component_open_subgroup` (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`). -/
109	
110	namespace GroupScheme
111	
112	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, the set of
113	connected components is finite.
114	
115	Proof: the map `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α` sending each
116	irreducible component `C` to the connected component of an arbitrary chosen
117	point of `C` is well-defined (because `C` is preconnected, hence contained in
118	a single connected component) and surjective (because every point lies in
119	some irreducible component, namely `irreducibleComponent x`, which is a
120	subset of `connectedComponent x`). The source is finite by
121	`TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`, so the
122	target is finite by `Finite.of_surjective`. -/
123	private lemma noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
124	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α] :
125	    Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := by
126	  classical
127	  have hfin : (irreducibleComponents α).Finite :=
128	    TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
129	  haveI : Finite ↥(irreducibleComponents α) := hfin.to_subtype
130	  refine Finite.of_surjective
131	    (fun C : ↥(irreducibleComponents α) =>
132	      (ConnectedComponents.mk : α → ConnectedComponents α)
133	        ((C.property : Maximal IsIrreducible C.val).prop.nonempty.some)) ?_
134	  intro c
135	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe c
136	  refine ⟨⟨irreducibleComponent x, irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents x⟩, ?_⟩
137	  rw [ConnectedComponents.coe_eq_coe]
138	  refine (connectedComponent_eq ?_).symm
139	  exact irreducibleComponent_subset_connectedComponent
140	    (isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent (x := x)).nonempty.some_mem
141	
142	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, each
143	connected component is open.
144	
145	Proof: `ConnectedComponents α` is totally disconnected
146	(`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1
147	(`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by
148	`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`. A finite T1 space is discrete
149	(`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`), so the singleton
150	`{ConnectedComponents.mk x}` is open in the quotient. The preimage of this
151	singleton under the continuous quotient map `ConnectedComponents.mk` is
152	`connectedComponent x` (`connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`), which is
153	therefore open. -/
154	private lemma noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent
155	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α]
156	    (x : α) :
157	    IsOpen (connectedComponent x) := by
158	  haveI : Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
159	  haveI : DiscreteTopology (ConnectedComponents α) := inferInstance
160	  have h := (isOpen_discrete
161	    ({(ConnectedComponents.mk x : ConnectedComponents α)} : Set _)).preimage
162	    ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe
163	  rwa [connectedComponents_preimage_singleton] at h
164	
165	/-- Helper (iter-188): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
166	underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
167	morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.
168	
169	The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
170	space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
171	Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
172	spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
173	IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
174	`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
175	implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
176	is the iter-188 project-side helper (`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`
177	+ `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent`).
178	
179	The classical proof: each point `y ∈ G.left` has an open affine
180	neighbourhood `W = Spec R` with `R` Noetherian, hence `|W|` is a Noetherian
181	topological space; in a Noetherian space, each `connectedComponent y` is
182	clopen (finite irreducible components ⟹ finite connected components ⟹
183	finite T1 quotient ⟹ discrete quotient ⟹ singletons clopen in quotient ⟹
184	preimages clopen). Pulled back along the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left`,
185	this gives an open connected neighbourhood of `y` inside any open `F`
186	containing `y`. -/
187	private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
189	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
190	    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
191	    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
192	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom
193	  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected]
194	  intro x F hF
195	  -- Extract an open neighbourhood `T ⊆ F` of `x`.
196	  obtain ⟨T, hTF, hT, hxT⟩ := mem_nhds_iff.mp hF
197	  -- Find an affine open `W ⊆ T` with `x ∈ W`.
198	  obtain ⟨W, hW, hxW, hWT⟩ :=
199	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := G.left) (U := ⟨T, hT⟩) hxT
200	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(G.left, W) :=
201	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨W, hW⟩
202	  haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace ↥W :=
203	    noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen W hW
204	  -- Inside `W` (Noetherian) the connected component of `x` is open; its image
205	  -- under the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left` is the connected open neighbourhood
206	  -- we want.
207	  let xW : ↥W := ⟨x, hxW⟩
208	  refine ⟨(Subtype.val : ↥W → G.left) '' connectedComponent xW, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
209	  · rintro _ ⟨z, _, rfl⟩
210	    exact hTF (hWT z.2)
211	  · exact W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val _
212	      (noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent xW)
213	  · exact ⟨xW, mem_connectedComponent, rfl⟩
214	  · exact isConnected_connectedComponent.image _ continuous_subtype_val.continuousOn
215	
216	/-- The image of the identity section `e : Spec k → G` (well-defined as a
217	single point of `|G|` because `Spec k` is a topological singleton). -/
218	private noncomputable def identitySectionPoint
219	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] : G.left :=
220	  ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base :
221	      ↥(Spec (.of k)) → G.left) (default : Spec (.of k))
222	
223	/-- Helper (iter-186; iter-187 closed): the **clopen carrier `Set`** of
224	the identity component of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type,
225	packaged as a `G.left.Opens`. The carrier set is `connectedComponent x`
226	for `x = e(*)` the image of the identity section
227	(`identitySectionPoint G`); openness is `isOpen_connectedComponent` which
228	needs the `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance above
229	(EGA I 6.1.9). -/
230	private noncomputable def identityComponentCarrier {k : Type u} [Field k]
231	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
232	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
233	    G.left.Opens :=
234	  ⟨connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G), isOpen_connectedComponent⟩
235	
236	/-- The **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
237	finite type.
238	
239	Encoded as a `k`-scheme: an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`, carrying the
240	intended substantive identity "this is the connected component of `|G|`
241	containing the identity section `e`, equipped with the open-subscheme
242	structure inherited from `G`". The associated open immersion
243	`IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` is the content of
244	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`.
245	
246	iter-186 body: built from the `identityComponentCarrier G` helper — the
247	open subscheme of `G` whose underlying topological space is the connected
248	component of `|G|` through the image of the identity section. The
249	structure morphism is the inherited `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`.
250	The substantive content (the actual carrier `Set`, plus its openness from
251	EGA I 6.1.9: locally Noetherian spaces have open connected components)
252	lives in the typed-sorry body of `identityComponentCarrier`. -/
253	noncomputable def IdentityComponent {k : Type u} [Field k]
254	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
255	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
256	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
257	  Over.mk ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
258	
259	/-- **The identity component is an open and closed subgroup scheme.**
260	
261	The bundled statement of Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3): the identity
262	component `G^0 = IdentityComponent G` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
263	finite type comes with a morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` (in
264	`Over (Spec (.of k))`) whose underlying scheme morphism is both an open
265	immersion and a closed immersion (i.e. the inclusion of a clopen
266	subscheme).
267	
268	The full Kleiman conclusion also packages the group-subscheme property (the
269	inclusion is a homomorphism of `k`-group schemes), finite-type-ness over `k`
270	(`LocallyOfFiniteType` + quasi-compactness), geometric irreducibility, and
271	base-change-commutation. Those refinements live as separate instances /
272	follow-up lemmas in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the clopen
273	open-immersion conclusion as a Nonempty-witness.
274	
275	iter-186 body: the inclusion morphism is `Over.homMk (identityComponentCarrier G).ι`,
276	with the over-category compatibility holding by definition of
277	`IdentityComponent G`. The `.left` of this morphism is
278	`(identityComponentCarrier G).ι`, an open immersion by the global
279	`Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι` instance. For the closed-immersion
280	half we apply `IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion` to the open immersion
281	and reduce to `IsClosed (↑(identityComponentCarrier G) : Set _)`. The
282	remaining inline `sorry` is the substantive content: the carrier of
283	`identityComponentCarrier G` is closed, by "closure of a connected subspace
284	is connected" applied to the connected component through the image of the
285	identity section. -/
286	theorem IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
287	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
288	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
289	    Nonempty {f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G //
290	        IsOpenImmersion f.left ∧ IsClosedImmersion f.left} := by
291	  -- Over-category compatibility for the underlying open immersion.
292	  have hcomp : (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
293	    simp [IdentityComponent]
294	  -- The over-morphism witness.
295	  let f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G :=
296	    Over.homMk (U := IdentityComponent G) (V := G) (identityComponentCarrier G).ι hcomp
297	  refine ⟨⟨f, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
298	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` (definitionally; `Over.homMk_left` simp).
299	    -- An open immersion by `Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι`.
300	    change IsOpenImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
301	    infer_instance
302	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` is a preimmersion (open immersion ⟹
303	    -- immersion ⟹ preimmersion). Its range is the carrier set (`Scheme.Opens.range_ι`),
304	    -- and closure of that connected subset is itself (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3):
305	    -- the connected component through the identity is closed in `|G|`).
306	    change IsClosedImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
307	    apply IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion
308	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
309	    -- The carrier set is `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`, which is
310	    -- closed by `isClopen_connectedComponent.1` (with the
311	    -- `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance providing the closedness
312	    -- half of EGA I 6.1.9: connected components of a locally Noetherian topological
313	    -- space are clopen).
314	    change IsClosed (connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G))
315	    exact isClopen_connectedComponent.1

317	/-! ### iter-192 Lane A.3.i: identity-component substrate
318	
319	Per `analogies/lane-a3i-isconnected-prod.md`, the substrate for the
320	group-structure inheritance and base-change-commutation arguments is
321	Stacks Tag 04KU / EGA IV₂ 4.5.14 (a connected `k`-scheme with a
322	`k`-rational section is geometrically connected) combined with Mathlib's
323	`ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance for
324	`[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f] [ConnectedSpace Y]`.
325	
326	This iter (Lane A.3.i): we add the AXIOM-CLEAN
327	`identityComponentCarrier_connectedSpace` helper below (the carrier is
328	connected by construction). The full Stacks 04KU bridge
329	"`ConnectedSpace X` + section ⟹ `GeometricallyConnected f`" requires
330	the Mathlib substrate "`X` connected with `k` algebraically closed in
331	`Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` ⟹ `X` geometrically connected over `k`" (Stacks 037Q +
332	04KU), not yet available in Mathlib at SHA b80f227 — pending that
333	Mathlib lemma, the geometric-connectedness step lives as a residual
334	sorry inside the downstream theorems
335	(`isSubgroupHomomorphism`, `baseChangeIso`, `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible`).
336	
337	Below: `baseChangeIso` partially closes via
338	`CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd` (iter-192 axiom-clean closure
339	of 2 of the 3 conjuncts; the third conjunct, the iso of identity-component
340	constructions, remains sorry pending the Stacks 04KU substrate). -/
341	
342	/-- Helper (iter-192 Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean): the **identity component
343	carrier** has connected underlying topological space.
344	
345	The carrier is defined as `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`
346	in `|G|` (a `G.left.Opens`), and its subspace topology coincides with the
347	open-subscheme topology; the subspace is connected because the connected
348	component is preconnected (Mathlib's `isPreconnected_connectedComponent`)
349	and nonempty (contains the identity point `identitySectionPoint G`). -/
350	private instance identityComponentCarrier_connectedSpace
351	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
352	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
353	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
354	    ConnectedSpace ((identityComponentCarrier G : G.left.Opens) : Type _) := by
355	  haveI : PreconnectedSpace ((identityComponentCarrier G : G.left.Opens) : Type _) :=
356	    Subtype.preconnectedSpace isPreconnected_connectedComponent
357	  exact ⟨⟨⟨identitySectionPoint G, mem_connectedComponent⟩⟩⟩
358	
359	/-- Helper (iter-192 Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean): the **identity component**
360	`IdentityComponent G` has connected underlying topological space.
361	
362	By definition `(IdentityComponent G).left` is the open subscheme
363	`identityComponentCarrier G : G.left.Opens` regarded as a scheme; its
364	underlying topological space coincides definitionally with the carrier's
365	subspace topology, so `ConnectedSpace` transports via
366	`identityComponentCarrier_connectedSpace` above.
367	
368	Downstream this combines with the (pending) Stacks 04KU substrate
369	`GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom` plus Mathlib's
370	`ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance for
371	`[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f] [ConnectedSpace Y]`
372	to give the key "`G⁰ ×_k G⁰` is connected" substrate for Kleiman's
373	group-structure inheritance argument in `isSubgroupHomomorphism`. -/
374	private instance identityComponent_connectedSpace
375	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
376	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
377	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
378	    ConnectedSpace (IdentityComponent G).left := by
379	  change ConnectedSpace ((identityComponentCarrier G : G.left.Opens) : Type _)
380	  infer_instance
381	
382	/-- **Stacks 04KU / EGA IV₂ 4.5.14**: a connected `k`-scheme with a `k`-rational
383	section is geometrically connected.
384	
385	Given a morphism `f : X ⟶ Spec k` from a `ConnectedSpace`-typed scheme `X`
386	admitting a section `s : Spec k ⟶ X` (i.e. `s ≫ f = 𝟙`), the morphism `f` is
387	geometrically connected: for any field extension `K/k`, the pullback
388	`X ×_{Spec k} Spec K` is connected.
389	
390	iter-193 Lane A.3.i, planner-set authorisation per `PROGRESS.md`: ships as a
391	typed `sorry` to enable downstream consumers (sanctioned temporary sorry-count
392	increase). The full body is Stacks 04KV: a hypothetical clopen partition
393	`X_K = U ⊔ V` would, via the base-changed section `s_K = s ≫ (pullback π s)⁻¹`
394	say `K`-rational point landing in exactly one of `U, V`; the image of the
395	chosen piece under the projection `X_K → X` would then be a clopen subset
396	of `X` whose disjointness from the image of the other piece contradicts
397	`ConnectedSpace X`. Mathlib at SHA b80f227 lacks the descent-of-clopen-
398	partitions-along-fpqc-base-change substrate (Stacks 02LB), preventing an
399	axiom-clean closure this iter.
400	
401	Alternative proof via Stacks 037Q (also project-side, also not yet built):
402	a `k`-scheme `X` is geometrically connected iff `X` is connected AND the
403	algebraic closure of `k` in `Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` equals `k`. With the section `s`,
404	the pullback `s^* : Γ(X, 𝒪_X) → k` is a `k`-algebra retraction; any subfield
405	`F ⊆ Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` containing `k` maps injectively into `k` (as `F` is a field),
406	forcing `F = k`. Combined with `ConnectedSpace X`, gives the conclusion.
407	
408	Downstream consumers:
409	- iso slot of `IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso` (the `(G^0)_K ≅ (G_K)^0`
410	  identification);
411	- chain into `[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f]
412	  [ConnectedSpace Y] ⟹ ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` to derive
413	  `ConnectedSpace (G^0 ×_k G^0)` for `isSubgroupHomomorphism`. -/
414	private theorem geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section
415	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}}
416	    (f : X ⟶ Spec (.of k))
417	    (s : Spec (.of k) ⟶ X) (_hsf : s ≫ f = 𝟙 _)
418	    [ConnectedSpace X] :
419	    GeometricallyConnected f := by
420	  -- iter-194 push-beyond restructure: expose the structural reduction via
421	  -- `geometrically_iff_of_commRing_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms`. The
422	  -- residual sorry now lives at the precise Stacks 037Q gap point.
423	  refine ⟨?_⟩
424	  rw [geometrically_iff_of_commRing_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms]
425	  intro K _ _
426	  -- Goal: `ConnectedSpace ↥(pullback f (Spec.map (ofHom (algebraMap k K))))`.
427	  --
428	  -- Stacks 037Q gap (precise INCOMPLETE surface, iter-194 prover lane):
429	  -- The Stacks-037Q route requires two missing Mathlib pieces at SHA b80f227:
430	  --
431	  -- (1) **Stacks 04KV reduction**: For `f : X → Spec k`, `GeometricallyConnected f`
432	  --     iff for every finite separable extension `k'/k`, the base change
433	  --     `X ×_k Spec k'` is connected. This is the descent of geometric
434	  --     connectedness from arbitrary extensions to finite separable ones; it
435	  --     uses Galois-fixed-point arguments on `Spec(k' ⊗_k K)` for `K`
436	  --     algebraically closed. Not in Mathlib.
437	  --
438	  -- (2) **Field-tensor-product criterion**: For `K = Γ(X, O_X)` with `k`
439	  --     algebraically closed in `K`, the tensor product `K ⊗_k k'` is a
440	  --     field for every finite separable extension `k'/k`. Not in Mathlib
441	  --     (the converse `Algebra.TensorProduct.isField_of_isAlgebraic` exists
442	  --     but expects `IsDomain` on the tensor product; the "alg-closed-in"
443	  --     hypothesis is not what `Algebra.IsAlgebraic` packages).
444	  --
445	  -- The section `s` supplies the algebraic-closure hypothesis of (2): the
446	  -- `k`-algebra retraction `s.app top : Γ(X, O_X) → k` of `f.app top`
447	  -- forces any intermediate subfield `F` of `Γ(X, O_X)` containing `k` to
448	  -- satisfy `F = k` (a `k`-algebra retraction of a field map is necessarily
449	  -- an isomorphism). With (2) in hand, `Spec(Γ(X, O_X) ⊗_k k')` is connected
450	  -- (Spec of a field is connected), then (1) chains this to the conclusion.
451	  --
452	  -- Alternative attack: descent of clopen partitions along faithfully flat
453	  -- (Spec K → Spec k) base change. Mathlib has
454	  -- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.isQuotientMap_of_surjective` for the
455	  -- faithfully-flat-quasi-compact-surjective case; the fibre-connectedness
456	  -- needed for the quotient-map argument to bootstrap is the same gap.
457	  --
458	  -- iter-200 mathlib-analogist sweep target: surface (1) + (2) on the
459	  -- "substrate unowned" tier; the project-side closure becomes mechanical
460	  -- once either is in Mathlib.
461	  --
462	  -- iter-194 structural advance (axiom-clean intermediate): build the
463	  -- base-changed section `sK` explicitly so the next prover starts from
464	  -- "ConnectedSpace + Nonempty witness" instead of bare connectedness.
465	  -- This is what the `_hsf` hypothesis encodes structurally — the
466	  -- `s ≫ f = 𝟙` triangle base-changes to a section of `pullback.snd`,
467	  -- giving a `K`-rational point of the base change.
468	  let g : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
469	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
470	  let _sK : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Limits.pullback f g :=
471	    Limits.pullback.lift (g ≫ s) (𝟙 _)
472	      (by rw [Category.assoc, _hsf, Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp])
473	  -- `_sK` is a section of `pullback.snd : pullback f g → Spec K` (by
474	  -- `pullback.lift_snd`); pushing `default` through gives nonemptiness:
475	  haveI : Nonempty ↑↑(Limits.pullback f g).toPresheafedSpace :=
476	    ⟨_sK.base default⟩
477	  -- Residual gap: connectedness of `pullback f g` given nonemptiness +
478	  -- `ConnectedSpace X`. Stacks 037Q / 04KV; cf. structural notes above.
479	  sorry
480	
481	/-- The range-containment hypothesis for `IsOpenImmersion.lift` used to build
482	the section `identityComponentSection G` below: the image of the identity
483	section `MonObj.one.left : Spec k ⟶ G.left` lies in the carrier of the
484	identity component (a singleton image `{identitySectionPoint G}` contained
485	in `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G) = identityComponentCarrier G`). -/
486	private lemma identityComponentSection_range_subset
487	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
488	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
489	    Set.range ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left).base ⊆
490	      Set.range (identityComponentCarrier G).ι.base := by
491	  rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
492	  let f : ↥(Spec (.of k)) → G.left := (MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base
493	  change Set.range f ⊆ _
494	  rintro y ⟨x, rfl⟩
495	  have hx : x = default := Subsingleton.elim _ _
496	  rw [hx]
497	  -- `f default = identitySectionPoint G` (by definition of
498	  -- `identitySectionPoint`); the carrier underlying set is
499	  -- `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`.
500	  exact mem_connectedComponent
501	
502	private noncomputable def identityComponentSection
503	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
504	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
505	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
506	    Spec (.of k) ⟶ (IdentityComponent G).left :=
507	  @IsOpenImmersion.lift (identityComponentCarrier G).toScheme (Spec (.of k))
508	    G.left (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
509	    ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left)
510	    inferInstance (identityComponentSection_range_subset G)
511	
512	/-- Helper (iter-193 Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean): the **identity section lift is
513	a section of `(IdentityComponent G).hom`**.
514	
515	Composing `identityComponentSection G` with `(IdentityComponent G).hom`
516	returns the identity on `Spec k`, by `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac` plus the
517	over-compatibility `MonObj.one.left ≫ G.hom = 𝟙` (the terminal of
518	`Over (Spec k)` has `.hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)` definitionally). -/
519	private lemma identityComponentSection_isSection
520	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
521	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
522	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
523	    identityComponentSection G ≫ (IdentityComponent G).hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of k)) := by
524	  -- `(IdentityComponent G).hom = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`
525	  -- (definitionally, by the `IdentityComponent` def).
526	  change identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = _
527	  rw [← Category.assoc]
528	  -- `identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι = MonObj.one.left`
529	  -- by `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac` (after unfolding the `identityComponentSection`
530	  -- def to expose the `IsOpenImmersion.lift` head).
531	  rw [show identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι =
532	        ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left) from
533	      IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ (identityComponentSection_range_subset G)]
534	  -- Now: `(MonObj.one (X := G)).left ≫ G.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of k))`.
535	  -- This is the over-morphism compatibility: for the terminal of
536	  -- `Over (Spec k)`, `(MonObj.one).w` says
537	  -- `MonObj.one.left ≫ G.hom = (𝟙_).hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)` (defeq).
538	  exact (MonObj.one (X := G)).w
539	
540	/-- Lemma (iter-193 Lane A.3.i, propagates through the sorry-bodied
541	`geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` helper): the structural
542	morphism `(IdentityComponent G).hom` is **geometrically connected**.
543	
544	**iter-194 demotion (per lean-auditor iter-193 must-fix): no longer a
545	`private instance` but a `private theorem`.** The earlier instance shape
546	silently propagated a `sorryAx` axiom into every downstream typeclass
547	search that happened to resolve `GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent
548	G).hom` — a soundness exposure. Downstream consumers should now invoke
549	this lemma explicitly via `letI := identityComponent_geometricallyConnected G`.
550	
551	Derived from:
552	- `identityComponent_connectedSpace`: `ConnectedSpace (IdentityComponent G).left`
553	  (axiom-clean iter-192).
554	- `identityComponentSection_isSection`: existence of a section
555	  `Spec k ⟶ (IdentityComponent G).left` (axiom-clean iter-193).
556	- `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section`: Stacks 04KU helper
557	  (sorry-bodied iter-193, planner-sanctioned).
558	
559	Once `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` lands axiom-clean
560	(via the Stacks 037Q substrate or descent-of-clopen-partitions substrate),
561	this lemma becomes axiom-clean automatically. Downstream consumers can
562	chain `letI := identityComponent_geometricallyConnected G` with
563	`UniversallyOpen` of `Spec k → Spec k` (which holds via
564	`[IsIntegral Y] [Subsingleton Y] ⟹ UniversallyOpen f`) to derive
565	`ConnectedSpace (pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom g)` for any
566	`g : Y ⟶ Spec (.of k)` from a `ConnectedSpace Y`. -/
567	private theorem identityComponent_geometricallyConnected
568	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
569	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
570	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
571	    GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom :=
572	  geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section
573	    (IdentityComponent G).hom (identityComponentSection G)
574	    (identityComponentSection_isSection G)

576	/-- **The identity component inclusion is a group-scheme homomorphism.**
577	
578	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (b): the clopen subscheme `G^0`
579	inherits a `k`-group-scheme structure from `G`, and the inclusion morphism
580	`G^0 ↪ G` (from `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`) is compatible
581	with the group laws on source and target. The statement-level pin asserts
582	the existence of the inherited `GrpObj` structure; the compatibility of
583	the inclusion with the group operations is the substantive content of
584	Kleiman's argument (the product `G^0 ×_k G^0` is connected by
585	EGA IV₂ 4.5.8; the group-multiplication map sends this connected subset
586	containing the identity into the connected component `G^0`).
587	
588	The full statement (existence of `GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)` *together*
589	with the homomorphism-compatibility of the inclusion in the relevant
590	`Hom`-form) lives in iter-193+. -/
591	theorem IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism {k : Type u} [Field k]
592	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
593	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
594	    Nonempty (GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)) :=
595	  sorry
596	
597	/-- **The identity component is of finite type and geometrically irreducible.**
598	
599	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (c): the open subgroup `G^0` of
600	a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type is itself
601	locally-of-finite-type-plus-quasi-compact (i.e., of finite type) over `k`,
602	and is geometrically irreducible. The proof reduces (after base change to
603	`\bar k`) to picking a nonempty smooth affine open subset `U ⊆ G^0`; the
604	translates `hg⁻¹U` give an open cover of `G^0` by smooth, hence irreducible,
605	neighbourhoods, so `G^0` is locally irreducible at every closed point; with
606	connectedness this gives irreducibility globally (EGA I 6.1.10). The image
607	`α(U × U) = G^0` is the image of the affine, hence quasi-compact, scheme
608	`U × U`, so `G^0` is quasi-compact.
609	
610	iter-189 partial progress: the `LocallyOfFiniteType` conjunct closes
611	axiom-clean via composition of an open immersion with `G.hom`. The
612	remaining two conjuncts (`QuasiCompact`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`)
613	require the group-structure argument and are bundled into the residual
614	sorry. -/
615	theorem IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible
616	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
617	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
618	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
619	    LocallyOfFiniteType (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
620	      QuasiCompact (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
621	      GeometricallyIrreducible (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
622	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
623	  · -- `LocallyOfFiniteType`: `(IdentityComponent G).hom` unfolds to
624	    -- `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`. Open immersion ∘ LFT
625	    -- is LFT (`locallyOfFiniteType_of_isOpenImmersion` +
626	    -- `locallyOfFiniteType_comp`).
627	    change LocallyOfFiniteType ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
628	    infer_instance
629	  · -- `QuasiCompact ∧ GeometricallyIrreducible`: Kleiman's group-theoretic
630	    -- argument. `α(U × U) = G^0` quasi-compact via the affine `U × U`;
631	    -- geometric irreducibility via base change to `\bar k`, picking a
632	    -- smooth affine `U ⊆ G^0`, and translating `hg⁻¹U` to cover. Cannot
633	    -- close axiom-clean without the substrate `isSubgroupHomomorphism`
634	    -- + EGA IV₂ 4.5.8 / 4.6.1 / EGA I 6.1.10 (not yet in Mathlib).
635	    sorry
636	
637	/-- **Formation of the identity component commutes with base change.**
638	
639	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (d): for any field extension
640	`K/k`, the natural comparison map `(G^0)_K → (G_K)^0` is an isomorphism of
641	`K`-schemes. The proof: `G^0` is geometrically connected as a connected
642	scheme with a `k`-rational point (EGA IV₂ 4.5.14), so its base change
643	`(G^0)_K` remains connected; it is also open and closed in `G_K`, so
644	coincides with the identity component `(G_K)^0`.
645	
646	The statement-level pin asserts existence of: a `K`-group-scheme structure
647	on the base change `G ×_{Spec k} Spec K` (with appropriate
648	locally-of-finite-type instance), and an isomorphism on underlying schemes
649	identifying the two iterated constructions.
650	
651	iter-192 partial closure (Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean modulo the iso slot):
652	the `_grpInst` slot closes via `CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd`
653	(per `analogies/lane-a3i-isconnected-prod.md` second analogue — the
654	category-theoretic base-change of `GrpObj` directly fitting the
655	`Over.mk (pullback.snd ...)` shape of `G_K`); the `_locFTInst` slot
656	closes via Mathlib's stability of `LocallyOfFiniteType` under base
657	change (`inferInstance` fires on the pullback). The remaining iso slot
658	`(IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅ pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ`
659	is the substantive content of Stacks 04KS / EGA IV₂ 4.5.16: identifying
660	the iterated identity-component construction with the base change of
661	the identity component, which requires the
662	`geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` helper above plus
663	descent of clopen partitions along base change (not yet in Mathlib). -/
664	theorem IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
665	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
666	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra k K] :
667	    let φ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
668	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
669	    let G_K : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) :=
670	      Over.mk (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ)
671	    Nonempty (Σ' (_grpInst : GrpObj G_K)
672	                 (_locFTInst : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom),
673	      (IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅
674	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) := by
675	  intro φ G_K
676	  -- `GrpObj G_K` via the category-theoretic base-change lemma
677	  -- `CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd`. The instance bridge
678	  -- `[GrpObj G] ⟹ [GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom)]` is by defeq (`G = Over.mk G.hom`
679	  -- when `G` is an over-category object whose right component is the unique
680	  -- inhabitant of `Discrete PUnit`).
681	  haveI hG : GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom) := ‹GrpObj G›
682	  haveI hGK_grp : GrpObj G_K := CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd
683	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom`: `G_K.hom = pullback.snd G.hom φ` is the
684	  -- base change of `G.hom` along `φ`; `LocallyOfFiniteType` is stable
685	  -- under base change (Mathlib instance).
686	  haveI hGK_lft : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom :=
687	    (inferInstance : LocallyOfFiniteType (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ))
688	  -- The iso slot: substrate is now in place (iter-193 push-beyond):
689	  -- `identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` provides
690	  -- `GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom`, which combined with
691	  -- Mathlib's `[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f]
692	  -- [ConnectedSpace Y] ⟹ ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance gives
693	  -- `ConnectedSpace (pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ)` (the connected-
694	  -- pullback substrate). The iso construction proceeds by:
695	  -- 1. Build an open immersion `e : pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ ⟶
696	  --    G_K.left` via `pullback.map _ _ _ _ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
697	  --    (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) ...`, open-immersion status from
698	  --    `Scheme.pullback_map_isOpenImmersion` (i₁ = ι OI; i₂ = 𝟙 OI;
699	  --    i₃ = 𝟙 mono).
700	  -- 2. Show `Set.range e.base = identityComponentCarrier G_K (as Set)`
701	  --    via clopen-+-connected → connected-component bidirectional argument.
702	  -- 3. Apply `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` to get the iso between
703	  --    `pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ` and
704	  --    `(identityComponentCarrier G_K).toScheme = (IdentityComponent G_K).left`.
705	  -- Substantive content of step 2 (`~80-100 LOC`) deferred to iter-194+.
706	  refine ⟨⟨hGK_grp, hGK_lft, ?_⟩⟩
707	  sorry
708	
709	end GroupScheme

711	/-! ## §2. The identity component of the Picard scheme
712	
713	We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
714	`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
715	integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).
716	
717	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.
718	`prp:pic0`). -/
719	
720	namespace Scheme
721	
722	/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.
723	
724	Defined as the identity component
725	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
726	`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
727	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
728	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
729	open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
730	geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
731	base field.
732	
733	iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
734	once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
735	`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
736	locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
737	`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
738	noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
739	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
740	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
741	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
742	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
743	  sorry
744	
745	/-! ## §3. The degree map
746	
747	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
748	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
749	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
750	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
751	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
752	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
753	
754	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
755	
756	namespace PicScheme
757	
758	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
759	
760	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
761	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
762	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
763	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
764	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
765	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
766	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
767	relative Picard functor).
768	
769	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
770	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
771	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
772	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
773	
774	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
775	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
776	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
777	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
778	`sorry`. -/
779	noncomputable def degree {k : Type u} [Field k]
780	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
781	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
782	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
783	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
784	  sorry
785	
786	end PicScheme
787	
788	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
789	
790	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
791	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
792	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
793	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
794	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
795	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
796	
797	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
798	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
799	
800	namespace Pic0Scheme
801	
802	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
803	
804	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
805	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
806	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
807	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
808	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
809	
810	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
811	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
812	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
813	
814	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
815	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
816	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
817	follow-up.
818	
819	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
820	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
821	  `PicScheme C`),
822	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
823	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
824	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
825	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
826	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
827	  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
828	  identity component).
829	For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
830	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
831	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
832	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
833	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
834	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
835	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
836	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
837	  sorry
838	
839	/-- **Dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals the genus of `C`.**
840	
841	Milne~§III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e): "The dimension of `J` is the genus of `C`".
842	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
843	`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
844	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
845	`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where
846	`Pic_{C/k}` is smooth, and for smooth proper curves
847	(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
848	(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`
849	by `def:genus`. -/
850	theorem finrank_eq_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
851	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
852	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
853	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
854	    topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
855	  sorry
856	
857	/-- **`k`-points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` are the kernel of the degree map.**
858	
859	Milne~§III.1, p.~88: `Pic⁰(C)` is the group of isomorphism classes of
860	invertible sheaves of degree zero on `C`. For a smooth proper geometrically
861	integral curve `C/k`, a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` lies in the image of
862	the inclusion `Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}` (the inclusion of
863	`def:pic_zero_subscheme`, packaged here via the existence of the
864	inclusion morphism) if and only if `degree C λ = 0`.
865	
866	The statement-level pin packages two pieces: existence of the inclusion
867	morphism `Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶ Pic_{C/k}` (extracted from
868	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` once `PicScheme C` has the
869	`GrpObj` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` instances), together with the
870	characterisation of `k`-points factoring through it as those with degree
871	zero. -/
872	theorem kPoints_iff_kerDegree {k : Type u} [Field k]
873	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
874	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
875	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
876	    Nonempty (Σ' (inc : (Pic0Scheme C).left ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
877	      ∀ (lambda : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
878	        (∃ mu : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left, mu ≫ inc = lambda) ↔
879	          PicScheme.degree C lambda = 0) :=
880	  sorry
881	
882	end Pic0Scheme
883	
884	end Scheme
885	
886	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 1): the bare scheme `ProjectiveLineBar` and its 2-chart cover
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 1** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
13	
14	* the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`;
15	* the `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f`;
16	* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
17	  `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`;
18	* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
19	* the scaffold `GeometricallyIrreducible` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instances;
20	* the 2-chart affine open cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`.
21	
22	Downstream strata: `ChartIso` (chart-ring iso), `Points` (k̄-points + `Ga` + `Gm`),
23	`GmScaling` (chart-bridge + `σ_×`).

37	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
38	
39	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
40	
41	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
42	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
43	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
44	
45	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
49	
50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
51	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
52	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
59	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
60	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
61	    Algebra kbar
62	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
63	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-! ### Project-local Mathlib supplement — MvPolynomial as standard smooth algebra
152	
153	This subsection ships an axiom-clean construction of the canonical submersive
154	presentation of `MvPolynomial ι R` as an `R`-algebra (no relations, generators
155	indexed by `ι`). Mathlib does not ship a direct instance
156	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)`
157	(per iter-182 prover scout); we build it here from the underlying
158	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` machinery. This is a pure
159	`Mathlib`-supplement: it depends only on `Mathlib`, and is independent
160	of the rest of the project.
161	-/
162	
163	/-- The canonical `Generators` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` indexed by `α`,
164	with `val := MvPolynomial.X` and section `σ' := id`. -/
165	noncomputable def mvPolyGenerators (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
166	    Algebra.Generators R (MvPolynomial α R) α :=
167	  Algebra.Generators.ofSurjective MvPolynomial.X <|
168	    fun s => ⟨s, MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left_apply s⟩
169	
170	/-- The canonical `Presentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R` with no relations
171	(`σ = PEmpty`) and generators indexed by `α`. The kernel of the natural
172	`aeval X` map is `⊥` since `aeval X` is the identity. -/
173	noncomputable def mvPolyPresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (α : Type w) :
174	    Algebra.Presentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
175	  __ := mvPolyGenerators R α
176	  relation := PEmpty.elim
177	  span_range_relation_eq_ker := by
178	    simp only [Set.range_eq_empty, Ideal.span_empty]
179	    rw [Algebra.Generators.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val]
180	    change ⊥ = RingHom.ker (MvPolynomial.aeval (R := R) (MvPolynomial.X : α → MvPolynomial α R))
181	    rw [MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]
182	    ext x
183	    simp [RingHom.mem_ker]
184	
185	/-- The canonical `PreSubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
186	The differential is on the zero module `PEmpty → P.Ring`. -/
187	noncomputable def mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
188	    (α : Type w) :
189	    Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
190	  __ := mvPolyPresentation R α
191	  map := PEmpty.elim
192	  map_inj := fun a _ _ => PEmpty.elim a
193	
194	/-- The canonical `SubmersivePresentation` of `MvPolynomial α R` over `R`.
195	The Jacobian is the determinant of the zero-sized matrix, which is `1` (a unit). -/
196	noncomputable def mvPolySubmersivePresentation (R : Type u) [CommRing R]
197	    (α : Type w) :
198	    Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial α R) α PEmpty.{1} where
199	  __ := mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation R α
200	  jacobian_isUnit := by
201	    rw [Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation.jacobian_eq_jacobiMatrix_det]
202	    simp [Matrix.det_isEmpty]
203	
204	/-- **Mathlib supplement**: `MvPolynomial (Fin n) R` is `R`-standard smooth of
205	relative dimension `n`. Built from `mvPolySubmersivePresentation` with
206	dimension `Nat.card (Fin n) - Nat.card PEmpty = n`. -/
207	instance mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
208	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) :
209	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
210	  (mvPolySubmersivePresentation R (Fin n)).isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension <| by
211	    simp [Algebra.Presentation.dimension]
212	
213	/-! ### `GeometricallyIrreducible` instance -/
214	
215	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
216	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
217	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
218	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
219	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
220	  sorry
221	
222	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
223	
224	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
225	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
226	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
227	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
228	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
229	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
230	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
231	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
232	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
233	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
234	  fun i => by
235	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
236	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
237	
238	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
239	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
240	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
241	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
242	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
243	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
244	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
245	
246	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
247	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
248	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
249	
250	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
251	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
252	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
253	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
254	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
255	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
256	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
257	    (m := ![1, 1])
258	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
259	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
260	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
261	    (by
262	      classical
263	      intro p hp
264	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
265	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
266	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
267	        have := hp
268	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
269	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
270	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
271	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
272	        exact this
273	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
274	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
275	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
276	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
277	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
278	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
279	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
280	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
281	        by_contra h
282	        push Not at h
283	        apply hd_ne
284	        ext k
285	        fin_cases k
286	        · simpa using h.1
287	        · simpa using h.2
288	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
289	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
290	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
291	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
292	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
293	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
294	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
295	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
296	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
297	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
298	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
299	
300	-- NOTE iter-197: relocated to AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean per BareScheme smoothness-relocation refactor.
301	
302	end AlgebraicGeometry

40	`HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
41	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}`; other annotations produce a
42	`HasExt.{max ?u_1 ?u_2, ?u_2, ?u_1}` mismatch. -/
43	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp (X : TopCat.{u}) :
44	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
45	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
46	        AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
47	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
48	
49	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
50	
51	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
52	
53	/-- Phase A step 4 first half: the structure sheaf of a scheme, viewed as a
54	sheaf of abelian groups via the iter-004 `HasSheafCompose` instance for the
55	forget composite `CommRingCat → RingCat → AddCommGrpCat`. -/
56	noncomputable def toAbSheaf (C : Scheme.{u}) :
57	    CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat)
58	      AddCommGrpCat.{u} :=
59	  (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat)
60	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
61	      CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj C.sheaf
62	
63	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
64

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 073 — Phase E closes by reduction)
15	
16	After the iter-073 refactor of `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J` (no genus-0 dite),
17	all three protected declarations reduce uniformly to the Albanese universal property
18	carried by `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`:
19	
20	* `ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
21	* `comp_ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
22	* `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`.
23	
24	The genus-0 rigidity content previously sitting at the existence step of
25	`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (classical rigidity `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) is now absorbed
26	into the single deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness` in
27	`Jacobian.lean`: the witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` field must verify the Albanese property
28	for genus-0 curves too, which is precisely the rigidity content.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
40	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
41	  [IsProper C.hom]
42	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
43	
44	namespace Jacobian
45	
46	variable {C} in
47	-- data
48	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
49	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. Projects the Albanese universal morphism carried
50	by the witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
51	noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
52	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
53	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
54	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
55	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
56	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve
57	
58	variable {C} in
59	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
60	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. Projects the pointed-property
61	of the Albanese witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
62	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
63	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
64	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
65	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
66	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
67	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
68	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve
69	
70	variable {C} in
71	/--
72	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
73	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
74	Jacobian of `C`.
75	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
76	
77	The existence-and-uniqueness data is projected unconditionally from the witness
78	`(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. The genus-0 rigidity content (`Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
79	Mumford §4) is absorbed into the deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
80	since the witness must verify the Albanese property for genus-0 curves too.
81	-/
82	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
83	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
84	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
85	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
86	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
87	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
88	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
89	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
90	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
91	
92	end Jacobian
93	
94	end AlgebraicGeometry
95
