393	/-- **Bridge 2 of the Rigidity Lemma (slice-constancy / the agreement equation), the residual
394	geometric input.** Let `X` be complete (proper) over an algebraically closed `k̄`, `x₀` a
395	`k̄`-point of `X`, and `f : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Z` into a separated `Z`. Let `U = p₂⁻¹(V)` be a `p₂`-saturated
396	open of `X ⊗ Y` (the preimage of a set `Vset ⊆ Y`) on which `f` lands inside a single **affine**
397	open `U₀ ⊆ Z`. Then `f` agrees on `U` with the collapsed map `retract ≫ f`
398	(`retract := lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`, i.e. `(x, y) ↦ (x₀, y)`):
399	
400	  `U.ι ≫ f.left = U.ι ≫ (retract ≫ f).left`.
401	
402	This is the cohomology-free **route B** of the iter-159 `mathlib-analogist` consult
403	(`analogies/rigidity-affineconst.md`); it was the genuinely-deep residual of the Rigidity-Lemma
404	chain and is now **PROVEN axiom-clean** (iter-162), assembled here as a named top-level obligation
405	from Step 2 (`morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`) over the per-slice Step 1
406	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`). The relative Stein-factorisation /
407	proper-pushforward `f_*𝒪 = 𝒪` framing is a confirmed Mathlib gap and is **deliberately avoided**.
408	
409	The intended proof (no coherent cohomology):
410	1. *Per closed slice.* For each closed point `y ∈ Vset`, `κ(y) = k̄` (`[IsAlgClosed kbar]`, finite
411	   type). Saturation puts the whole fibre `X_y` inside `U`, so `f` maps the proper integral slice
412	   `X_y ≅ X` into the affine `U₀`. By `isField_of_universallyClosed` +
413	   `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` + alg-closedness, `Γ(X_y) = k̄`, so the slice maps to a
414	   single `k̄`-point of `U₀`
415	   (`ext_of_isAffine`); that point is `f(x₀, y)`, since `(x₀, y) ∈ X_y`. Hence `f` and `retract ≫ f`
416	   agree at every closed point of `U`.
417	2. *Globalise.* Closed points are dense in the locally-of-finite-type `k̄`-scheme `U`
418	   (`closure_closedPoints`, the Jacobson-space property). Turning "agrees at each closed point"
419	   into one dominant probe (the coproduct `∐_{x∈closedPoints U} Spec κ(x) ⟶ U`, dense range) and
420	   feeding it to `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` (the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity
421	   `rigidity_core` already uses) yields the morphism equality on all of `U`. This last
422	   "dense-closed-points ⟹ hom-ext" connective is the one piece Mathlib does not package directly. -/
423	theorem rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine
424	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
425	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
426	    [IsProper X.hom]
427	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
428	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
429	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
430	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
431	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
432	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
433	    (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens)
434	    (Vset : Set Y.left)
435	    (_hUV : (U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left) = (snd X Y).left.base ⁻¹' Vset)
436	    (U₀ : Z.left.Opens) (_hU₀ : IsAffineOpen U₀)
437	    (_hfU : ∀ u ∈ (U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left), f.left.base u ∈ U₀) :
438	    (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫ f.left =
439	      (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫
440	        (lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f).left := by
441	  -- Target separatedness (absolute), from `IsSeparated Z.hom` and the affine base `Spec k̄`:
442	  -- `terminal.from Z.left = Z.hom ≫ terminal.from (Spec k̄)`, a composite of separated maps.
443	  haveI : Z.left.IsSeparated := by
444	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
445	    have heq : terminal.from Z.left = Z.hom ≫ terminal.from (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) :=
446	      terminal.hom_ext _ _
447	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
448	  -- JACOBSON DERIVATION (iter-161: now a routine instance discharge, NOT an as-typed gap).
449	  -- The route-B globalisation of the per-closed-slice constancy needs the closed points of `U` to
450	  -- be DENSE, i.e. `U` to be a Jacobson space. The chain now carries
451	  -- `[LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]` as a hypothesis of this lemma, so `JacobsonSpace U` is
452	  -- derivable: `Spec k̄` is a Jacobson space (a field is an `IsJacobsonRing`,
453	  -- `PrimeSpectrum.instJacobsonSpaceOfIsJacobsonRing`); `LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace` then
454	  -- transports it to `(X ⊗ Y).left`; and `JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding` inherits it onto the
455	  -- open subscheme `U`. This `sorry` is therefore the assembly of those three Mathlib facts, left
456	  -- for the prover phase — it is no longer an as-typed-unprovability.
457	  haveI : JacobsonSpace ((U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme) := by
458	    -- `Spec k̄` is Jacobson (a field is `IsArtinianRing`, hence `IsJacobsonRing`); transport
459	    -- across the locally-of-finite-type structure map to `(X ⊗ Y).left`; then inherit onto the
460	    -- open subscheme `U` along the open embedding `U.ι`.
461	    haveI : JacobsonSpace (X ⊗ Y).left :=
462	      LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace (X ⊗ Y).hom
463	    exact JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding U.ι.isOpenEmbedding
464	  -- Globalise the per-closed-point slice-constancy (Step 1,
465	  -- `rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`) over the dense closed points (Step 2,
466	  -- `morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`). This wires bridge 2's route B end to end.
467	  exact morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints fun x hx =>
468	    rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine f x₀ U Vset _hUV U₀ _hU₀ _hfU x hx
469	
470	/-- **The dense-open agreement (the genuine geometric content).** Mumford's open
471	`X × V` together with the slice-constancy `f(x, y) = f(x₀, y)` on it, packaged as the single
472	existential that `rigidity_core`'s gluing step consumes: there is a non-empty open `U` of
473	`(X ⊗ Y).left` on which `f` and the collapsed map `retract ≫ f` agree as scheme morphisms.
474	
475	**Status (iter-159): this lemma is now `sorry`-free in its own body.** The construction of the
476	non-empty open `U = X × V` (closed-map bridge 1, `snd_left_isClosedMap`) and its non-emptiness
477	(`y₀ ∉ G` via the collapse hypothesis `_hf`, using the pullback-fibre fact `hfib` over the
478	`k̄`-point `y₀`) are discharged here. The one remaining geometric input — slice-constancy on `U`
479	(bridge 2) — is delegated to the named helper `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`, now
480	assembled from the proven Step 2 (`morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`) over the per-slice Step 1
481	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`); its affine-containment hypothesis is read off
482	the definition of `G` inside this proof. The whole chain — including Step 1's geometric
483	slice/section assembly — is now PROVEN axiom-clean (iter-162).
484	
485	This lemma carries the **collapse hypothesis** `_hf : f(X × {y₀}) = {z₀}` (encoded
486	`lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀`), which is exactly what makes Mumford's open
487	`V := Y ∖ G` non-empty (`y₀ ∉ G`, since the rigidified slice `f(X × {y₀}) = {z₀} ⊆ U` lands in
488	the affine `U`, so its image under `snd` avoids `G = snd '' (f ⁻¹ F)`). Without `_hf` the lemma
489	is **false** (e.g. `f := fst : X ⊗ Y ⟶ X = Z` has no open of agreement). The full instance set
490	(`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `IsReduced`, `IsSeparated`) and `_hf` only strengthen the
491	antecedent.
492	
493	Of the two char-free Mathlib bridges of `rigidity_core`'s docstring, **bridge 1** (the closed-map
494	argument, `IsProper.toUniversallyClosed` ⟹ the projection is closed) is discharged here — it
495	produces the non-empty open `U = X × V`. **Bridge 2** (the affine-constancy argument,
496	`isField_of_universallyClosed` on each proper integral slice mapping to an affine, supplying the
497	scheme-level equality on `U`) is the residual content, isolated in the named helper
498	`rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`. -/
499	theorem rigidity_eqOn_dense_open
500	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
501	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
502	    [IsProper X.hom]
503	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
504	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
505	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
506	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
507	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
508	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
509	    (y₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Y)
510	    (z₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Z)
511	    (_hf : lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀) :
512	    ∃ U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens, (U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left).Nonempty ∧
513	      (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫ f.left =
514	        (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) ≫
515	          (lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f).left := by
516	  -- Mumford's construction of the open `U = X × V`, `V = Y - G`, `G = p₂(f⁻¹(Z - U₀))`.
517	  -- Bridge 1 (`snd_left_isClosedMap`) makes `G` closed; the collapse hypothesis `_hf` makes
518	  -- `y₀ ∉ G` so `V` (hence `U`) is non-empty; bridge 2 (affine-constancy) is the agreement.
519	  have hclosed : IsClosedMap (snd X Y).left.base := snd_left_isClosedMap
520	  -- `Spec k̄` is a single point; transport the instance to the monoidal unit's underlying scheme.
521	  haveI hsub : Subsingleton (↥(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)))).left) :=
522	    inferInstanceAs (Subsingleton (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)))
523	  -- The chosen `k̄`-point of `Spec k̄` (`= (𝟙_).left`).
524	  have ptk : (𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)))).left :=
525	    (inferInstance : Inhabited (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))).default
526	  -- The image point `z₀` in `Z` and an affine open neighbourhood `U₀ ∋ z₀`.
527	  let z₀pt : Z.left := z₀.left.base ptk
528	  obtain ⟨U₀, _hU₀aff, hz₀U₀, -⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := Z.left)
529	    (x := z₀pt) (U := ⊤) trivial
530	  -- `G = p₂(f⁻¹(Z - U₀))` is closed (image of a closed set under the closed map `p₂`).
531	  set Gset := (snd X Y).left.base '' (f.left.base ⁻¹' (U₀ : Set Z.left)ᶜ) with hGdef
532	  have hG : IsClosed Gset := hclosed _ (U₀.isOpen.isClosed_compl.preimage f.left.base.hom.2)
533	  -- `U = p₂⁻¹(Y - G)` (Mumford's `X × V`) is open.
534	  have hUopen : IsOpen ((snd X Y).left.base ⁻¹' Gsetᶜ) :=
535	    (hG.isOpen_compl).preimage (snd X Y).left.base.hom.2
536	  -- The slice section `s : X → X ⊗ Y`, `x ↦ (x, y₀)`, and the points `y₀`, `x₀`.
537	  let s := (lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀)).left
538	  let y₀pt : Y.left := y₀.left.base ptk
539	  let x₀pt : X.left := x₀.left.base ptk
540	  -- Every point of the slice `p₂⁻¹{y₀}` lies in the image of the section `s`. True because `y₀`
541	  -- is a `k̄`-point: the slice section `s : X → X ⊗ Y` exhibits `X.left` as the fibre of the
542	  -- pullback projection `p₂` over `y₀`. We paste the identity (iso) outer square for `s` against
543	  -- the canonical pullback square and read the fibre off the coarse `PullbackCarrier` layer
544	  -- (`Scheme.image_preimage_eq_of_isPullback`); no residue fields / `Triplet` machinery.
545	  have hfib : (snd X Y).left.base ⁻¹' {y₀pt} ⊆ Set.range s.base := by
546	    set p₁ := pullback.fst X.hom Y.hom with hp₁def
547	    set p₂ := pullback.snd X.hom Y.hom with hp₂def
548	    -- `(toUnit X).left = X.hom`: the structure map of the unit is `𝟙`, and `Over.w` collapses.
549	    have htoUnit : (toUnit X).left = X.hom := by simp
550	    -- Triangle identities of the slice section `s = (x ↦ (x, y₀)).left`.
551	    have hsp1 : s ≫ p₁ = 𝟙 X.left := by
552	      rw [hp₁def, ← Over.fst_left, ← Over.comp_left, lift_fst, Over.id_left]
553	    have hsp2 : s ≫ p₂ = X.hom ≫ y₀.left := by
554	      rw [hp₂def, ← Over.snd_left, ← Over.comp_left, lift_snd, Over.comp_left]
555	      exact congrArg (· ≫ y₀.left) htoUnit
556	    -- `y₀` is a section of `Y.hom` (it is a `k̄`-point of `Y`).
557	    have hsec : y₀.left ≫ Y.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of kbar)) := by simpa using Over.w y₀
558	    -- The outer square `(s ≫ p₁ ; X.hom) = (X.hom ; y₀.left ≫ Y.hom)` is a pullback: both
559	    -- horizontal legs are identities (isos).
560	    have houter : IsPullback (s ≫ p₁) X.hom X.hom (y₀.left ≫ Y.hom) := by
561	      have hiso : IsPullback (𝟙 X.left) X.hom X.hom (𝟙 (Spec (.of kbar))) :=
562	        IsPullback.of_horiz_isIso ⟨by simp⟩
563	      rwa [← hsp1, ← hsec] at hiso
564	    -- Paste off the canonical right pullback square to recover the left square `hL`.
565	    have hL : IsPullback s X.hom p₂ y₀.left :=
566	      IsPullback.of_right houter hsp2 (IsPullback.of_hasPullback X.hom Y.hom)
567	    -- Range of `s` = fibre of `p₂` over `range y₀.left`, via the coarse pullback-carrier lemma.
568	    have hrange : Set.range s.base = p₂.base ⁻¹' Set.range y₀.left.base := by
569	      simpa [Set.image_univ, Set.preimage_univ] using
570	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.image_preimage_eq_of_isPullback hL.flip Set.univ
571	    rw [Over.snd_left, ← hp₂def, hrange]
572	    exact Set.preimage_mono (Set.singleton_subset_iff.mpr ⟨ptk, rfl⟩)
573	  -- `y₀ ∉ G`: any point over `y₀` is `s x`, and `_hf` collapses `f (s x) = z₀ ∈ U₀`.
574	  have hy₀ : y₀pt ∉ Gset := by
575	    rintro ⟨q, hq, hsndq⟩
576	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := hfib (by simpa using hsndq)
577	    apply hq
578	    have hcomp : s ≫ f.left = (toUnit X ≫ z₀).left := by
579	      rw [← Over.comp_left]; exact congrArg Over.Hom.left _hf
580	    have hfx : f.left.base (s.base x) = z₀pt := by
581	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, hcomp, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
582	      change z₀.left.base ((toUnit X).left.base x) = z₀.left.base ptk
583	      congr 1; exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
584	    rw [hfx]; exact hz₀U₀
585	  -- Assemble `U`, witness its non-emptiness by `s x₀` (which lies over `y₀ ∈ V`).
586	  refine ⟨⟨_, hUopen⟩, ⟨s.base x₀pt, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
587	  · change (snd X Y).left.base (s.base x₀pt) ∈ Gsetᶜ
588	    have hsnd : (snd X Y).left.base (s.base x₀pt) = y₀pt := by
589	      have hcomp : s ≫ (snd X Y).left = (toUnit X ≫ y₀).left := by
590	        rw [← Over.comp_left]; exact congrArg Over.Hom.left (lift_snd (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀))
591	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, hcomp, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
592	      change y₀.left.base ((toUnit X).left.base x₀pt) = y₀.left.base ptk
593	      congr 1; exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
594	    rw [Set.mem_compl_iff, hsnd]; exact hy₀
595	  · -- Bridge 2 (affine-constancy): on `U = X × V` (saturated: `U = p₂⁻¹ Gsetᶜ`) `f` lands in the
596	    -- affine `U₀`, so each proper slice maps to a single point `f(x₀, y)` and `f` agrees with
597	    -- `retract ≫ f` on `U`. The affine-containment `hfU` is read off the definition of `Gset`;
598	    -- the slice-constancy + dense-closed-points globalisation is the residual `route B`, isolated
599	    -- as the named helper `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine` (cohomology-free, ≈1–2 iter).
600	    have hfU : ∀ u ∈ ((⟨_, hUopen⟩ : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens) : Set (X ⊗ Y).left),
601	        f.left.base u ∈ U₀ := by
602	      intro u hu
603	      by_contra hcon
604	      -- `u ∈ U` means `p₂ u ∉ Gset`; but `f u ∉ U₀` puts `p₂ u` into `Gset = p₂ '' (f⁻¹ U₀ᶜ)`.
605	      exact hu ⟨u, hcon, rfl⟩
606	    exact rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine f x₀ ⟨_, hUopen⟩ Gsetᶜ rfl U₀ _hU₀aff hfU

608	/-- **Geometric core of the Rigidity Lemma (PROVEN axiom-clean, iter-162).** With
609	`X` complete (proper) and `x₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ X` a `k̄`-point, the morphism `f : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Z` is invariant
610	under the "collapse-the-`X`-axis-onto-`x₀`" endomorphism
611	`retract := lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`:
612	
613	  `f = retract ≫ f`,
614	
615	i.e. `f(x, y) = f(x₀, y)` for all `(x, y)` — `f` depends only on the `Y`-coordinate. This is
616	the entirety of the *geometric* content of Mumford's Rigidity Lemma (Form I); the rest of
617	`rigidity_lemma` is the cartesian-monoidal algebra discharged by `rigidity_snd_lift`.
618	
619	This core is now **proven** by the project's scheme-level rigidity (replicated inline from
620	Mathlib's `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`, since the wrapper `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`
621	lives in the downstream `AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity`): two maps out of the geometrically
622	irreducible reduced `X ⊗ Y` into the separated `Z` that agree on a non-empty open agree
623	everywhere. The existence of that non-empty open together with the agreement on it —
624	`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`, the actual geometry below — is now itself PROVEN axiom-clean.
625	
626	This core carries the **collapse hypothesis** `_hf : f(X × {y₀}) = {z₀}` (encoded
627	`lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀`), passed straight through to
628	`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`: it is exactly what makes Mumford's open `V := Y ∖ G` non-empty
629	(`y₀ ∉ G`). Without it the core statement is **false** (`f := fst` is not collapse-invariant).
630	
631	## Mumford's proof of `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` (Abelian Varieties, Ch. II §4, p. 43)
632	
633	Set `g(y) = f(x₀, y)` (so `retract ≫ f` is exactly `(x, y) ↦ g(y)`). The open `V` is produced as
634	follows: let `U` be an affine open neighbourhood of a chosen point in `Z`, `F = Z ∖ U`, and
635	`G = (snd X Y) '' (f ⁻¹ F)`. Then for each `y ∈ V := Y ∖ G`, the slice `f(X × {y}) ⊆ U`, and `f`
636	agrees with `retract ≫ f` on the non-empty open `X × V`.
637	
638	This rests on **two char-free Mathlib bridges** (the cube-free heart); bridge 1 is **built**,
639	bridge 2 (`rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`) is **decomposed and assembled** — Step 2
640	proven (`morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`), Step 1's deep algebra proven
641	(`eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral`), Step 1's geometric slice/section assembly
642	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`) also proven via `isIntegral_of_retract`:
643	
644	  1. **Properness ⇒ the projection is a closed map — BUILT** (`snd_left_isClosedMap`, iter-158).
645	     `IsProper X.hom` is universally closed (`AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper.toUniversallyClosed`), and
646	     `snd X Y` is the base change of `X.hom` along `Y.hom`, hence universally closed, hence a closed
647	     map. The glue identifying the monoidal `snd X Y` in `Over (Spec k̄)` with the scheme-theoretic
648	     pullback projection `Limits.pullback.snd X.hom Y.hom` is the exact rewrite `Over.snd_left`, and
649	     `IsClosedMap` is transported across the canonical pullback square via
650	     `universallyClosed_isStableUnderBaseChange`. So `G = snd '' (closed)` is closed, `V` open.
651	
652	  2. **A proper connected variety mapping to an affine has image a single point.** For `y ∈ V`,
653	     the proper connected slice `X × {y}` maps under `f` into the affine `U`; a global regular
654	     function on a proper integral `k̄`-scheme is constant. Mathlib *has* the key fact:
655	     `AlgebraicGeometry.isField_of_universallyClosed` — for `X` integral and `f : X ⟶ Spec K`
656	     universally closed (`K` a field), `Γ(X, ⊤)` is a field. Combined with `Γ(affine U) → Γ(X×{y})`
657	     being a `k̄`-algebra map into a field that is finite over `k̄`
658	     (`AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` under `LocallyOfFiniteType`) and
659	     `k̄` algebraically closed, the map `X × {y} → U` factors through a single point. This is the
660	     "global-sections-constant" argument; assembling it into "image is one point" is the
661	     remaining work.
662	
663	These are exactly the "two Mathlib bridges to find/build" flagged in `PROGRESS.md`. Both bridges
664	are char-free (no theorem of the cube, no cohomology beyond `H⁰`). Bridge 1 is built
665	(`snd_left_isClosedMap`) and the non-emptiness fibre fact (`hfib`) is closed, so
666	`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` is `sorry`-free; bridge 2 (slice-constancy) is decomposed and
667	assembled (Steps 1–2), with Step 1's geometric slice/section assembly
668	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`) now PROVEN. The categorical reduction
669	(`rigidity_lemma`, `rigidity_snd_lift`) and the scheme-level gluing (`rigidity_core`) are closed —
670	the entire chain is axiom-clean (iter-162). -/
671	theorem rigidity_core
672	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
673	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
674	    [IsProper X.hom]
675	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
676	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
677	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
678	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
679	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
680	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
681	    (y₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Y)
682	    (z₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Z)
683	    (_hf : lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀) :
684	    f = lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f := by
685	  -- The gluing step is scheme-level rigidity: two maps out of a geometrically irreducible reduced
686	  -- source into a separated target that agree on a non-empty open agree everywhere. We replicate
687	  -- the argument of `AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity`'s `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` inline (that
688	  -- file is *downstream* of this one, so its wrapper is unavailable here) directly from Mathlib's
689	  -- `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`. The non-empty open and the agreement on it are the
690	  -- genuine geometric content, isolated in `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`.
691	  obtain ⟨U, hU, h⟩ := rigidity_eqOn_dense_open f x₀ y₀ z₀ _hf
692	  -- `Spec k̄` is a single point, so geometric irreducibility makes `(X ⊗ Y).left` irreducible.
693	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace (X ⊗ Y).left :=
694	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (X ⊗ Y).hom
695	  -- A non-empty open of an irreducible space is dense, so its inclusion is dominant.
696	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : (X ⊗ Y).left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left) :=
697	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
698	  -- Provide separatedness of `Z.left` over `Spec k̄` in the `OverClass.fromOver` form.
699	  haveI : IsSeparated (Z.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := ‹IsSeparated Z.hom›
700	  -- Promote the underlying-scheme equality to an `Over (Spec k̄)` equality.
701	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
702	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of kbar))
703	    (X := (X ⊗ Y).left) (Y := Z.left) (f := f.left)
704	    (g := (lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) ≫ f).left) U.ι h
705	
706	/-- **Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I).** Let `X` be a complete (proper) variety and `Y`, `Z`
707	any varieties over `k̄`. If `f : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Z` collapses the slice `X × {y₀}` to a single point
708	`z₀` (encoded as `lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀`), then `f` factors through
709	the second projection `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y`, i.e. there is `g : Y ⟶ Z` with `f = snd ≫ g`.
710	
711	This is the cube-free, cohomology-free entry point of the chain: its only inputs are that
712	completeness of `X` makes the projection a closed map, and that a proper connected variety has
713	no nonconstant map to an affine variety. Valid in arbitrary characteristic.

718	## iter-157 signature correction (the scaffold was false as stated)
719	
720	The iter-157 scaffold carried only `[IsProper X.hom]`. **That statement is false**: take `X` =
721	two disjoint reduced `k̄`-points (proper but disconnected), `Y` = two points, `Z = X`, and
722	`f : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Z` collapsing the `y₀`-fibre to one point while separating the other fibre. Then
723	`_hf` holds yet `f` does not factor through `snd`. Mumford's hypothesis is that `X` is a complete
724	**variety** (irreducible) and `Y`, `Z` are **varieties**; the formal statement therefore needs
725	`X ⊗ Y` geometrically irreducible and reduced (so the dense-open rigidity glue applies) and `Z`
726	separated (so agreement on a dense open propagates). These three instances are added; they are
727	exactly what `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` (the gluing step) consumes, and what Milne's Rigidity
728	Theorem 1.1 lists ("`V` complete, `V × W` geometrically irreducible, `Z` separated").
729	
730	## iter-157 prover progress
731	
732	The categorical *skeleton* is fully discharged here; only the single geometric core
733	remains. Concretely, Mumford "chooses any point `x₀ ∈ X`" and sets `g(y) = f(x₀, y)`. We
734	make this explicit by adding a `k̄`-point `x₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ X` to the hypotheses (Mumford's
735	"complete variety `X`" is nonempty, so over `k̄` such a point exists; the downstream
736	consumer `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` supplies it from `ℙ¹`). The witness is then
737	
738	  `g := lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) ≫ f`   (the section `y ↦ (x₀, y)` post-composed with `f`).
739	
740	The goal `f = snd X Y ≫ g` rewrites — by pure cartesian-monoidal algebra (`comp_lift`,
741	`toUnit` uniqueness, `Category.comp_id`) — to
742	
743	  `f = retract ≫ f`,   where   `retract := lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`
744	
745	is the endomorphism `(x, y) ↦ (x₀, y)` of `X ⊗ Y` that collapses the `X`-axis onto `x₀`.
746	This reduction (lemma `rigidity_snd_lift`, then one `rw`) is closed below. The
747	remaining `f = retract ≫ f` is the genuine geometric heart (`rigidity_core`): `f` depends
748	only on the `Y`-coordinate. Its proof is Mumford's properness/closed-map/affine-constant
749	argument — see `rigidity_core` for the full decomposition into the two char-free bridges.
750	
751	**Status (iter-162)**: PROVEN axiom-clean. Categorical reduction (`rigidity_snd_lift`) and
752	scheme-level gluing (`rigidity_core`, `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`) all closed; bridge 2
753	(`rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`) decomposed and assembled — Step 2, Step 1's deep
754	algebra (`eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral`), and Step 1's geometric slice/section assembly
755	(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`, via `isIntegral_of_retract`) all PROVEN. The
756	whole Rigidity-Lemma chain is `sorry`-free. -/
757	theorem rigidity_lemma
758	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
759	    {X Y Z : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
760	    [IsProper X.hom]
761	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ⊗ Y).hom]
762	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom]
763	    [IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left]
764	    [IsSeparated Z.hom]
765	    (f : (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ Z)
766	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X)
767	    (y₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Y)
768	    (z₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Z)
769	    (_hf : lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ y₀) ≫ f = toUnit X ≫ z₀) :
770	    ∃ g : Y ⟶ Z, f = snd X Y ≫ g := by
771	  -- Mumford's witness: `g(y) = f(x₀, y)`, i.e. restrict `f` to the slice `{x₀} × Y`.
772	  refine ⟨lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) ≫ f, ?_⟩
773	  -- Reassociate and collapse the projection-then-section composite.
774	  rw [← Category.assoc, rigidity_snd_lift]
775	  -- Goal is now the geometric core `f = retract ≫ f`.
776	  exact rigidity_core f x₀ y₀ z₀ _hf

778	/-! ## The Milne §I.3 chain: additivity and homomorphisms
779	
780	From the proven `rigidity_lemma` we derive the two additive-structure corollaries of Milne §I.1
781	that feed the genus-`0` base case (Route C): the additive decomposition of a morphism out of a
782	product (Corollary 1.5) and the fact that a pointed regular map of abelian varieties is a group
783	homomorphism (Corollary 1.2). Both are cube-free and cohomology-free — pure consequences of the
784	Rigidity Lemma and the `GrpObj`-induced group structure on hom-sets
785	(`CategoryTheory.MonObj.Hom.group`/`Hom.monoid`: `u * v = lift u v ≫ μ`, `u⁻¹ = u ≫ ι`,
786	`(1 : X ⟶ A) = toUnit X ≫ η`). -/
787	
788	/-- **Additive decomposition over a product (Milne Corollary 1.5).** Let `V` be complete (proper)
789	and `V ⊗ W` a variety (geometrically irreducible, reduced, locally of finite type) over an
790	algebraically closed `k̄`, and let `A` be an abelian variety. Then any morphism `h : V ⊗ W ⟶ A`
791	based at the identity (`h(v₀, w₀) = η[A]`, encoded `lift v₀ w₀ ≫ h = η[A]`) decomposes — in the
792	`GrpObj`-induced group on `Hom(V ⊗ W, A)` — as the product of its two axis-restrictions pulled back
793	along the projections:
794	
795	  `h = (p ≫ f) · (q ≫ g)`,
796	
797	where `p = fst V W`, `q = snd V W`, `f = (v ↦ (v, w₀)) ≫ h` is `h|_{V × {w₀}}`, and
798	`g = (w ↦ (v₀, w)) ≫ h` is `h|_{{v₀} × W}`. The operation `·` is the hom-group multiplication
799	(`u * v = lift u v ≫ μ`); the statement does **not** assume `A` commutative.
800	
801	PROOF (Milne, Cor 1.5, §I.1). Form the group difference `φ := h / ((p ≫ f) · (q ≫ g))`. A direct
802	hom-group computation shows `φ` collapses the complete `V`-axis `V × {w₀}` to the identity (this is
803	exactly the `_hf` collapse hypothesis of `rigidity_lemma`, using `h(v₀, w₀) = η[A]`), so by the
804	Rigidity Lemma `φ = q ≫ g'` factors through the second projection; `φ` also vanishes on the
805	section `{v₀} × W` of `q`, forcing `g' = 1`, hence `φ = 1` and `h = (p ≫ f) · (q ≫ g)`. -/
806	theorem hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
807	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
808	    {V W : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
809	    [IsProper V.hom]
810	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (V ⊗ W).hom]
811	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (V ⊗ W).hom]
812	    [IsReduced (V ⊗ W).left]
813	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
814	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
815	    (v₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ V)
816	    (w₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ W)
817	    (h : V ⊗ W ⟶ A)
818	    (hh : lift v₀ w₀ ≫ h = η[A]) :
819	    h = (fst V W ≫ (lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ h)) *
820	        (snd V W ≫ (lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ h)) := by
821	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
822	  -- Name the two axis-restrictions (folding the goal's RHS).
823	  set f : V ⟶ A := lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ h with hf
824	  set g : W ⟶ A := lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ h with hg
825	  -- Projection/section identities for the two slice sections.
826	  have hsVfst : lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ fst V W = 𝟙 V := by simp
827	  have hsVsnd : lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ snd V W = toUnit V ≫ w₀ := by simp
828	  have hsWfst : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ fst V W = toUnit W ≫ v₀ := by simp
829	  have hsWsnd : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ snd V W = 𝟙 W := by simp
830	  -- The cross-restrictions of `h` are the identity, because `h(v₀, w₀) = η[A]`.
831	  have hwsW : w₀ ≫ lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) = lift v₀ w₀ := by
832	    rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id, ← Category.assoc,
833	      toUnit_unique (w₀ ≫ toUnit W) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
834	  have hvsV : v₀ ≫ lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) = lift v₀ w₀ := by
835	    rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id, ← Category.assoc,
836	      toUnit_unique (v₀ ≫ toUnit V) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
837	  have hwg : w₀ ≫ g = η[A] := by rw [hg, ← Category.assoc, hwsW, hh]
838	  have hvf : v₀ ≫ f = η[A] := by rw [hf, ← Category.assoc, hvsV, hh]
839	  -- The group difference `φ = h / (f∘p · g∘q)`.
840	  set φ : V ⊗ W ⟶ A := h / ((fst V W ≫ f) * (snd V W ≫ g)) with hφ
841	  -- `φ` collapses the complete `V`-axis `V × {w₀}` to the identity.
842	  have hcolV : lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ φ = toUnit V ≫ η[A] := by
843	    rw [← Hom.one_def, hφ, GrpObj.comp_div, ← hf, MonObj.comp_mul,
844	      ← Category.assoc, hsVfst, Category.id_comp,
845	      ← Category.assoc, hsVsnd, Category.assoc, hwg, ← Hom.one_def, _root_.mul_one, div_self']
846	  -- `φ` vanishes on the `{v₀} × W` section of the second projection.
847	  have hcolW : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ φ = (1 : W ⟶ A) := by
848	    rw [hφ, GrpObj.comp_div, ← hg, MonObj.comp_mul,
849	      ← Category.assoc, hsWfst, Category.assoc, hvf, ← Hom.one_def,
850	      ← Category.assoc, hsWsnd, Category.id_comp, _root_.one_mul, div_self']
851	  -- Rigidity: `φ` factors through the second projection.
852	  obtain ⟨g', hg'⟩ := rigidity_lemma φ v₀ w₀ η[A] hcolV
853	  -- The factor is the identity, read off the `{v₀} × W` section.
854	  have hg'1 : g' = 1 := by
855	    have hsec : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ φ = g' := by
856	      rw [hg', ← Category.assoc, hsWsnd, Category.id_comp]
857	    rw [← hsec, hcolW]
858	  have hφ1 : φ = 1 := by rw [hg', hg'1, MonObj.comp_one]
859	  -- Conclude `h = f∘p · g∘q`.
860	  have hdiv : h / ((fst V W ≫ f) * (snd V W ≫ g)) = 1 := by rw [← hφ]; exact hφ1
861	  exact div_eq_one.mp hdiv
862	
863	/-- **A pointed regular map of abelian varieties is a homomorphism (Milne Corollary 1.2).** Let
864	`A`, `B` be abelian varieties over an algebraically closed `k̄` (with `A ⊗ A` a variety —
865	geometrically irreducible, reduced, locally of finite type, the hypotheses
866	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` consumes with `V = W = A`). If a regular map `α : A ⟶ B` sends the
867	identity to the identity (`η[A] ≫ α = η[B]`), then `α` is a monoid (hence group) homomorphism:
868	`IsMonHom α`, i.e. `α(a · a') = α(a) · α(a')`.
869	
870	PROOF (Milne, Cor 1.2, §I.1). Apply the additive decomposition `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
871	(Cor 1.5) to `h := μ[A] ≫ α : A ⊗ A ⟶ B`, based at `η[A]` (the hypothesis `h(η, η) = η[B]` is
872	`η[A] ≫ α = η[B]` since `η[A]` is the hom-group identity). The two axis-restrictions of `h` both
873	collapse to `α` (by the monoid unit laws `lift_comp_one_right`/`left`: `(a, η) ↦ a` and
874	`(η, a) ↦ a`), so the decomposition reads `μ[A] ≫ α = (fst ≫ α) · (snd ≫ α) = (α ⊗ α) ≫ μ[B]`,
875	which is exactly the `mul_hom` axiom; `one_hom` is the pointed hypothesis. -/
876	theorem av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero
877	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
878	    {A B : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
879	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
880	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (A ⊗ A).hom]
881	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (A ⊗ A).hom]
882	    [IsReduced (A ⊗ A).left]
883	    [GrpObj B] [IsProper B.hom]
884	    (α : A ⟶ B) (hα : η[A] ≫ α = η[B]) :
885	    IsMonHom α := by
886	  -- `η[A]` is the identity of the hom-group `Hom(𝟙_, A)`, so `η[A] · η[A] = η[A]`.
887	  have h1 : (η[A] : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ A) = 1 := by
888	    rw [Hom.one_def, toUnit_unique (toUnit _) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
889	  have hbase : lift η[A] η[A] ≫ μ[A] = η[A] := by
890	    rw [← Hom.mul_def, h1, _root_.mul_one]
891	  -- Corollary 1.5 applied to `h := μ[A] ≫ α` with `V = W = A`, based at `η[A]`.
892	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity (V := A) (W := A) (A := B)
893	    η[A] η[A] (μ[A] ≫ α) (by rw [← Category.assoc, hbase, hα])
894	  -- Both axis-restrictions of `μ[A] ≫ α` reduce to `α` (monoid unit laws).
895	  rw [show lift (𝟙 A) (toUnit A ≫ η[A]) ≫ μ[A] ≫ α = α by
896	        rw [← Category.assoc, lift_comp_one_right, Category.id_comp],
897	      show lift (toUnit A ≫ η[A]) (𝟙 A) ≫ μ[A] ≫ α = α by
898	        rw [← Category.assoc, lift_comp_one_left, Category.id_comp]] at key
899	  -- `key : μ[A] ≫ α = (fst A A ≫ α) · (snd A A ≫ α)`; package as `IsMonHom`.
900	  exact { one_hom := hα, mul_hom := by rw [key, Hom.mul_def, lift_fst_comp_snd_comp] }
901	
902	end AlgebraicGeometry
903

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
64	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
65	  [IsProper C.hom]
66	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
67	
68	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
69	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
70	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
71	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
72	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
73	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
74	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
75	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
76	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
77	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
78	
79	namespace IsAlbanese
80	
81	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
82	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
84	  Classical.choose h
85	
86	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
87	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
88	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
89	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
90	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
91	
92	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
95	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
96	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
97	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
98	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
99	
100	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
101	universal morphisms. -/
102	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
103	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
104	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
105	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
106	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
107	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
108	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
109	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
110	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
111	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
112	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
113	    simp
114	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
115	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
116	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
117	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
118	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
119	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
120	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
121	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
122	    simp
123	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
124	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
125	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
126	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
127	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
128	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
129	
130	end IsAlbanese
131	
132	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
133	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
134	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
135	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
136	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
137	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
138	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
139	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
140	      (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
141	
142	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
143	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
144	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
145	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
146	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
147	
148	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
149	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
150	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
151	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
152	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
153	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
154	
155	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
156	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
157	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
159	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
160	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
161	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
162	  grpObj : GrpObj J
163	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
164	  proper : IsProper J.hom
165	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
166	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
167	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
168	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
169	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
170	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
171	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
172	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
173	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
174	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
175	
176	/-- Genus-`0` Albanese witness. The witness object is the trivial terminal scheme
177	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec k))`; the `JacobianWitness` structural fields (proper, smooth,
178	geometrically irreducible, genus-stable, group-object) reduce to the
179	corresponding trivialities on `Spec k` via the structural cluster
180	`(𝟙_).hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)`. The `isAlbaneseFor` field consumes the **genus-0
181	abelian-variety rigidity** statement: for every pointed `(C, P)` and every
182	target group scheme `(A, η[A])`, the genus-0 hypothesis forces a morphism
183	`f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]` to equal `toUnit C ≫ η[A]`. Over an
184	algebraically closed base `k̄` this is
185	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (in
186	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`, char-free, route-C, iter-163).
187	For a general `[Field k]` base the keystone is consumed via pullback to
188	`Spec k̄` + descent of the morphism equality along the faithfully-flat
189	surjection `Spec k̄ → Spec k`. The uniqueness clause cancels the epimorphism
190	`toUnit C` via `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective` (smooth ⟹ flat,
191	geometrically irreducible ⟹ surjective on `Spec k`).
192	
193	**Status (iter-172):** the `key` body remains `sorry`; the rigidity consumer
194	exists (declared) but still propagates upstream sorries from the iter-171
195	body skeleton in `Genus0BaseObjects.lean`. Wiring closes once
196	(i) the `Genus0BaseObjects` body-skeleton internal sorries discharge, and
197	(ii) the `k → k̄` pullback / descent step is filled. -/
198	noncomputable def genusZeroWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
199	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
200	    (h : genus C = 0) :
201	    JacobianWitness C where
202	  J := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))
203	  grpObj := inferInstance
204	  -- `(𝟙_).hom` is defeq `𝟙 (Spec k)`, but instance search does not unfold it;
205	  -- supply each instance for the identity and let term-elaboration close by defeq.
206	  proper := (inferInstance : IsProper (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
207	  smooth := (inferInstance : Smooth (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
208	  geomIrred := geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec k
209	  smoothGenus := by
210	    rw [h]; exact (inferInstance : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
211	  isAlbaneseFor := by
212	    -- For every `k`-rational marked point `P`, exhibit the Albanese datum for
213	    -- the pointed curve `(C, P)` with `J = 𝟙_` (blueprint C.2 / C.3).
214	    intro P
215	    -- The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map `toUnit C`.
216	    refine ⟨toUnit C, ?_, ?_⟩
217	    · -- Pointed condition: both `P ≫ toUnit C` and `η[𝟙_]` are maps `𝟙_ ⟶ 𝟙_`,
218	      -- equal by uniqueness of morphisms into the terminal object.
219	      exact toUnit_unique _ _
220	    · -- Universal factorisation: given `f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]`, the
221	      -- factoring morphism `𝟙_ ⟶ A` is the identity element `η[A]`.
222	      intro A _ _ _ _ f _hf
223	      -- Genus-0 RIGIDITY (C.2): `f` equals the constant morphism at the identity.
224	      -- The route-C content `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (declared in
225	      -- `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`) is char-free and import-clean
226	      -- (proven iter-163 onward). To consume it for the general `[Field k]` goal,
227	      -- this body needs to pull back `(C, A, f, P)` along an algebraic closure
228	      -- `Spec k̄ → Spec k`, apply `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` in the
229	      -- pulled-back setting, then descend the morphism equality along the
230	      -- faithfully-flat surjection (the descent step itself is
231	      -- `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`, already used below to cancel the
232	      -- terminal epimorphism). The pullback functor + the descent of a morphism
233	      -- equality is the residual sub-build; it is real but is not gated on
234	      -- any out-of-file plan-level decision.
235	      have key : f = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
236	        sorry
237	      refine ⟨η[A], key, ?_⟩
238	      -- Uniqueness: any `g'` with `f = toUnit C ≫ g'` equals `η[A]`, by cancelling
239	      -- the epimorphism `toUnit C`. Its underlying scheme morphism is `C.hom`
240	      -- (`Over.toUnit_left`), which is faithfully flat (smooth ⟹ flat) and
241	      -- surjective (a geometrically irreducible curve over a field is nonempty and
242	      -- `Spec k` is a single point), hence an epimorphism (`Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`).
243	      intro g' hg'
244	      have hcancel : toUnit C ≫ g' = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by rw [← hg', ← key]
245	      have hepiL : Epi (toUnit C).left := by
246	        rw [Over.toUnit_left]; exact Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective C.hom
247	      haveI : Epi (toUnit C) := Over.epi_of_epi_left _
248	      exact (cancel_epi (toUnit C)).mp hcancel
249	
250	/-- The Albanese witness for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C`
251	of positive genus `g ≥ 1` over `k`. The underlying scheme is the Albanese
252	variety of `C` — classically the connected component of the identity of
253	`Pic_{C/k}`, or equivalently the Stein factorisation of the Abel–Jacobi
254	morphism on `Sym^g(C)`. Both constructions require infrastructure not yet
255	in Mathlib (FGA representability of the Picard functor for Route A;
256	symmetric powers + finite-group quotients + Stein factorisation +
257	Brill–Noether–Riemann–Roch for Route B); the iter-123 M3 route audit
258	(`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`) records both at midpoint ~6500 and
259	~9000 LOC respectively.
260	
261	**Status**: iter-134 scaffold — body is `sorry`. The body closure is M3
262	work, currently OFF-CRITICAL-PATH until M2 closes (per STRATEGY.md
263	§ M3, user-escalation-pending).
264	
265	**Iter-135**: the genus-stratified body restructure of
266	`nonempty_jacobianWitness` is now in place (Change 2.1 of iter-135
267	refactor); this scaffold is the positive-genus arm. Body closure
268	remains M3 work (off-critical-path; user-escalation-pending per
269	`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`). -/
270	noncomputable def positiveGenusWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
271	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
272	    (hg : 0 < genus C) :
273	    JacobianWitness C :=
274	  sorry
275	
276	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
277	curve. This is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C Jacobian
278	scaffolding (the other being `genusZeroWitness`); both are scheduled for body
279	closure post M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. It packages all five protected sorries
280	(`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`,
281	`instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records
282	that the Albanese property of the underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every
283	choice of $k$-rational marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor`
284	field of `JacobianWitness`).
285	
286	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
287	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
288	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
289	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
290	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
291	existence is assumed.
292	
293	**Iter-135 body restructure**: the body is now a `by_cases h :
294	genus C = 0` decomposition consuming `genusZeroWitness` (genus-0 arm)
295	and `positiveGenusWitness` (positive-genus arm). Both arms' bodies
296	remain `sorry` (M2.b body closure iter-138+; M3 body closure
297	off-critical-path); the restructure converts the inline `sorry` here
298	into honest delegation to the two scaffolds, removing one inline-`sorry`
299	site without prejudging either arm's body closure. -/
300	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
301	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
302	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := by
303	  by_cases h : genus C = 0
304	  · exact ⟨genusZeroWitness C h⟩
305	  · exact ⟨positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)⟩
306	
307	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
308	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
309	on it. -/
310	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
311	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
312	    JacobianWitness C :=
313	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
314	
315	-- data
316	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
317	
318	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
319	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
320	witness is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C scaffolding
321	(the other being `genusZeroWitness`), both scheduled for body closure post
322	M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the
323	witness — a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of
324	relative dimension `0` is `Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is
325	absorbed into `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
326	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
327	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
328	  (jacobianWitness C).J
329	
330	namespace Jacobian
331	
332	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
333	
334	-- data
335	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
336	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
337	
338	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
339	genus of `C`. -/
340	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
341	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
342	
343	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
344	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
345	
346	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
347	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
348	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
349	
350	end Jacobian
351	
352	end AlgebraicGeometry
353

4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the structure presheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (1/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	ships the categorical gap-fills, the sheafification / `Ext` typeclass prerequisites,
13	and the per-open ring-map helpers (1)–(5) that culminate in the presheaf-of-
14	`k`-modules `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6). Its sibling `SheafProperty.lean`
15	discharges the sheaf condition and bundles `toModuleKSheaf` (helpers 7–8); the
16	sibling `Carriers.lean` carries the downstream finite-length carriers and producer
17	instances.
18	
19	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
20	-/
21	
22	set_option autoImplicit false
23	
24	universe u v
25	
26	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	universe u₂ v₂
31	
32	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is additive when `D`
33	is preadditive. The `map_add` field reduces to `f + g = f + g` componentwise after
34	`ext; rfl`. -/
35	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
36	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
37	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
38	
39	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear when
40	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
41	load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
42	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
43	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
44	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
45	    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
46	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
47	
48	namespace Adjunction
49	
50	variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
51	    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
52	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]
53	
54	include adj
55	
56	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
57	then `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
58	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
59	default simp lemmas. -/
60	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
61	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
62	    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])
63	
64	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
65	then `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
66	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
67	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
68	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
69	
70	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): linear lifting of `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`. Under
71	`[F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]`, the additive equivalence
72	`(F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃+ (X ⟶ G.obj Y)` upgrades to a `LinearEquiv` over `R`. The
73	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
74	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
75	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
76	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
77	    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
78	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
79	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
80	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
81	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
82	      simp [adj.homEquiv_unit] }
83	
84	end Adjunction
85	
86	end CategoryTheory
87	
88	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
89	
90	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
91	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
92	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
93	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
94	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
95	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
96	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
97	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
98	  inferInstance
99	
100	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
101	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
102	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
103	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
104	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
105	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
106	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
107	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
108	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
109	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
110	
111	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
112	
113	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
114	
115	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
116	
117	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
118	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
119	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
120	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
121	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
122	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
123	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
124	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
125	
126	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
127	map of `kToSection`. -/
128	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
129	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
130	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
131	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
132	
133	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
134	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
135	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
136	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
137	  rfl
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
140	natural in `U`. -/
141	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
142	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
143	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
144	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
145	  rfl
146	
147	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
148	`algebraMap`. -/
149	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
150	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
151	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
152	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
153	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
154	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
155	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
156	
157	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
158	
159	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
160	
161	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
162	
163	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
164	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
165	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
166	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
167	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
168	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
169	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
170	      map_add' := fun x y => by
171	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
172	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
173	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
174	        congr 1
175	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
176	          (C := C) f r }
177	  map_id U := by
178	    ext x
179	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
180	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
181	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
182	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
183	    ext x
184	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
185	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
186	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
187	
188	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
189	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
190	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
191	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
192	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
193	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
194	  rfl
195	
196	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
197

2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.

4	Authors: Christian Merten

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.Presheaf
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the sheaf property and bundled structure sheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (2/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	discharges the sheaf condition for `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 7) and bundles the
13	result as `toModuleKSheaf` (helper 8); the forget-and-recover natural iso to the
14	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf is also packaged here. The downstream
15	finite-length carriers live in the sibling `Carriers.lean`.
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
18	-/
19	
20	set_option autoImplicit false
21	
22	universe u v
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
27	
28	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
29	
30	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
31	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
32	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
33	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
34	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
35	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
36	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
37	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
40	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
41	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
42	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
43	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
44	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
45	
46	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
47	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
48	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
49	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
50	via `forget₂` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
51	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
52	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
53	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
54	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
55	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
56	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
57	  Iso.refl _
58	
59	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
60

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.SheafProperty
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: finite-length carriers and producer instances
10	
11	Sub-file (3/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	hosts the downstream finite-length carriers (`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`,
13	`IsHModuleHomFinite`), their algebraic bridges (the `HModule` / `HModule'`
14	abbrevs, the H⁰ `LinearEquiv` transport companions, the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction
15	upgrade), and the curve-side producer instances. Upstream sub-files
16	`Presheaf.lean` and `SheafProperty.lean` provide the structure-sheaf object the
17	carriers depend on.
18	
19	Downstream finite-length carriers in this file:
20	
21	* H>0 affine cohomology vanishing is carried by iter-040's
22	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` (the producer instance for the curve case
23	  lives at iter-051).
24	* H⁰ Hom-finiteness is carried by iter-043's **wholespace**
25	  `IsHModuleHomFinite`; the producer instance on a proper geometrically
26	  integral $k$-curve is supplied at iter-046 from Stein finiteness
27	  (`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper`).
28	* The iter-041 per-affine-open variant `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` was an
29	  abandoned attempt (dead scaffolding — $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is not finite
30	  over $k$ on a proper affine open of a non-trivial curve) and has been
31	  removed from this file.
32	
33	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
34	-/
35	
36	set_option autoImplicit false
37	
38	universe u v
39	
40	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
43	
44	/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
45	`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
46	parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
47	requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
48	automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
49	`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
50	`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
51	through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
52	noncomputable abbrev HModule
53	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
54	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
55	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
56	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) :=
57	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n
58	
59	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
60	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
61	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
62	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)` in any
63	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
64	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
65	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
66	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`;
67	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
68	identify `H⁰(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Γ(C, O_C)` viewed as a
69	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
70	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
71	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
72	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
73	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
74	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
75	    HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k]
76	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :=
77	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
78	
79	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
80	cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
81	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
82	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
83	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
84	
85	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
86	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
87	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
88	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
89	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` — under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
90	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).
91	
92	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
93	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
94	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
95	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
96	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n ≅ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
97	for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
98	iter-015+. -/
99	noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
100	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
101	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
102	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
103	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=
104	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
105	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
106	
107	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
108	mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
109	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
110	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
111	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)`
112	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
113	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
114	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
115	`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
116	closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
117	plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
118	derived `H⁰(C, O_C) ≃ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
119	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
120	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
121	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
122	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
123	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
124	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
125	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
126	    HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
127	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
128	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) :=
129	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
130	
131	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule k F 0`. The iter-010
132	H⁰ bridge `HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf J _).obj
133	(ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` identifies the degree-zero cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom
134	group; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across.
135	The `.symm` is required: iter-010's bridge has `HModule k F 0` on the LHS and the
136	Hom group on the RHS, but we want the Hom hypothesis on the LHS and the
137	`HModule` conclusion on the RHS, so we apply `.symm` first. Mirrors iter-037's
138	`module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄` pattern at degree $0$, with no Mayer-Vietoris
139	machinery required. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k
140	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness
141	input is supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves. -/
142	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero
143	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
144	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
145	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
146	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
147	    [Module.Finite k ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)] :
148	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0) :=
149	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule_zero_linearEquiv k F).symm
150	
151	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule' k F 0 X`. Parallel
152	of `Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised carrier
153	`HModule'`. The iter-015 H⁰ bridge `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv : HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
154	((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X) ⟶ F)` identifies the
155	degree-zero open-evaluation cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom group from the sheafified
156	representable; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness
157	across. The `.symm` is required for the same orientation reason as in
158	`Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero`. -/
159	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero
160	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
161	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
162	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
163	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
164	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C)
165	    [Module.Finite k ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
166	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F)] :
167	    Module.Finite k (HModule' k F 0 X) :=
168	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule'_zero_linearEquiv k F X).symm

170	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero` to
171	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`. The
172	Grothendieck topology `Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat` is auto-inferred
173	via the iter-005 instances `instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK` and
174	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK`. The sheaf argument is inferred from the
175	result type. Mirrors iter-030 / iter-035 / iter-036 / iter-037's `_curve`
176	patterns. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k (HModule k
177	(toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness input is
178	supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves (typically the morally
179	trivial `H^0(C, O_C) ≃ k` from Stein factorization on a connected proper
180	curve). -/
181	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_curve
182	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
183	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
184	    [Module.Finite k
185	      ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
186	        (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
187	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
188	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero k _

190	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule'_zero` to
191	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`,
192	evaluated at an arbitrary open `U` of the underlying topological space. Parallel
193	of `module_finite_HModule_zero_curve` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised
194	carrier `HModule'`.  Same auto-inferred topology / instance setup. The open `U`
195	is an explicit parameter (unlike the implicit topology); the sheaf argument is
196	inferred. Used downstream in the cover-evaluation chain for proper geometrically
197	integral $k$-curves: for each affine corner $X_i$ of an `AffineCoverMVSquare`,
198	the iter-039 transport propagates `Module.Finite k`-ness from the Hom group
199	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ free_k).obj X_i) ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)` —
200	morally `Module.Finite k (Γ(X_i, O_C))` for affine $X_i$ — to the H⁰ cohomology
201	piece `HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0 X_i`. -/
202	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero_curve
203	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
204	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
205	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
206	    [Module.Finite k
207	      ((presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
208	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ⟶
209	          Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
210	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0 U) :=
211	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule'_zero k _ U
