9	/-!
10	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 3): standard `k̄`-points on `ℙ¹`; `𝔾_a` and `𝔾_m`
11	
12	This file is **Stratum 3** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
14	
15	* the three standard `k̄`-points `0 = [0 : 1]`, `1 = [1 : 1]`, `∞ = [1 : 0]` of
16	  `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, encoded as sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`;
17	* the additive group `Ga` over `Spec k̄` (= `AffineSpace (Fin 1)`) and its
18	  affine, finite-presentation, and reduced instances;
19	* the multiplicative group `Gm` over `Spec k̄` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) and its
20	  affine, finite-presentation, reduced, domain, and irreducible-space instances,
21	  plus the scaffold `GrpObj Gm` and the resulting `Smooth Gm.hom`;
22	* the multiplicative identity `Gm.onePt = η[Gm kbar]`.
23	
24	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`. Downstream stratum: `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
39	
40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
120	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
121	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
122	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
123	
124	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
125	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
126	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
128	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
129	
130	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
131	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
132	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
133	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
134	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
135	
136	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
137	
138	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
139	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
140	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
141	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
142	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
143	
144	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
145	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
146	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
147	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
148	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
149	
150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance
239	
240	/-! ### `gm_grpObj` — `GrpObj` instance on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`
241	
242	The route is per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy (8-step recipe).
243	
244	Step A: build the units-of-global-sections functor `gmHomFunctor : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat`,
245	which sends `T ↦ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents.
246	
247	Step B: build the `RepresentableBy` witness asserting `Hom(-, Gm) ≃ Γ(-, ⊤)ˣ` naturally.
248	The per-`T` bijection is the 3-step chain (over-cat unfold → Γ-Spec adjunction →
249	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` universal property of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`).
250	
251	Step C: `gm_grpObj := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) gmHomFunctor _`. -/
252	
253	/-- The `(Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}` functor of units of global sections.
254	This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents. -/
255	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
256	    (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u} where
257	  obj T := GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
258	  map {_ _} φ := GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.left.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
259	  map_id := by
260	    intro T
261	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
262	    apply MonoidHom.ext
263	    intro u
264	    apply Units.ext
265	    change ((𝟙 T.unop : T.unop ⟶ T.unop).left.appTop).hom u.val = u.val
266	    rw [Over.id_left]
267	    simp
268	  map_comp := by
269	    intros T T' T'' φ ψ
270	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
271	    apply MonoidHom.ext
272	    intro u
273	    apply Units.ext
274	    change ((φ ≫ ψ).unop.left.appTop).hom u.val
275	      = ((ψ.unop.left.appTop).hom (((φ.unop.left.appTop).hom) u.val))
276	    rw [show (φ ≫ ψ).unop = ψ.unop ≫ φ.unop from rfl, Over.comp_left]
277	    simp
278	
279	/-- **Forward** of the per-`T` bijection: a morphism `f : T ⟶ Gm kbar` in `Over (Spec k̄)`
280	gives the unit in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` corresponding to the image of the standard generator
281	`t ∈ k̄[t, t⁻¹]` under the induced ring map.
282	
283	Concretely, `f.left.appTop : Γ(Spec(GmRing), ⊤) ⟶ Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; composing with
284	`ΓSpecIso.inv` gives a ring map `GmRing → Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. The image of the algebra-map
285	generator `t ∈ GmRing` (a unit via `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`) is a unit
286	in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. -/
287	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_toFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
288	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (f : T ⟶ Gm kbar) : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ :=
289	  letI ringMap : (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)) ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
290	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
291	  Units.map ringMap.hom.toMonoidHom
292	    (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit
293	      (S := GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)).unit
294	
295	/-- Helper for `gmHomEquiv_invFun`: the underlying scheme morphism factors over `Spec k̄`.
296	Substantive content: reduces to `IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp` + `MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq`
297	+ `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` (the Γ⊣Spec unit triangle). -/
298	private lemma gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
299	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) :
300	    letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
301	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
302	    letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
303	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
304	    haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
305	      change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
306	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
307	      exact u.isUnit
308	    letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
309	      IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
310	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
311	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap)) ≫ (Gm kbar).hom = T.hom := by
312	  -- Set up local abbreviations matching the letI's of the goal.
313	  set structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
314	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
315	  set polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
316	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val) with polyMap_def
317	  have hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
318	    rw [polyMap_def, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
319	    exact u.isUnit
320	  set liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
321	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
322	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg with liftedMap_def
323	  -- Unfold `(Gm kbar).hom`.
324	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap) ≫
325	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
326	  -- Combine the Spec.maps.
327	  rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
328	  -- `liftedMap ∘ algebraMap kbar GmRing = structureRingMap.hom`.
329	  have halg : (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) =
330	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
331	        (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
332	    ext r
333	    exact (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) r).symm
334	  have hcomp : liftedMap.comp (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) = structureRingMap.hom := by
335	    change (IsLocalization.Away.lift _ hg).comp _ = _
336	    rw [halg, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
337	    ext r
338	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ ((algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) r) = _
339	    rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
340	  rw [hcomp]
341	  -- Now: T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map structureRingMap.hom = T.hom
342	  -- structureRingMap = ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ T.hom.appTop  (as CommRingCat morphism)
343	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫
344	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
345	          T.hom.appTop).hom)) = T.hom
346	  -- CommRingCat.ofHom (...).hom = the original morphism (since ofHom and .hom are inverses)
347	  rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
348	        T.hom.appTop).hom) = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop from
349	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom _]
350	  rw [Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality]
351	  -- Now: (T.hom ≫ (Spec _).toSpecΓ) ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso.inv) = T.hom
352	  rw [Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
353	
354	/-- **Backward** of the per-`T` bijection: a unit `u : Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` gives the morphism
355	`T ⟶ Gm kbar` defined by the ring map `k̄[t, t⁻¹] → Γ(T.left, ⊤)` sending `t ↦ u.val`.
356	
357	Concretely: build the polynomial-ring map `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)` via
358	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` (sending `X() ↦ u.val`); lift through the localization via
359	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` (using that `u.val` is a unit); convert to a scheme morphism
360	via `Spec.map`-then-`toSpecΓ`; bundle with the `Over`-commutativity proof. -/
361	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_invFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
362	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) : T ⟶ Gm kbar :=
363	  -- structure ring map kbar → Γ(T.left, ⊤), via T.hom.appTop and ΓSpecIso
364	  letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
365	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
366	  letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
367	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
368	  haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
369	    change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
370	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
371	    exact u.isUnit
372	  letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
373	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
374	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
375	  Over.homMk
376	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap))
377	    (gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver kbar T u)
378	
379	/-- Round-trip identity 1: `invFun ∘ toFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
380	the Γ⊣Spec adjunction injectivity (`ext_to_Spec`) + `IsLocalization.Away.lift` uniqueness
381	applied to the lifted ring map from the morphism's `appTop`. -/
382	private lemma gmHomEquiv_left_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
383	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
384	    Function.LeftInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
385	  intro f
386	  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
387	  -- Goal: (invFun (toFun f)).left = f.left as T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing).
388	  -- (a) `Over.w f` transferred through `Spec.appTop` + `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality`:
389	  --     `structureRingMap = algebraMap kbar GmRing ≫ g_f` (as CommRingCat morphisms),
390	  --     where `g_f := (ΓSpecIso (GmRing)).inv ≫ f.left.appTop`.
391	  have hw : (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop =
392	      CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) ≫
393	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop) := by
394	    have hOver := Over.w f
395	    -- `(Gm kbar).hom = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))` by defeq.
396	    change f.left ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
397	      at hOver
398	    rw [← hOver, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
399	    set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
400	      rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]
401	  -- (b) Compare the two morphisms `T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing)` via `ext_to_Spec`.
402	  apply AlgebraicGeometry.ext_to_Spec
403	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
404	      (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T f)).left.appTop
405	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
406	  -- Reduce LHS to `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f}` (same chain as in `right_inv`).
407	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
408	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
409	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (IsLocalization.Away.lift
410	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop
411	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
412	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
413	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
414	  -- Goal: `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f} = g_f` as CommRingCat morphisms.
415	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
416	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, IsLocalization.Away.lift]
417	  -- Apply `IsLocalization.lift_unique`: it suffices to show
418	  -- `g_f.hom ∘ algebraMap_{MvPoly→GmRing} = polyMap` on the MvPoly side.
419	  refine IsLocalization.lift_unique _ ?_
420	  change ∀ (x : MvPolynomial Unit kbar),
421	    (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop).hom.comp
422	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) x = (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _) x
423	  apply DFunLike.congr_fun
424	  -- Both sides are RingHoms `MvPoly Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. By `ringHom_ext`,
425	  -- it suffices to compare on `C r` (constants) and `X i` (variables).
426	  refine MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext (fun r => ?_) (fun i => ?_)
427	  · -- `C r`-case: by `Over.w f`, `g_f ∘ algebraMap_{kbar→GmRing} = structureRingMap`.
428	    have hwr := congrArg (fun (g : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ _) => g.hom r) hw
429	    simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom] at hwr
430	    simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
431	    -- Now LHS = g_f.hom (algebraMap kbar GmRing r), RHS = structureRingMap.hom r
432	    exact hwr.symm
433	  · -- `X i`-case: `i : Unit`, so `i = ()`; then both sides reduce to `(toFun f).val`.
434	    obtain ⟨⟩ := i
435	    simp [gmHomEquiv_toFun]
436	
437	/-- Round-trip identity 2: `toFun ∘ invFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
438	`IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` (image of the algebra-map generator is the unit)
439	plus `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` + `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop` (canceling the unit triangle). -/
440	private lemma gmHomEquiv_right_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
442	    Function.RightInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
443	  intro u
444	  apply Units.ext
445	  -- Stage the proof at the CommRingCat morphism level.
446	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
447	        (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T u).left.appTop).hom
448	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = u.val
449	  -- Unfold `invFun` to expose `T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ofHom liftedMap)` as the
450	  -- underlying scheme morphism.
451	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
452	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
453	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
454	        (IsLocalization.Away.lift
455	          (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop).hom
456	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X ())) = u.val
457	  -- Step (a): morphism equality `(ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ invFun(u).left.appTop = ofHom liftedMap`
458	  -- via `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`, `← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc`,
459	  -- `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop`, `Iso.inv_hom_id`, `Category.comp_id`.
460	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
461	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
462	  -- Step (b)+(c): conclude via `IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X'`.
463	  simp [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
464	
465	/-- Naturality of `homEquiv`: pre-composition by `φ : T' ⟶ T` in `Over (Spec k̄)` corresponds
466	to `Units.map` of `φ.left.appTop` on the units side. Reduces to `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`
467	and the unit-element transfer through `IsLocalization.Away.lift` and the functor's `map` action. -/
468	private lemma gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
469	    {T T' : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} (f : T ⟶ T') (g : T' ⟶ Gm kbar) :
470	    gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T (f ≫ g) =
471	      (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).map f.op (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T' g) := by
472	  apply Units.ext
473	  simp only [gmHomEquiv_toFun, gmHomFunctor, Functor.comp_map, Units.coe_map,
474	    IsUnit.unit_spec, GrpCat.hom_ofHom, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
475	  rfl
476	
477	/-- The `RepresentableBy` witness exhibiting `Gm kbar` as a representing object for the
478	units-of-global-sections functor. Per-`T` bijection is `gmHomEquiv_toFun` / `gmHomEquiv_invFun`;
479	round-trip identities split into `gmHomEquiv_left_inv` / `gmHomEquiv_right_inv` (named
480	substantive sorries) and naturality `homEquiv_comp` (named substantive sorry). The deep
481	Mathlib-gap content per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy — no
482	`Spec(Localization.Away)` analogue of `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` in Mathlib. -/
483	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor_representableBy (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
484	    (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy (Gm kbar) where
485	  homEquiv {T} :=
486	    { toFun := gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T
487	      invFun := gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T
488	      left_inv := gmHomEquiv_left_inv kbar T
489	      right_inv := gmHomEquiv_right_inv kbar T }
490	  homEquiv_comp := gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp kbar
491	
492	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
493	
494	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
495	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
496	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
497	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection).
498	
499	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
500	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
501	`W = Gm`). -/
502	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) :=
503	  GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) (gmHomFunctor kbar) (gmHomFunctor_representableBy kbar)
504	
505	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
506	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
507	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
508	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
509	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
510	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
511	
512	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
513	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
514	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
515	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
516	  η[Gm kbar]
517	
518	end AlgebraicGeometry
519	
520	end

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Coheight ↔ Krull dimension of stalk bridge
10	
11	This file packages the project-side bridge between the **topological coheight**
12	of a scheme point and the **ring-theoretic Krull dimension** of its stalk. It
13	is the iter-183 Lane M deliverable per
14	`analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md` Decision 2, with blueprint chapter
15	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CoheightBridge.tex`.
16	
17	The four declarations are:
18	
19	1. `Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding` — for `X` a topological space and
20	   `U ⊆ X` open, `coheight z (in X) = coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in U)` (both wrt the
21	   specialisation preorder, pinned explicitly via `@`).
22	2. `Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum` — for `R` a `CommRingCat` and
23	   `p : Spec R`, `coheight p (in Spec R) = height ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩
24	   (in PrimeSpectrum R)`.
25	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` — the bridge:
26	   `ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z) = coheight z` for any scheme point.
27	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one` — codim-1
28	   wrapper: `coheight z = 1 → Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk z)`.
29	
30	The first three lemmas are natural Mathlib upstream candidates; the fourth is
31	the project-facing consumer wrapper used in
32	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (`hreg_dim` Krull-dim conjunct) and
33	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` (`Scheme.RationalMap.order` instance argument).
34	-/
35	
36	open Order TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory
37	
38	universe u
39	
40	namespace Order
41	
42	/-- **Open subsets preserve `Order.coheight`** for points of a topological
43	space carrying the specialisation preorder. Both `Order.coheight` instances
44	are pinned to `specializationPreorder` explicitly (via `@`) so the statement
45	is well-defined even when `X` carries no other `Preorder` instance.
46	
47	Forward bound: `Subtype.val` is continuous, hence spec-monotone, and is
48	injective; therefore strictly monotone. Reverse bound: every generisation of a
49	point in an open set lies in that open (`Specializes.mem_open`), so a chain
50	starting at `z` in `X` lifts to a chain in `↥U` of the same length. -/
51	lemma coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding
52	    {X : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] {U : Set X} (hU : IsOpen U)
53	    (z : X) (hz : z ∈ U) :
54	    @Order.coheight X (specializationPreorder X) z
55	      = @Order.coheight U (specializationPreorder U) ⟨z, hz⟩ := by
56	  letI : Preorder X := specializationPreorder X
57	  letI : Preorder ↥U := specializationPreorder ↥U
58	  -- `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is monotone for the spec preorder (since it is
59	  -- continuous), and is injective; hence strictly monotone.
60	  have hmono : Monotone (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) :=
61	    continuous_subtype_val.specialization_monotone
62	  have hstrict : StrictMono (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) := by
63	    intro a b hab
64	    refine ⟨hmono hab.le, fun h => ?_⟩
65	    apply hab.not_ge
66	    change a ⤳ b
67	    exact (subtype_specializes_iff a b).mpr h
68	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
69	  · -- Reverse direction: coheight z (in X) ≤ coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in ↥U).
70	    refine Order.coheight_le_iff'.mpr ?_
71	    intro p hphead
72	    -- Every p i lies in U: p.head ≤ p i in X means p i ⤳ z; combined with
73	    -- `z ∈ U` open, gives p i ∈ U via `Specializes.mem_open`.
74	    have hmem : ∀ i, p i ∈ U := by
75	      intro i
76	      have hle : z ≤ p i := by
77	        have := p.head_le i
78	        rwa [hphead] at this
79	      exact Specializes.mem_open (show p i ⤳ z from hle) hU hz
80	    -- Construct the LTSeries in ↥U.
81	    let q : LTSeries ↥U :=
82	      { length := p.length
83	        toFun := fun i => ⟨p i, hmem i⟩
84	        step := by
85	          intro i
86	          have hlt : p i.castSucc < p i.succ := p.step i
87	          have hspec : (p i.succ) ⤳ (p i.castSucc) := hlt.le
88	          have hsub : (⟨p i.succ, hmem _⟩ : ↥U) ⤳ ⟨p i.castSucc, hmem _⟩ :=
89	            (subtype_specializes_iff _ _).mpr hspec
90	          refine ⟨hsub, fun hbad => ?_⟩
91	          -- hbad : (⟨p i.castSucc, _⟩) ⤳ ⟨p i.succ, _⟩ in ↥U
92	          apply hlt.not_ge
93	          exact (subtype_specializes_iff _ _).mp hbad }
94	    have hqhead : q.head = ⟨z, hz⟩ := by
95	      apply Subtype.ext
96	      change p 0 = z
97	      exact hphead
98	    have hlen : q.length = p.length := rfl
99	    have hbound :=
100	      Order.length_le_coheight (x := (⟨z, hz⟩ : ↥U)) (p := q) (by rw [hqhead])
101	    simpa [hlen] using hbound
102	  · -- Forward direction: coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in ↥U) ≤ coheight z (in X).
103	    have h :=
104	      Order.coheight_le_coheight_apply_of_strictMono
105	        (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) hstrict ⟨z, hz⟩
106	    simpa using h
107	
108	end Order
109	
110	namespace Order
111	
112	/-- **The specialisation preorder on `Spec R` is the dual of the inclusion
113	preorder on `PrimeSpectrum R`.** Coheight in `Spec R` equals height in
114	`PrimeSpectrum R`. -/
115	lemma coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum
116	    {R : CommRingCat} (p : Spec R) :
117	    Order.coheight (α := Spec R) p
118	      = Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum R) ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ := by
119	  -- The order iso `Spec R ≃o (PrimeSpectrum R)ᵒᵈ` built from `spec_le_iff`.
120	  let e : Spec R ≃o (PrimeSpectrum R)ᵒᵈ :=
121	    { toFun := fun q => OrderDual.toDual ⟨q.asIdeal, q.isPrime⟩
122	      invFun := fun q => (OrderDual.ofDual q : PrimeSpectrum R)
123	      left_inv := fun _ => rfl
124	      right_inv := fun _ => rfl
125	      map_rel_iff' := by
126	        intro a b
127	        -- After unfolding the dual order, this becomes
128	        -- `b.asIdeal ≤ a.asIdeal ↔ a ≤ b in Spec R`, i.e. `spec_le_iff`.
129	        exact (AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.spec_le_iff R a b).symm }
130	  have h1 :
131	      Order.coheight (α := (PrimeSpectrum R)ᵒᵈ) (e p)
132	        = Order.coheight (α := Spec R) p :=
133	    Order.coheight_orderIso e p
134	  rw [← h1]
135	  -- `e p = OrderDual.toDual ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩`; reduce via
136	  -- `coheight (toDual x) = height x`.
137	  rfl
138	
139	end Order
140	
141	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
142	
143	namespace Scheme
144	
145	/-- **Coheight ↔ Krull dimension bridge for scheme points.** For any scheme
146	`X` and any point `z : X`, the Krull dimension of the stalk at `z` equals the
147	coheight of `z` in the underlying topological space (with the specialisation
148	preorder). -/
149	theorem ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight
150	    (X : Scheme.{u}) (z : X) :
151	    ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z) = Order.coheight z := by
152	  -- Step 1: pick an affine open U ∋ z.
153	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hzU, _⟩ :=
154	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := z) (U := ⊤)
155	      (by trivial)
156	  -- Step 2: name the prime corresponding to z inside U.
157	  set p : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U) := hU.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzU⟩ with hp
158	  -- Bind the section-to-stalk algebra explicitly so the localisation theorem
159	  -- sees the expected `Algebra` instance.
160	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, U) (X.presheaf.stalk z) :=
161	    TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨z, hzU⟩
162	  -- Step 3: stalk is the localisation of Γ(X,U) at p.
163	  haveI hloc : IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.presheaf.stalk z) p.asIdeal :=
164	    hU.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzU⟩
165	  -- Step 4: ringKrullDim stalk = p.asIdeal.height (= primeHeight p).
166	  have h4 :
167	      ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z)
168	        = (Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) p : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
169	    rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height
170	          (R := Γ(X, U)) p.asIdeal (X.presheaf.stalk z),
171	        Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight]
172	    rfl
173	  -- Step 5: relate height(p) to coheight(z) via Decls 1 + 2 and the
174	  -- homeomorphism `Spec Γ(X,U) ≃ U.toScheme` from `hU.isoSpec`.
175	  -- (5a) Decl 1: coheight z in X = coheight ⟨z, hzU⟩ in U.1 subspace.
176	  have h1' :
177	      @Order.coheight X (specializationPreorder X) z
178	        = @Order.coheight U.1 (specializationPreorder U.1) ⟨z, hzU⟩ :=
179	    Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding (X := X) (U := U.1) U.isOpen z hzU
180	  -- For a Scheme, the standard `Preorder` instance is definitionally
181	  -- `specializationPreorder`. Cast h1' to use the Scheme's preorder on the
182	  -- X side and U.toScheme's preorder on the U side (both defeq).
183	  have h1 :
184	      Order.coheight (α := X) z
185	        = Order.coheight (α := U.toScheme) ⟨z, hzU⟩ := h1'
186	  -- (5b) The scheme iso `hU.isoSpec : U.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(X,U)` is a Scheme
187	  -- iso; the underlying carrier-level map is a homeomorphism, giving an
188	  -- order iso of spec preorders.
189	  let hHomeo : U.toScheme ≃ₜ Spec Γ(X, U) :=
190	    TopCat.homeoOfIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso hU.isoSpec)
191	  let eOrder : U.toScheme ≃o Spec Γ(X, U) :=
192	    { toEquiv := hHomeo.toEquiv
193	      map_rel_iff' := by
194	        intro a b
195	        constructor
196	        · intro h
197	          have hsp : hHomeo b ⤳ hHomeo a := h
198	          have hsp' := hsp.map hHomeo.symm.continuous
199	          rw [hHomeo.symm_apply_apply, hHomeo.symm_apply_apply] at hsp'
200	          exact (hsp' : a ≤ b)
201	        · intro h
202	          have hsp : b ⤳ a := h
203	          exact (hsp.map hHomeo.continuous : hHomeo a ≤ hHomeo b) }
204	  have h2 : Order.coheight (α := U.toScheme) ⟨z, hzU⟩
205	      = Order.coheight (α := Spec Γ(X, U)) (eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩) :=
206	    (Order.coheight_orderIso eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩).symm
207	  have h3 : eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩ = p := rfl
208	  -- (5c) Decl 2: coheight (Spec R) p = height (PrimeSpectrum R) p.
209	  have h6 : Order.coheight (α := Spec Γ(X, U)) p
210	      = Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U))
211	          ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ :=
212	    Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum p
213	  have h7 : (⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) = p := rfl
214	  -- Assemble.
215	  rw [h4, h1, h2, h3, h6, h7]
216	
217	end Scheme
218	
219	end AlgebraicGeometry
220	
221	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
222	
223	namespace Scheme

225	/-- **Codim-1 wrapper: coheight = 1 ⟹ stalk Krull dim ≤ 1.** Consumer-facing
226	specialisation of `ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` for the codim-`1` case used
227	in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` and `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. -/
228	lemma ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one
229	    (X : Scheme.{u}) (z : X) (hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
230	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk z) := by
231	  rw [Ring.krullDimLE_iff, ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight, hz]
232	  -- Goal: ((1 : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ((1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)
233	  norm_cast
234	
235	end Scheme

11	# Cross01 substrate — Lane B (𝔾ₘ-scaling) reusable lemmas
12	
13	This file ships **substrate** lemmas needed to close the Lane B
14	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` cocycle proof (path (III.c) of
15	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`) together with two off-target sorries
16	(`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`) in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`.
17	
18	The substrates are generic in the geometric situation and live in their own
19	file rather than inline so they can be re-used by the three GmScaling consumer
20	applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.  Their construction
21	follows the recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` (§2) and the prover-facing
22	specification at `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`.
23	
24	## Contents (this iter)
25	
26	* `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` —
27	  Substrate 1 (iter-189, axiom-clean): the closed-immersion universal
28	  property phrased as a topological range containment (under reduced

50	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

52	namespace IsClosedImmersion
53	
54	/--
55	**Closed immersion lift by range inclusion.**
56	For a closed immersion `i : Z ⟶ X` whose source `Z` is reduced and a
57	quasi-compact morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` whose source `Y` is reduced, `g` factors
58	through `i` if and only if the topological range of `g` is contained in the
59	topological range of `i`.
60	
61	This is the topological-range form of the universal property of closed
62	immersions; the kernel-inequality form is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` /
63	`IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac`.  The two forms are interchanged by the Galois
64	connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support` on ideal sheaves
65	(`IdealSheafData.gc` / `IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`).
66	
67	This is **Substrate 1** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
68	`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
69	`Theorem~\ref{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}`.
70	-/
71	theorem lift_iff_range_subset
72	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
73	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
74	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔ Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base := by
75	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
76	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: range pushdown.
77	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
78	    rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩
79	    refine ⟨h.base y, ?_⟩
80	    simp [Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
81	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substantive content.
82	    intro hrange
83	    -- Helper: the kernel of a ring hom into a reduced ring is a radical ideal.
84	    have ker_isRadical : ∀ {R S : Type u} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S]
85	        [_root_.IsReduced S] (f : R →+* S), (RingHom.ker f).IsRadical := by
86	      intro R S _ _ _ f x hx
87	      obtain ⟨n, hxn⟩ := hx
88	      rw [RingHom.mem_ker] at hxn ⊢
89	      have h₁ : (f x) ^ n = 0 := by
90	        rw [← map_pow]; exact hxn
91	      exact eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero h₁
92	    -- Step 1: For a closed immersion, the support of its kernel equals its range.
93	    -- (`support_ker` gives `closure (range)`; closed range collapses the closure.)
94	    have hi_range_eq : (i.ker.support : Set X) = Set.range i.base := by
95	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
96	      exact i.isClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range.closure_eq
97	    -- Step 2: `g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support` (set-theoretic, via `closure_minimal`).
98	    have hsup_set : (g.ker.support : Set X) ⊆ (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
99	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
100	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.support.isClosed
101	      exact hrange.trans hi_range_eq.ge
102	    -- Promote the set inclusion to the `Closeds X` ordering.
103	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := hsup_set
104	    -- Step 3: `g.ker` is radical (because `Y` is reduced, so each `Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ U)` is reduced
105	    -- and the ring kernel is therefore radical, pointwise on affine opens).
106	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
107	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
108	      intro U
109	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
110	      exact ker_isRadical (g.app U.1).hom
111	    -- Step 4: `i.ker` is radical (same argument; closed immersions are quasi-compact).
112	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
113	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
114	      intro U
115	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
116	      exact ker_isRadical (i.app U.1).hom
117	    -- Step 5: thread the Galois connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support`.
118	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
119	      calc i.ker
120	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
121	        _ = Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal i.ker.support :=
122	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support.symm
123	        _ ≤ Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal g.ker.support :=
124	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
125	        _ = g.ker.radical := Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support
126	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
127	    -- Step 6: feed the kernel inequality to Mathlib's `IsClosedImmersion.lift`.
128	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker, IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
129	
130	end IsClosedImmersion
131	
132	/--
133	**Substrate 2 — `Away f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄` is a domain.**
134	
135	For a non-zero homogeneous element `f` of positive degree `d` in the
136	`projectiveLineBar` grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, the tensor
137	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading k̄) f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄`
138	is an integral domain.
139	
140	The proof identifies the tensor with the localization
141	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` via the
142	chain of natural isomorphisms in `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3:
143	* `Away f ⊗_{k̄} 𝕜[t] ≃ 𝕜[t][Away f]` (`MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`),
144	* tensor base change with the localization
145	  `𝕜[t] → GmRing k̄` produces `Localization.Away (X ())`
146	  (`IsLocalization.Away.tensorRight`).
147	
148	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` is a domain because
149	it is the localization of `MvPolynomial Unit (Away f)` (a polynomial ring
150	over the domain `Away f`) at the powers of the non-zero generator `X ()`.
151	
152	This is **Substrate 2** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
153	`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
154	`Theorem~\ref{thm:gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}`.
155	-/
156	@[nolint unusedArguments]
157	theorem gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain
158	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] {d : ℕ} (_hd : 0 < d)
159	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
160	    (_hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar d)
161	    (_hf_ne : f ≠ 0) :
162	    IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar
163	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f)
164	      (GmRing kbar)) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- Abbreviation for the homogeneous localization (a `kbar`-algebra and a domain).
167	  let A : Type u := HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f
168	  -- =====================================================================
169	  -- Step 1: `A` is a domain.
170	  -- =====================================================================
171	  -- The bridge `val : A → Localization.Away f` is injective, and we transport
172	  -- domain-ness via `Function.Injective.isDomain`.  This pattern is the same
173	  -- one used inside `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` (`GmScaling.lean` L734-738).
174	  haveI hLocAway_dom :
175	      IsDomain (Localization.Away (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) := by
176	    have hPoly_dom : IsDomain (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) := inferInstance
177	    by_cases hf_zero : f = 0
178	    · -- if f = 0, Localization.Away f is Subsingleton, but we still need IsDomain.
179	      -- Instead, contradict with hf_ne.
180	      exact absurd hf_zero _hf_ne
181	    · exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
182	        (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors hf_zero)
183	  haveI hA_dom : IsDomain A :=
184	    Function.Injective.isDomain (algebraMap A (Localization.Away f))
185	      (fun x y h => HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h)
186	  -- =====================================================================
187	  -- Step 2: `MvPolynomial Unit A` is a domain (polynomial ring over a domain).
188	  -- =====================================================================
189	  haveI hT_dom : IsDomain (MvPolynomial Unit A) := inferInstance
190	  -- =====================================================================
191	  -- Step 3: Build the natural iso
192	  --   `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar) ≃ₐ[A] Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit A)`.
193	  -- =====================================================================
194	  -- Step 3a:  `A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial Unit A`.
195	  -- This is `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` directly.
196	  let e1 : TensorProduct kbar A (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial Unit A :=
197	    MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv kbar A
198	  -- Step 3b: Establish IsLocalization of the target tensor.
199	  -- We use the `Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` to view the tensor as a
200	  -- localization. The plan is to set up the algebra structure correctly
201	  -- so that `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRight` applies.
202	  --
203	  -- Plan: build a `RingEquiv` from `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar)` to
204	  -- `Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit A)`, then transport IsDomain.
205	  --
206	  -- For the iso, we use:
207	  --   `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar) ≃ A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) localized at X()`
208	  --   ≃ `MvPolynomial Unit A` localized at X () (via e1)
209	  --   ≃ `Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit A)`.
210	  --
211	  -- The first iso uses that tensor commutes with localization.
212	  -- We package this via `IsLocalization` instances + uniqueness of localization.
213	  --
214	  -- Concretely, we apply `IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` once we
215	  -- establish that `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar)` is the localization of a domain
216	  -- at a non-zero-divisor submonoid.
217	  --
218	  -- The cleanest path: show `IsLocalization (powers (1 ⊗ X())) (A ⊗[kbar] Gm)`,
219	  -- where the base ring `S := A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` is a domain
220	  -- (via `e1`), and `1 ⊗ X()` is non-zero (corresponding to `X ()` in
221	  -- `MvPolynomial Unit A` via `e1`).
222	  let S : Type u := TensorProduct kbar A (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
223	  haveI hS_dom : IsDomain S :=
224	    Function.Injective.isDomain e1.toRingHom e1.injective
225	  -- The element we localize at: `1 ⊗ X () : S`.
226	  let xS : S := (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
227	  -- `xS ≠ 0`: it maps under `e1` to `X () : MvPolynomial Unit A`, which is non-zero.
228	  have hxS_ne : xS ≠ 0 := by
229	    intro h
230	    have he1 : e1 xS = e1 0 := by rw [h]
231	    have he1' : e1 xS = (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit A) := by
232	      simp [e1, xS, MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv_tmul]
233	    rw [he1'] at he1
234	    simp at he1
235	  -- Now establish: `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar)` is an `IsLocalization` of `S` at `powers xS`.
236	  -- We use `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRight` with a tweak.
237	  --
238	  -- Detail: set up the algebra structures so that S = A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
239	  -- is a `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-algebra via `rightAlgebra`, and then
240	  -- `(GmRing kbar) ⊗[MvPolynomial Unit kbar] S` is a localization of `S`
241	  -- at the image of `powers (X())`, which equals `powers xS`.
242	  --
243	  -- Then identify `(GmRing kbar) ⊗[MvPolynomial Unit kbar] S ≃ A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar)`
244	  -- via base-change cancellation.
245	  --
246	  -- To avoid heavy elaboration, we construct the iso explicitly by
247	  -- universal properties.
248	  letI rightAlg : Algebra (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) S :=
249	    Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
250	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) S := inferInstance
251	  -- A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar) has commuting algebra maps from A and Gm.
252	  -- Define forward: φ : A ⊗[kbar] Gm →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS
253	  -- via TensorProduct.lift on (A → LocAwayxS) and (Gm → LocAwayxS).
254	  -- (b) Gm → LocAwayxS via IsLocalization.Away.lift, using that X () maps to
255	  --     a unit in Localization.Away xS.
256	  -- The base map MvPolynomial Unit kbar → Localization.Away xS sends X () to
257	  -- the image of `xS` in `Localization.Away xS`, which is a unit.
258	  let polkToLocAwayxS : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS :=
259	    (Algebra.ofId S (Localization.Away xS)).restrictScalars kbar |>.comp
260	      (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight :
261	        MvPolynomial Unit kbar →ₐ[kbar] S)
262	  -- Image of X () under polkToLocAwayxS is the image of xS in LocAwayxS.
263	  have hX_unit_polk : IsUnit
264	      (polkToLocAwayxS (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
265	    have h_eq : polkToLocAwayxS (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
266	        = (algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS)) xS := by
267	      show (Algebra.ofId S (Localization.Away xS)).toRingHom
268	        ((Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →ₐ[kbar] S)
269	          (MvPolynomial.X ())) = _
270	      rfl
271	    rw [h_eq]
272	    exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit (S := Localization.Away xS) xS
273	  let f2_ring : GmRing kbar →+* Localization.Away xS :=
274	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar) (P := Localization.Away xS)
275	      (g := polkToLocAwayxS.toRingHom)
276	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hX_unit_polk
277	  -- Promote to AlgHom over kbar.
278	  let f2 : GmRing kbar →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS :=
279	    { f2_ring with
280	      commutes' := fun k => by
281	        show f2_ring _ = _
282	        have step :
283	            (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) k
284	              = (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))
285	                ((algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) k) :=
286	          (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
287	            (GmRing kbar) k).symm
288	        rw [step]
289	        show f2_ring ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) _) = _
290	        rw [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq]
291	        exact polkToLocAwayxS.commutes k }
292	  -- Promote f1 to AlgHom kbar via restrictScalars.
293	  let f1_kbar : A →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS :=
294	    (Algebra.ofId A (Localization.Away xS)).restrictScalars kbar
295	  -- Build φ.
296	  let φ : TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar) →ₐ[kbar] Localization.Away xS :=
297	    Algebra.TensorProduct.lift f1_kbar f2 (fun _ _ => Commute.all _ _)
298	  -- =====================================================================
299	  -- Step 4: Build ψ : Localization.Away xS →+* TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar)
300	  -- =====================================================================
301	  -- First the map S = A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) → target.
302	  -- We use Algebra.TensorProduct.map (AlgHom.id A) (algebraMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar → Gm).
303	  let target : Type u := TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar)
304	  let s_to_target_alg : S →ₐ[kbar] target :=
305	    Algebra.TensorProduct.map (AlgHom.id kbar A)
306	      (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))
307	  let s_to_target : S →+* target := s_to_target_alg.toRingHom
308	  -- Image of xS is `1 ⊗ X()` (where X () is mapped to GmRing kbar), which is a unit.
309	  have hxS_unit_target : IsUnit (s_to_target xS) := by
310	    have h_eq : s_to_target xS = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
311	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
312	          (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
313	      show s_to_target_alg ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = _
314	      simp [s_to_target_alg, Algebra.TensorProduct.map_tmul]
315	      rfl
316	    rw [h_eq]
317	    -- 1 ⊗ (unit element) is a unit. Use the inverse 1 ⊗ (inv element).
318	    refine IsUnit.map
319	      (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : GmRing kbar →ₐ[kbar] target).toRingHom ?_
320	    exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit
321	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
322	  -- Lift to ψ.
323	  let ψ_ring : Localization.Away xS →+* target :=
324	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := Localization.Away xS) (P := target)
325	      (g := s_to_target) xS hxS_unit_target
326	  -- =====================================================================
327	  -- Step 5: Show φ ∘ ψ_ring = id  and  ψ_ring ∘ φ = id on generators.
328	  -- For Step 5, we only need ψ_ring ∘ φ = id (left inverse → injective);
329	  -- then `IsDomain` transfers via `Function.Injective.isDomain` + LocAwayxS domain.
330	  -- =====================================================================
331	  -- First establish that LocAwayxS is a domain.
332	  haveI hLocAwayxS_dom : IsDomain (Localization.Away xS) :=
333	    IsLocalization.isDomain_of_le_nonZeroDivisors (Localization.Away xS)
334	      (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors hxS_ne)
335	  -- =====================================================================
336	  -- Step 5: Show ψ_ring ∘ φ = id (left inverse → φ injective → IsDomain).
337	  -- =====================================================================
338	  -- Helper A: ψ_ring on the image of algebraMap S → Localization.Away xS is s_to_target.
339	  have hψ_alg :
340	      ∀ a : S, ψ_ring ((algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS)) a) = s_to_target a := by
341	    intro a
342	    show IsLocalization.Away.lift xS hxS_unit_target
343	        ((algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS)) a) = _
344	    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq xS hxS_unit_target a
345	  -- Helper B: ψ_ring (f1_kbar a) = a ⊗ 1.
346	  have h_psi_f1 : ∀ a : A, ψ_ring (f1_kbar a) = (a : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (1 : GmRing kbar) := by
347	    intro a
348	    have heq : f1_kbar a = (algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS))
349	        ((a : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
350	      show (algebraMap A (Localization.Away xS)) a = _
351	      rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply A S (Localization.Away xS) a]
352	      rfl
353	    rw [heq, hψ_alg]
354	    show s_to_target_alg ((a : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = _
355	    rw [show s_to_target_alg ((a : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
356	        = (AlgHom.id kbar A) a ⊗ₜ[kbar]
357	          (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) 1 from rfl]
358	    simp
359	  -- Helper C: ψ_ring (f2 b) = 1 ⊗ b. We show ψ_ring ∘ f2 = includeRight via
360	  -- the universal property of `IsLocalization.Away (X()) (GmRing kbar)`.
361	  have h_psi_f2_aux : ∀ p : MvPolynomial Unit kbar,
362	      ψ_ring (f2 ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p))
363	        = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
364	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) p) := by
365	    intro p
366	    -- f2 (algebraMap _ _ p) = polkToLocAwayxS p
367	    have step1 : f2 ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p)
368	        = polkToLocAwayxS p := by
369	      show f2_ring _ = _
370	      exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
371	        hX_unit_polk p
372	    rw [step1]
373	    -- polkToLocAwayxS p = (algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS)) ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ p)
374	    have step2 : polkToLocAwayxS p
375	        = (algebraMap S (Localization.Away xS))
376	            ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] p) := rfl
377	    rw [step2, hψ_alg]
378	    -- s_to_target ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ p) = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ algebraMap _ _ p
379	    show s_to_target_alg ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] p) = _
380	    rw [show s_to_target_alg ((1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] p)
381	        = (AlgHom.id kbar A) 1 ⊗ₜ[kbar]
382	          (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p from rfl]
383	    simp
384	  -- Helper D: ψ_ring (f2 b) = 1 ⊗ b. Two ring homs `GmRing kbar →+* target` agree iff
385	  -- they agree after composing with `algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)`.
386	  have h_psi_f2 : ∀ b : GmRing kbar,
387	      ψ_ring (f2 b) = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[kbar] (b : GmRing kbar) := by
388	    have h_eq_ringhoms : (ψ_ring.comp f2.toRingHom)
389	        = ((Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight :
390	            GmRing kbar →ₐ[kbar] target).toRingHom) := by
391	      refine IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (S := GmRing kbar) (P := target)
392	        (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) ?_
393	      refine RingHom.ext fun p => ?_
394	      show ψ_ring (f2 ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p))
395	        = (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : GmRing kbar →ₐ[kbar] target)
396	            ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) p)
397	      rw [h_psi_f2_aux p]
398	      rfl
399	    intro b
400	    have hb := RingHom.congr_fun h_eq_ringhoms b
401	    -- hb : ψ_ring (f2 b) = includeRight b = 1 ⊗ b
402	    exact hb
403	  -- Main: ψ_ring ∘ φ = id on every tensor element.
404	  have h_left_inv : ∀ x : TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar), ψ_ring (φ x) = x := by
405	    intro x
406	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
407	    | zero =>
408	      have hφ0 : φ (0 : TensorProduct kbar A (GmRing kbar)) = 0 := φ.map_zero
409	      rw [hφ0, ψ_ring.map_zero]
410	    | tmul a b =>
411	      have h_phi : φ (a ⊗ₜ b) = f1_kbar a * f2 b := by
412	        show Algebra.TensorProduct.lift f1_kbar f2 _ (a ⊗ₜ b) = _
413	        rw [Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul]
414	      rw [h_phi]
415	      rw [ψ_ring.map_mul]
416	      rw [h_psi_f1 a, h_psi_f2 b]
417	      rw [Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul]
418	      rw [mul_one, one_mul]
419	    | add x y hx hy =>
420	      have hφxy : φ (x + y) = φ x + φ y := φ.map_add _ _
421	      rw [hφxy, ψ_ring.map_add, hx, hy]
422	  -- Conclude IsDomain via φ injective.
423	  refine Function.Injective.isDomain φ.toRingHom ?_
424	  intro x y hxy
425	  have h1 : ψ_ring (φ x) = ψ_ring (φ y) := by
426	    show ψ_ring (φ.toRingHom x) = ψ_ring (φ.toRingHom y)
427	    rw [hxy]
428	  rw [h_left_inv x, h_left_inv y] at h1
429	  exact h1
430	
431	end AlgebraicGeometry
432	
433	end
434

10	/-!
11	# `Pic⁰_{C/k}` as an abelian variety (A.3.iii–vii)
12	
13	This file is the **iter-193 Pic0AbelianVariety** file-skeleton (NEW lane,
14	blueprint-doctor flagged orphan resolution for the iter-192 plan-phase chapter
15	`Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`). It packages the strategy phase A.3.iii–vi
16	substrate and the A.3.vii assembly into the load-bearing Route~A statement
17	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety, the gate of `thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness`
18	for `g(C) ≥ 1` curves.
19	
20	The chapter consumes the abstract identity-component substrate set up in
21	`Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` (`Pic⁰_{C/k}` as a clopen subgroup scheme of
22	finite type, geometrically irreducible, formation commuting with base change)
23	and adds the curve-specific input: the tangent-space isomorphism
24	`T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`, the resulting smoothness of dimension `g` at
25	the origin (hence everywhere by translation), properness inherited from the
26	projectivity of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` for `C/k` geometrically normal, and the assembly
27	into an abelian variety in the sense of Milne §I.1.
28	
29	## Status (iter-193 NEW file-skeleton)
30	
31	Each of the five blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
32	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
33	`Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`) with a `sorry` body. The bodies are
34	iter-194+ work; iter-193's mandate is the mechanical scaffold only.
35	
36	The 5 pinned declarations are:
37	
38	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (theorem, A.3.iii) — the
39	   canonical isomorphism `T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` (Kleiman §5
40	   Thm.~`thm:tgtsp`). Packaged as an `AddEquiv` between the cotangent space
41	   `m_e/m_e²` at a `k`-rational identity-section point `e` and the project's
42	   first-cohomology `k`-module `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1`
43	   (from `Genus.lean`). The `k`-module structure on the cotangent space at a
44	   `k`-rational point is supplied as part of the existential bundle.
45	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.smooth` (theorem, A.3.iv) — `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is
46	   `Smooth` over `k`. Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Cor.~`cor:ch0`
47	   (characteristic-zero) + Ex.~`ex:jac` (curve case).
48	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.proper` (theorem, A.3.v) — `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is
49	   `IsProper` over `k`. Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (projectivity upgrade for
50	   geometrically normal `C/k`).
51	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (theorem, A.3.vi)
52	   — `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is `GeometricallyIrreducible` over `k`. Specialisation of
53	   `IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` (sibling) to
54	   `G = PicScheme C`. Kleiman §5 Prp.~`prp:pic0`.
55	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.isAbelianVariety` (theorem, A.3.vii) —
56	   `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety: `IsProper ∧ Smooth ∧
57	   GeometricallyIrreducible ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)`. Milne §I.1, p.~8
58	   (defining axioms) + Kleiman §5 Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`. Load-bearing A.3.vii gate
59	   of Route~A.
60	
61	## Note on type expressivity
62	
63	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
64	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
65	
66	- `tangentSpaceIso C` — existentially quantifies over a `k`-rational
67	  identity-section point `e : Spec k ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left` and a `k`-module
68	  structure on the cotangent space `m_e/m_e²` (the `k`-module structure
69	  arising via the `k`-algebra structure on the stalk at a `k`-rational point;
70	  for the file-skeleton this Module instance is bundled as part of the Σ').
71	  Bundles an `AddEquiv` between the cotangent space and the
72	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`-as-`k`-module. The body is iter-194+ work.
73	- `smooth C`, `proper C`, `geometricallyIrreducible C` — genuine `Prop`-valued
74	  statements on the structural morphism `(Pic0Scheme C).hom : Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶
75	  Spec k`, not tautological.
76	- `isAbelianVariety C` — assembles the conjunction of the four
77	  abelian-variety properties (proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible,
78	  group-object structure); not vacuous because each conjunct is a genuine
79	  property/structure on the (typed-sorry) `Pic0Scheme C`.
80	
81	## Coordination with `Pic0Scheme`
82	
83	The underlying `k`-scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is supplied by
84	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme C` from sibling
85	`Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` (`def:pic_zero_subscheme` in the blueprint).
86	We do not redefine the underlying scheme here; the `Pic0` namespace below
87	collects the curve-specific abelian-variety facts about `Pic0Scheme C`.
88	
89	## References
90	
91	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`
92	(735 LOC, 5 pins). Sources:
93	- Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §5, Thm.~`thm:tgtsp` (tangent space),
94	  Cor.~`cor:sm` + Cor.~`cor:ch0` (smoothness), Thm.~`th:qpp&p`
95	  (quasi-projectivity/projectivity), Prp.~`prp:pic0` (irreducibility),
96	  Rmk.~`rmk:Jac` (assembly to abelian scheme); arXiv:math/0504020.
97	- Milne, "Abelian Varieties" (course notes, 2008), §I.1, p.~8
98	  (definition of abelian variety) + Rmk. III.1.4(e) (dimension equals genus).
99	-/

111	/-! ## §1. The `Pic0` namespace
112	
113	The blueprint chapter pins five Lean declarations under the
114	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0` namespace. Their underlying scheme
115	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is `Pic0Scheme C` from sibling `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`;
116	the declarations below collect the curve-specific abelian-variety facts
117	about `Pic0Scheme C` (tangent space at the identity, smoothness, properness,
118	geometric irreducibility, abelian-variety assembly). -/
119	
120	namespace Pic0
121	
122	/-- **Tangent space at the identity: `T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.**
123	
124	The Kleiman §5 Thm.~`thm:tgtsp` tangent-space isomorphism. For a smooth
125	proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the Zariski tangent space at the
126	identity of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is canonically isomorphic to the first sheaf
127	cohomology `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.
128	
129	In Lean (skeleton form): we bundle existentially over a `k`-rational
130	identity-section point `e : Spec k ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left` and a `k`-module
131	structure on the cotangent space `m_e/m_e²` (the natural `k`-module structure
132	arising from the `k`-algebra structure on the stalk at a `k`-rational point;
133	for the file-skeleton this instance is supplied via Σ' to avoid hard-coding
134	a global instance prematurely). The substantive content is an `AddEquiv`
135	between the cotangent space and the project's `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` module
136	`Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1`.
137	
138	A k-LinearEquiv refinement (iter-194+) replaces the `AddEquiv` once the
139	`k`-module structure on the cotangent space is consistently threaded through
140	downstream consumers; the underlying additive group structure is enough for
141	the dimension corollary `dim_k T₀ Pic⁰ = dim_k H¹ = g(C)`. -/
142	theorem tangentSpaceIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
143	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
144	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
145	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
146	    Nonempty (Σ' (e : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left),
147	      IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace ((Pic0Scheme C).left.presheaf.stalk (e.base default))
148	        ≃+ Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
149	  sorry
150	
151	/-- **Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
152	
153	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
154	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is smooth over `k` (Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Cor.~`cor:ch0` in
155	characteristic zero + Ex.~`ex:jac` for the curve case).
156	
157	The proof (iter-194+): smoothness at the identity propagates to smoothness
158	everywhere by translation (Pic⁰ is a `k`-group scheme, so multiplication by
159	any `λ ∈ Pic⁰(k̄)` is an automorphism). At the identity, smoothness reduces
160	to the inequality
161	`dim_0 Pic⁰_{C/k} ≤ dim_k T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} = dim_k H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = g(C)`
162	(`tangentSpaceIso`) becoming an equality. For a smooth proper curve this
163	equality holds: in characteristic zero by Cartier's theorem (every
164	`k`-group scheme is smooth), in positive characteristic by the curve-specific
165	vanishing `H²(C, 𝒪_C) = 0` (the obstruction to lifting tangent vectors). -/
166	theorem smooth {k : Type u} [Field k]
167	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
168	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
169	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
170	    Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom :=
171	  sorry
172	
173	/-- **Properness of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
174	
175	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
176	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is proper over `k` (Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p`; a smooth proper
177	curve is geometrically normal, hence the projectivity upgrade applies).
178	
179	The proof (iter-194+): `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is quasi-projective (Kleiman §5
180	Thm.~`th:qpp&p`, first conclusion); to upgrade to projective (hence proper)
181	we use the Chevalley–Rosenlicht structure theorem to reduce to ruling out
182	non-constant `k̄`-morphisms `𝔾_m → Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. The latter follows from
183	normality of `C ×_k 𝔾_m` and Hartshorne II Ex.~6.15 (invertible sheaves on
184	a normal integral scheme are sheaves of Cartier divisors). -/
185	theorem proper {k : Type u} [Field k]
186	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
187	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
188	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
189	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom :=
190	  sorry
191	
192	/-- **Geometric irreducibility of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
193	
194	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
195	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is geometrically irreducible over `k` (Kleiman §5 Prp.~`prp:pic0`,
196	specialisation of the abstract identity-component substrate
197	`IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` of
198	`Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` to `G = PicScheme C`).
199	
200	The proof (iter-194+): apply `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible`
201	to `G = PicScheme C` (locally of finite type by `FGAPicRepresentability`), and
202	identify `IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` with `Pic0Scheme C` via the
203	`Pic0Scheme` definition (sibling). -/
204	theorem geometricallyIrreducible {k : Type u} [Field k]
205	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
206	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
207	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
208	    GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom :=
209	  sorry
210	
211	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety (A.3.vii assembly).**
212	
213	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity
214	component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety over `k`: the underlying
215	`k`-scheme is smooth (`Pic0.smooth`), proper (`Pic0.proper`), and
216	geometrically irreducible (`Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible`), and it carries
217	a `k`-group-scheme structure inherited from `Pic_{C/k}` via the inclusion
218	`Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}`
219	(`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism`). Commutativity is
220	automatic (Milne §I.1, Cor.~1.4).
221	
222	Milne §I.1, p.~8: "A complete connected group variety is called an abelian
223	variety." This is the load-bearing A.3.vii gate of Route~A for
224	`thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness`.
225	
226	The proof (iter-194+) assembles the four conjuncts: `proper` (this file),
227	`smooth` (this file), `geometricallyIrreducible` (this file), and the
228	`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` slot, the latter from
229	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism` applied to
230	`G = PicScheme C` (sibling `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`). -/
231	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
232	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
233	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
234	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
235	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
236	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
237	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
238	  sorry
239	
240	end Pic0
241	
242	end Scheme
243	
244	end AlgebraicGeometry

11	/-!
12	# The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (RR.2_*)
13	
14	This file is the **RR.2_*** satellite sub-build chapter for the project's
15	headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
17	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
18	
19	Together with `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`), `RR.2`
20	(`RRFormula.lean`), `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`), and `RR.4`
21	(`RationalCurveIso.lean`), the present chapter forms the four-stage
22	sub-build closing the project's headline **RR bridge**.
23	
24	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain consumed by `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` of
25	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` relies on the invertible-sheaf functor
26	`𝒪_C(-) : Div(C) → 𝐒𝐡(C, 𝐌𝐨𝐝_k̄)` sending a Weil divisor
27	`D = Σᵢ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` to its associated invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` on `C`.
28	
29	This file hosts the four pinned declarations of the chapter
30	`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` — the invertible sheaf
33	   `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve `C / k̄`,
34	   realised as Hartshorne's subsheaf of the function-field constant
35	   sheaf `K_C` (II §6 p. 144). Replaces the iter-174 typed-`sorry`
36	   placeholder currently living at `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean:168`.
37	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_zero` — the zero
38	   divisor `D = 0` gives the structure sheaf `𝒪_C(0) = 𝒪_C`.
39	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_singlePoint` — the
40	   closed-point specialisation `𝒪_C([P]) = lineBundleAtClosedPoint P`
41	   of `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`).
42	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_ses_single_add` — the
43	   Hartshorne IV.1.3 inductive-step short exact sequence
44	   `0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0` consumed by Lane H's
45	   `RRFormula` induction.
46	
47	## Status (iter-183 Lane K file-skeleton)
48	
49	This file is the **iter-183 Lane K** file-skeleton: each declaration
50	carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
51	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`).
52	All bodies are `sorry`; the iter-184+ closure follows the recipe in
53	chapter §"Sheaf-property correctness" (subsheaf-of-`K_C` per-open
54	constraint set, identity-on-`K(C)` restrictions, sheaf property via
55	stalk-locality of the order conditions). The construction is
56	`noncomputable`.
57	
58	**3-tier disclosure** (per iter-181 vocabulary): each of the four
59	declarations is a **Tier-3 honest typed sorry** — the body is a
60	substantive mathematical construction (Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C`)
61	whose closure is iter-184+ work; the types encode genuine claims about
62	the invertible sheaf, its specialisations, and its SES additivity.
63	
64	## References
65	
66	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`.
67	Source: Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II §6 pp. 144–145 (definition of
68	`ℒ(D)`; Propositions 6.13, 6.15, 6.18; Remark 6.17.1) and IV §1 p. 296
69	(the `D ↝ D + [Y]` SES). Stacks Project tags 02RW (Weil divisors),
70	0AUW (sheaf `𝒪_X(D)`), 0BE3 (degree-zero of a principal divisor).

73	set_option autoImplicit false

75	universe u

77	open CategoryTheory Limits TopCat
78	open scoped Classical

80	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
