82	/-! ## §1. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor -/
83	
84	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
85	
86	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
87	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
88	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
89	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
90	
91	/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth
92	proper curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144, definition of `ℒ(D)`).
93	
94	For a Weil divisor `D = Σ_Q n_Q · [Q] ∈ Div(C)` on a smooth proper
95	geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the invertible sheaf
96	`𝒪_C(D) := ℒ(D)` is the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant
97	sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` (Hartshorne Proposition 6.15: on an integral scheme
98	the sheaf of total quotient rings is the constant sheaf at the function
99	field) defined section-wise on each open `U ⊆ C` as
100	`Γ(U, 𝒪_C(D)) = { f ∈ K(C) | f = 0 ∨ ord_Q(f) ≥ −n_Q ∀ prime divisor Q
101	∈ U }`, with restriction along `V ⊆ U` given by the identity on `K(C)`.
102	
103	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
104	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf carrier used
105	by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so that `H⁰` and
106	`H¹` of `𝒪_C(D)` are accessible via
107	`Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf D) 0/1`).
108	
109	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The iter-184+
110	body recipe (per chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` §"Sheaf-property
111	correctness"): per-open `Submodule kbar K(C)` cut out by the order
112	conditions (the closure proofs reduce to `Ring.ordFrac`-multiplicativity
113	and the non-archimedean inequality of the DVR valuation at each prime
114	divisor); presheaf functor via the identity-on-`K(C)` restriction; sheaf
115	property via gluing-by-stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at
116	each prime divisor). The construction parallels the project's existing
117	`Scheme.toModuleKPresheaf` / `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` template in
118	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/`.
119	
120	**Coordination with `RRFormula.lean:168`.** The iter-174 typed-`sorry`
121	placeholder `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` in `RRFormula.lean` is
122	slated to be retired (Lane H) by re-export of this declaration. Both
123	files compile in isolation; cross-imports are coordinated by Lane H.
124	
125	Blueprint reference: `def:sheafOf` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144;
126	Propositions 6.13, 6.15; Remark 6.17.1).
127	
128	**iter-185 Lane K body fragment.** The case `D = 0` is implemented on the
129	nose as the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (Hartshorne's
130	``\(\mathcal O_C(0) = \mathcal O_C\)'' identification; chapter
131	`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `lem:sheafOf_zero`). The general case
132	remains an honest typed `sorry` pending the iter-186+ Hartshorne
133	subsheaf-of-`K_C` construction described in the docstring above. The
134	`if`-branching uses `Classical.dec` (via `open Classical in`) on the
135	`WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)` carrier, which has no canonical
136	decidable equality. -/
137	noncomputable def sheafOf (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
138	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
139	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
140	  open Classical in
141	  if D = 0 then Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C else sorry
142	
143	/-! ## §2. Immediate corollaries -/
144	
145	/-- **Sheaf of the zero divisor is the structure sheaf**
146	(chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `sheafOf_zero`).
147	
148	At `D = 0` the coefficient `n_Q = 0` at every prime divisor `Q`, so the
149	section condition of `sheafOf` reduces to the standard
150	"non-negative-order = regular" identification of the structure sheaf on
151	an integral scheme (Hartshorne II §6 immediately before Proposition 6.11).
152	Both sides are sub-`𝒪_C`-modules of `𝒦_C` with identity restriction
153	maps, so the equality of presheaves promotes to an equality of sheaves
154	of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules.
155	
156	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The body closes
157	on iter-184+ closure of `sheafOf` together with the standard `Γ(U, 𝒪_C)`
158	identification.
159	
160	**iter-185 Lane K body**: closes via the explicit `D = 0` branch in the
161	`sheafOf` def above (modified iter-185 to land the structure-sheaf value
162	on the `0` divisor on the nose). Unfolding `sheafOf` exposes the
163	`if 0 = 0 then toModuleKSheaf C else sorry` and `if_pos rfl` picks the
164	true branch.
165	
166	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_zero`. -/
167	lemma sheafOf_zero :
168	    sheafOf (C := C) (0 : C.left.WeilDivisor) = Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C := by
169	  unfold sheafOf
170	  exact if_pos rfl
171	
172	/-- **Sheaf at a single closed point is the line bundle of `RR.3`**
173	(chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `sheafOf_singlePoint`).
174	
175	For a closed point `P ∈ C` viewed as a Weil divisor `[P] ∈ Div(C)` via
176	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint`, the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C([P])` of
177	`sheafOf` agrees on the nose with the closed-point line bundle
178	`lineBundleAtClosedPoint P` of `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`).
179	
180	The agreement is by unfolding both definitions: at `D = [P]`, the order
181	conditions of `sheafOf` (`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P`, `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`)
182	are exactly those characterising sections of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint P`
183	(via `lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff` of `OCofP.lean`).
184	
185	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. Closes on
186	iter-184+ closure of both `sheafOf` and `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`.
187	
188	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_singlePoint`. -/
189	lemma sheafOf_singlePoint [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
190	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
191	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
192	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
193	    sheafOf (C := C) (ofClosedPoint P hP) =
194	      lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh := by
195	  sorry
196	
197	/-- **Short exact sequence for `D ↝ D + [P]`** (Hartshorne IV.1.3
198	inductive step, p. 296; chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma
199	`sheafOf_ses_single_add`).
200	
201	For a Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)` and a prime divisor `P` of `C`
202	(equivalently, a closed point on a smooth curve), the inclusion
203	`𝒪_C(D) ↪ 𝒪_C(D + [P])` of sub-`𝒪_C`-modules of `𝒦_C` fits into a short
204	exact sequence
205	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`
206	in `Sh(C, 𝐌𝐨𝐝_k̄)`, where `k(P)` is the skyscraper sheaf at `P` with
207	stalk `k̄`.
208	
209	The statement bundles the existence of a `ShortComplex` whose `X₁ ↦ X₂`
210	arm matches `sheafOf D ↪ sheafOf (Finsupp.single P 1 + D)` and that is
211	`ShortExact` (mono + epi + exact). The third term `X₃` is the
212	skyscraper `k(P)` — encoded here as a (nonempty) isomorphism class with
213	`Mathlib`'s `skyscraperSheaf` (decidability of `P.point ∈ U` supplied
214	classically). Lane H's `RRFormula` consumes this sequence via
215	χ-additivity to derive `χ(𝒪_C(D + [P])) = χ(𝒪_C(D)) + 1`.
216	
217	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The iter-184+
218	body recipe (per chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` §"Immediate
219	corollaries", Beat 1 + Beat 2): build the ideal-sheaf SES
220	`0 → 𝒪_C(−[P]) → 𝒪_C → k(P) → 0`, tensor with `𝒪_C(D + [P])` (which is
221	locally free of rank 1, hence the tensor is exact), and identify the
222	three terms via `𝒪_C(−[P]) ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ 𝒪_C(D)` (Hartshorne 6.13(b)),
223	`𝒪_C ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ 𝒪_C(D + [P])` (tensor unit), and
224	`k(P) ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ k(P)` (rank-1 stalk at `P`).
225	
226	The argument `D` carries the underlying `Finsupp` type rather than
227	`Scheme.WeilDivisor` so that the `Finsupp.single P 1 + D` term elaborates
228	cleanly (the addition is in the unambiguous Finsupp instance); the result
229	is consumed via the definitional equality `WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀
230	ℤ)`, matching the pattern of `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add`
231	in `RRFormula.lean`.
232	
233	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_ses_single_add` (Hartshorne IV.1, p. 296). -/
234	theorem sheafOf_ses_single_add
235	    (D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor) :
236	    ∃ S : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
237	        (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
238	          (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)),
239	      S.ShortExact ∧
240	      S.X₁ = sheafOf (C := C) D ∧
241	      S.X₂ = sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single P 1 + D) ∧
242	      Nonempty (S.X₃ ≅ skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar)
243	        P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) := by
244	  sorry
245	
246	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
247	
248	end AlgebraicGeometry

12	/-!
13	# The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero (RR.2)
14	
15	This file is the **RR.2** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
16	headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically
17	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
18	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
19	
20	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
21	through:
22	
23	- `RR.1` (`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` / `WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil
24	  divisor group `Div(C)` and the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
25	- **`RR.2` (this file, `RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`)**: the Riemann–Roch
26	  dimension formula `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0` (with `deg D ≥ 0`),
27	  via the auxiliary Euler-characteristic identity
28	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`.
29	- `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, future): the invertible sheaf
30	  `𝒪_C(D)`, the linear-equivalence isomorphism `𝒪_C(D) ≅ 𝒪_C(D')` for
31	  `D ∼ D'`, and the `H¹`-vanishing input
32	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0` for `deg D ≥ 0` on a genus-`0` curve.
33	- `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`, future): the "two-section
34	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
35	
36	## Status (iter-174 Lane F file-skeleton)
37	
38	This file is the **iter-174 Lane F** file-skeleton: each of the four pinned
39	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
40	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`). The
41	Euler-characteristic carrier definition is concrete (a one-line subtraction
42	of `H⁰` and `H¹` `Module.finrank`s, mirroring the `genus` definition of
43	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus`); the remaining pins carry `sorry` bodies whose
44	closure is iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters `RR.3`
45	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`) and `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`) land.
46	
47	The 4 pinned declarations are:
48	
49	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic` — Euler characteristic
50	   `χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)` of a `ModuleCat k̄`-valued
51	   sheaf on `C` (the curve specialisation of the alternating sum, since
52	   `H^i = 0` for `i ≥ 2` on a one-dimensional scheme by Grothendieck
53	   vanishing).
54	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l` — the `ℓ`-invariant
55	   `ℓ(D) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D))` of a Weil divisor `D`.
56	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`
57	   — the Euler-characteristic identity `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` for
58	   every `D ∈ Div(C)` on a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
59	   `C / k̄` of genus `g = g(C)`.
60	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero`
61	   — the Riemann–Roch formula in genus `0`: `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` for any
62	   Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)` with `deg D ≥ 0` on a smooth proper
63	   geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` with `g(C) = 0` (threading
64	   the `H¹`-vanishing of `𝒪_C(D)` explicitly as a named premise until
65	   `RR.3` lands).

102	/-! ## §1. The Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve -/
103	
104	/-- **Euler characteristic of a `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf on a smooth proper
105	curve `C / k̄`.**
106	
107	On a curve (one-dimensional scheme), Grothendieck vanishing
108	(Hartshorne III.2.7) gives `H^i(C, 𝓕) = 0` for `i ≥ 2`, so the classical
109	alternating sum
110	`χ(𝓕) = Σ_{i ≥ 0} (-1)^i dim_{k̄} H^i(C, 𝓕)` collapses to the two-term
111	expression
112	`χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)`.
113	
114	This is the definition we ship. Coherence of `𝓕` on the proper `k̄`-scheme
115	`C` guarantees that both `H⁰` and `H¹` are finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector
116	spaces (Serre's coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem, the same
117	finiteness backing `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`), so the two `Module.finrank`s
118	are honest natural numbers and the difference is a well-defined integer.
119	
120	The `Module k̄`-valued cohomology pipeline is the project's
121	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009), the same wrapper used by
122	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`.
123	
124	Blueprint reference: `def:eulerChar_curve`
125	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295, displayed inside the proof of Theorem 1.3). -/
126	noncomputable def Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
127	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
128	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
129	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
130	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
131	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
132	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) : ℤ :=
133	  (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 0) : ℤ)
134	    - (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 1) : ℤ)
135	
136	/-! ## §2. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (sibling chapter)
137	
138	The honest construction of `𝒪_C(D)` lives in the sibling chapter `RR.3`
139	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OcOfD.lean`),
140	where the locally-principal ideal sheaves of closed points are glued
141	into an invertible `𝒪_C`-module. **iter-183 Lane K** opened that file
142	with the typed-`sorry` pin `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` plus the three
143	immediate corollaries `sheafOf_zero`, `sheafOf_singlePoint`, and
144	`sheafOf_ses_single_add`. **iter-183 Lane H** (this file) retires the
145	former local `sheafOf` typed-`sorry` placeholder (previously at L168 in
146	the iter-174 skeleton) by importing `OcOfD.lean`; downstream pins (the
147	`ℓ`-invariant, the χ-identity, the genus-`0` Riemann–Roch formula) now
148	reference the chapter's canonical `sheafOf` directly. -/
149	
150	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
151	
152	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
153	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
154	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
155	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
156	
157	/-! ## §3. The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor -/
158	
159	/-- **The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve
160	`C / k̄`.**
161	
162	By definition,
163	`ℓ(D) := dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D)) ∈ ℕ`,
164	the `k̄`-dimension of the space of global sections of the invertible sheaf
165	`𝒪_C(D)` associated to `D`. Finiteness of `ℓ(D)` is a consequence of the
166	coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem on a proper `k̄`-scheme
167	(Hartshorne II.5.19 / III.5.2), the same input that backs the finiteness
168	of `genus C`.
169	
170	The classical *complete linear system* `|D|` has `k̄`-projective dimension
171	`ℓ(D) − 1`; the chapter never uses this projective interpretation —
172	`ℓ(D)` is the only quantity consumed by the Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain.
173	
174	Blueprint reference: `def:l_invariant` (Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295,
175	"We denote `dim_k H⁰(X, 𝒪(D))` by `l(D)`"). -/
176	noncomputable def l (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) : ℕ :=
177	  Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf (C := C) D) 0)
178	
179	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
180	
181	/-! ## §4. The χ-identity: `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`
182	
183	The bridge from the structural Euler characteristic to the arithmetic
184	degree of a divisor. Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3 reduces Riemann–Roch to
185	this identity; the proof is the inductive `D ↔ D + [P]` step (additivity
186	of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence, with base case `D = 0`
187	giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` from `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` and the definition of
188	the genus). The closure of the body is iter-175+ work after the
189	`Euler-characteristic additivity on a short exact sequence`-style
190	project-side helper is supplied and the `RR.3` sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` has a
191	body.
192	
193	**Iter-181 Lane H factoring.** Both Hartshorne IV.1.3 inputs (the base
194	case `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` and the inductive step `χ(𝒪_C(D + Y)) = χ(𝒪_C(D))
195	+ n`) intrinsically reference the body of `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf`,
196	which is still a typed-`sorry` placeholder waiting on the sibling
197	chapter `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`). We therefore factor the proof
198	into two named substantive helper lemmas (3-tier disclosure: **honest
199	named-sorry helpers** — each helper has a substantive type encoding a
200	nontrivial mathematical claim about the `sheafOf` line bundle whose
201	closure is downstream of the `RR.3` body):
202	
203	1. `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` — base case `χ(sheafOf 0) = 1 − g`,
204	   which on closure of `RR.3` reduces to `sheafOf 0 = toModuleKSheaf C`
205	   plus the Hartshorne I.3.4 input `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` plus the
206	   definition of the genus.
207	2. `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` — inductive step
208	   `χ(sheafOf (Finsupp.single Y n + D)) = χ(sheafOf D) + n` for any
209	   `Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor` and `n : ℤ`. On closure of `RR.3` this
210	   reduces to the Hartshorne IV.1.3 SES additivity argument iterated
211	   `|n|` times (with sign for `n < 0`).
212	
213	The main theorem then closes by induction on the `Finsupp`-structure of
214	`D : C.left.WeilDivisor` (via `Finsupp.induction`), using the two
215	helpers and the additivity of `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree`. -/
216	
217	/-- **Hartshorne I.3.4 bridge** (Lane H helper A — iter-183).
218	On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the global
219	sections of the structure sheaf form a one-dimensional `k̄`-vector space:
220	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1`.
221	
222	This is the Hartshorne~I.3.4 statement "for any projective variety `X`
223	over an algebraically closed field `k`, `H⁰(X, 𝒪_X) ≅ k`" specialised to
224	the project's `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured cohomology pipeline
225	(`Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0`). The closure lives
226	in the project's `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK` chapter (the H⁰
227	bridge from the constant-sheaf adjunction) and is gated on the
228	remaining cohomology-API work in that file.
229	
230	**iter-183 Lane H status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. Body iter-184+

295	/-- **χ-additivity on a SES of `ModuleCat kbar`-valued sheaves** (Lane H
296	sub-helper — iter-186).
297	
298	For a short exact sequence `0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` of sheaves on the
299	curve `C`, the Euler characteristic is additive:
300	`χ(S.X₂) = χ(S.X₁) + χ(S.X₃)`.
301	
302	**Proof outline (queued infrastructure).** Apply the project-side
303	`ModuleCat kbar`-flavoured covariant Ext-LES of Mathlib's
304	`Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence` to `L = (constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of
305	kbar kbar)`, getting the long exact sequence
306	`H⁰(F) → H⁰(G) → H⁰(H) → H¹(F) → H¹(G) → H¹(H) → H²(F) → H²(G) → H²(H)`.
307	Apply Grothendieck vanishing `H^i = 0` for `i ≥ 2` on a curve to truncate
308	to the 6-term form; rank-counting on a 6-term exact sequence of
309	finite-dimensional `kbar`-vector spaces gives the alternating identity
310	`finrank H⁰(F) − finrank H⁰(G) + finrank H⁰(H) − finrank H¹(F) +
311	finrank H¹(G) − finrank H¹(H) = 0`,
312	which rearranges to
313	`(finrank H⁰(G) − finrank H¹(G)) =
314	  (finrank H⁰(F) − finrank H¹(F)) + (finrank H⁰(H) − finrank H¹(H))`,
315	i.e. `χ(G) = χ(F) + χ(H)`.

320	vanishing for `HModule kbar _ i` with `i ≥ 2` on a curve, and (c) the
321	6-term alternating-rank identity on `kbar`-finite-dimensional vector
322	spaces. -/

337	/-- **Iso-invariance of the Euler characteristic** (Lane H sub-helper —
338	iter-186).
339	
340	If `F ≅ G` in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat kbar)`, then `χ(F) = χ(G)`. The proof
341	constructs an explicit `kbar`-linear equivalence
342	`HModule kbar F n ≃ₗ[kbar] HModule kbar G n` for each `n ∈ {0, 1}` via
343	`Abelian.Ext.postcompOfLinear` applied to `Ext.mk₀ e.hom` /
344	`Ext.mk₀ e.inv`; the mutual-inverse identities reduce, via
345	`comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero` + `mk₀_comp_mk₀` + `Iso.hom_inv_id` /
346	`Iso.inv_hom_id` + `comp_mk₀_id`, to identities on `Ext` elements.
347	`LinearEquiv.finrank_eq` then transports the `kbar`-dimension. -/
348	private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_iso
349	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
350	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
351	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
352	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
353	    {F G : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
354	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)}
355	    (e : F ≅ G) :
356	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C F = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C G := by
357	  -- The proof works uniformly at any cohomological degree `n`: build a
358	  -- `kbar`-linear equivalence `HModule kbar F n ≃ₗ[kbar] HModule kbar G n`.
359	  have hEquiv : ∀ n : ℕ, Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F n)
360	      = Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar G n) := by
361	    intro n
362	    have hEqv :
363	        Scheme.HModule kbar F n ≃ₗ[kbar] Scheme.HModule kbar G n := by
364	      refine LinearEquiv.ofLinear
365	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ e.hom).postcompOfLinear kbar _ (add_zero n))
366	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ e.inv).postcompOfLinear kbar _ (add_zero n))
367	        ?_ ?_
368	      · -- `f ∘ g = id` on `HModule kbar G n`: α ↦ α.comp (mk₀ e.inv).comp (mk₀ e.hom) = α.
369	        ext α
370	        change (α.comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ e.inv) (add_zero n)).comp
371	            (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ e.hom) (add_zero n) = α
372	        rw [Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero,
373	          Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀, e.inv_hom_id]
374	        exact Abelian.Ext.comp_mk₀_id _
375	      · -- `g ∘ f = id` on `HModule kbar F n`: α ↦ α.comp (mk₀ e.hom).comp (mk₀ e.inv) = α.
376	        ext α
377	        change (α.comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ e.hom) (add_zero n)).comp
378	            (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ e.inv) (add_zero n) = α
379	        rw [Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero,
380	          Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀, e.hom_inv_id]
381	        exact Abelian.Ext.comp_mk₀_id _
382	    exact hEqv.finrank_eq
383	  unfold Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
384	  rw [hEquiv 0, hEquiv 1]

604	private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add
605	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
606	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
607	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
608	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
609	    (D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) (Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
610	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
611	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y n + D))
612	      = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
613	          (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) + n := by
614	  refine Int.induction_on (motive := fun m : ℤ =>
615	      Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
616	          (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y m + D))
617	        = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
618	            (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) + m) n ?_ ?_ ?_
619	  · -- n = 0
620	    simp
621	  · -- positive step: motive (↑k) → motive (↑k + 1)
622	    intro k ih
623	    have hsplit :
624	        (Finsupp.single Y ((k : ℤ) + 1) + D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
625	          = Finsupp.single Y 1 + (Finsupp.single Y (k : ℤ) + D) := by
626	      rw [show ((k : ℤ) + 1) = (1 + (k : ℤ)) from by ring,
627	        Finsupp.single_add, add_assoc]
628	    have hstep := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ
629	      C (Finsupp.single Y (k : ℤ) + D) Y
630	    have h1 : Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
631	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C)
632	          (Finsupp.single Y ((k : ℤ) + 1) + D))
633	        = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
634	            (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C)
635	              (Finsupp.single Y 1 + (Finsupp.single Y (k : ℤ) + D))) :=
636	      congrArg (fun e => Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
637	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) e)) hsplit
638	    linarith [h1, hstep, ih]
639	  · -- negative step: motive (-↑k) → motive (-↑k - 1)
640	    intro k ih
641	    have hsplit :
642	        (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ)) + D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
643	          = Finsupp.single Y 1 +
644	              (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ) - 1) + D) := by
645	      rw [← add_assoc, ← Finsupp.single_add]
646	      congr 2
647	      ring
648	    have hpred := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ
649	      C (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ) - 1) + D) Y
650	    -- hpred: χ(sheafOf (single Y 1 + (single Y (-k-1) + D))) = χ(sheafOf (single Y (-k-1) + D)) + 1
651	    -- Combine via hsplit and congr 1 to bridge dot-notation vs full-name display:
652	    have key : Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
653	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ)) + D))
654	        = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
655	            (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C)
656	              (Finsupp.single Y 1 + (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ) - 1) + D))) :=
657	      congrArg (fun e => Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
658	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) e)) hsplit
659	    -- ih: χ(sheafOf (single Y (-k) + D)) = χ(sheafOf D) + (-k)
660	    -- key: χ(sheafOf (single Y (-k) + D)) = χ(sheafOf (single Y 1 + (single Y (-k - 1) + D)))
661	    -- hpred: χ(sheafOf (s Y 1 + (s Y (-k-1) + D))) = χ(sheafOf (s Y (-k-1) + D)) + 1
662	    -- Goal: χ(sheafOf (single Y (-k - 1) + D)) = χ(sheafOf D) + (-k - 1)
663	    linarith [key, hpred, ih]
664	
665	/-- **Euler-characteristic identity for `𝒪_C(D)` on a smooth proper curve
666	of genus `g`.**
667	
668	For every Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)`,
669	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g(C)`.
670	
671	The proof is Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's reduction: induction on the
672	free-abelian-group structure of `Div(C)` on closed points, base case `D =
673	0` giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` (the `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` is the Hartshorne
674	I.3.4 input via the project's `H⁰`-bridge), and inductive step via
675	additivity of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence
676	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`.
677	
678	Blueprint reference: `thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus`
679	(Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3, p. 295).
680	
681	**Iter-181 Lane H status** — the main theorem is closed by induction on
682	the `Finsupp` structure of `D`, consuming the two `sheafOf`-side helper
683	sorries `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` (base case) and
684	`eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` (inductive step). The body
685	itself is honest; its `sorryAx`-transitivity is the inevitable
686	consequence of `sheafOf` still being a typed sorry waiting on `RR.3`. -/
687	theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus
688	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
689	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
690	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
691	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
692	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
693	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D)
694	      = (Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D) + 1 - (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℤ) := by
695	  -- Unfold `WeilDivisor` to expose the underlying `Finsupp` structure so
696	  -- `Finsupp.induction` applies directly.
697	  unfold Scheme.WeilDivisor at D
698	  induction D using Finsupp.induction with
699	  | zero =>
700	    -- D = 0: χ(sheafOf 0) = 1 - g by helper 1, and degree 0 = 0.
701	    exact (Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero C).trans
702	      (by simp [Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree])
703	  | single_add Y n D' _hY _hn hD' =>
704	    -- D = single Y n + D': use helper 2 then the inductive hypothesis.
705	    rw [Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add, hD']
706	    -- Goal: (degree D' + 1 - g) + n = degree (single Y n + D') + 1 - g.
707	    have hdeg : Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree
708	        ((Finsupp.single Y n + D' : C.left.WeilDivisor))
709	        = n + Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D' := by
710	      change ((Finsupp.single Y n + D' : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)).sum
711	          (fun _ z => z) = _
712	      classical
713	      rw [Finsupp.sum_add_index (fun _ _ => rfl) (fun _ _ _ _ => rfl)]
714	      simp [Finsupp.sum_single_index]
715	      rfl
716	    linarith
717	
718	/-! ## §5. The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero -/
719	
720	/-- **Riemann–Roch in genus zero (Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5).**
721	
722	Let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over the
723	algebraically closed field `k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, and let `D ∈ Div(C)` with
724	`deg D ≥ 0`. Then
725	`ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1`.
726	
727	The proof specialises the χ-identity
728	`eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` to `g = 0`,
729	unfolds `χ` via `def:eulerChar_curve`, and absorbs the `H¹`-vanishing
730	hypothesis (named premise `_hH1`: `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0`, which is the
731	`H¹`-vanishing of a non-negative-degree invertible sheaf on a
732	genus-`0` curve, to be discharged by `RR.3` once `𝒪_C(D)` has a body and
733	the cohomology of `𝒪_{ℙ¹}(d)` is computed).
734	
735	Blueprint reference: `thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero` (Hartshorne IV.1
736	Example 1.3.5, p. 297). -/
737	theorem Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero
738	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
739	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
740	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
741	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
742	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0)
743	    (_hdeg : (0 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D)
744	    (_hH1 : Module.finrank kbar
745	      (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) 1) = 0) :
746	    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.l (C := C) D : ℤ)
747	      = Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D + 1 := by
748	  have h := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus C D
749	  simp only [Scheme.eulerCharacteristic, _hg, _hH1,
750	    Nat.cast_zero, sub_zero] at h
751	  exact h
752	
753	end AlgebraicGeometry

96	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
97	
98	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
99	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
100	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
101	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
102	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
103	
104	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
105	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
106	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
107	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
108	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
109	This is the strictly-weaker, sheafified-tensor-free formulation that
110	Mathlib's `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes
111	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); it is
112	equivalent to the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` predicate under the
113	dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. See
114	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the iter-178 consult that
115	identified this idiom.
116	
117	Blueprint reference: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras` (Stacks 01LL,
118	situation-relative-spec). -/
119	
120	/-- A **quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras** (iter-179 Block A,
121	Mathlib-aligned form).
122	
123	A triple of
124	- `sheaf` : a sheaf of commutative rings on the underlying topological space
125	  of `X`,
126	- `unit` : an `O_X`-algebra unit, i.e. a morphism of sheaves of commutative
127	  rings `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` from the structure sheaf to the carrier, and
128	- `coequifibered` : the Stacks-01LL form of quasi-coherence — every
129	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is the
130	  `IsLocalization.Away f` of `sheaf U`. This is the exact predicate
131	  consumed by `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
132	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
133	
134	The shape matches the input of Mathlib's relative-gluing construction
135	verbatim, so `RelativeSpec` is defined directly as
136	`(relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`. The
137	`NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full
138	sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
139	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`, but equivalent under the dense-subsite
140	equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`, so no information is lost. See
141	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2 for the consult that
142	identified this carrier shape. -/
143	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
144	  /-- The underlying sheaf of commutative rings on `X`. -/
145	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace
146	  /-- The `O_X`-algebra unit `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` exhibiting `sheaf` as a sheaf
147	  of `O_X`-algebras. -/
148	  unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf
149	  /-- **Stacks 01LL quasi-coherence overlay (`Coequifibered` form)**: every
150	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
151	  Strictly weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` (which needs
152	  sheafified-tensor infrastructure not yet in Mathlib); equivalent under the
153	  dense-subsite equivalence `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the
154	  exact predicate consumed by `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
155	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); see
156	  `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2. -/
157	  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered
158	    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)
159	
160	namespace RelativeSpec

162	/-! ## §2. The relative-spectrum scheme
163	
164	The construction proceeds affine-locally: on an affine open `U = Spec R ⊆ X` we
165	set `π⁻¹(U) := Spec(𝒜(U))`, where `𝒜(U)` is regarded as an `R`-algebra. The local
166	pieces glue compatibly because `𝒜` is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
167	`𝒜(U) ⊗_R S ≅ 𝒜(V)` for `V = Spec S ⊆ U` gives an open immersion of the
168	corresponding Specs).
169	
170	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_exists` (Stacks 01LQ
171	lemma-glue-relative-spec). -/
172	
173	end RelativeSpec
174	
175	/-- The **relative spectrum** scheme `Spec_X(𝒜)` of a quasi-coherent sheaf of
176	`O_X`-algebras `𝒜`.
177	
178	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
179	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`
180	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102`,
181	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`). The
182	`coequifibered` field of `QcohAlgebra` is precisely the Stacks-01LL form of
183	quasi-coherence that Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` consumes: the affine
184	restriction of the `O_X`-algebra unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. every
185	basic-open restriction is an `IsLocalization.Away`. The construction glues the
186	affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` along the directed affine open cover
187	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X` and is the exact Mathlib-aligned
188	template used in `Hom.normalization`
189	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`); see
190	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the consult that identified this
191	idiom. -/
192	noncomputable def RelativeSpec {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
193	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
194	
195	/-- The **structure morphism** `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum.
196	
197	This auxiliary declaration (not in the 6 blueprint pins) is needed to express
198	the intended substantive types of `UniversalProperty`, `base_change`, and
199	`functor` — they all reference the structure morphism.
200	
201	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
202	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`
203	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`). The map is the colimit
204	descent of the natural transformation `Spec(𝒜(U)) → U` over the directed
205	affine open cover; see `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 3.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: implicit in `thm:relative_spec_exists`. -/
208	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism {X : Scheme.{u}}
209	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
210	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase
211	
212	/-! ### Note: `QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor (iter-179 Lane B helper)
213	
214	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
215	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
216	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
217	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
218	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
219	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
220	`coequifibered` field is the Mathlib-gap claim that pushforward along an
221	affine morphism preserves the Stacks-01LL Coequifibered overlay
222	(Stacks 01LR pullback compatibility): `q` is affine via
223	`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` applied to `UniversalProperty 𝒜`, so on
224	every affine `U ⊆ T` with section `f ∈ Γ(T, U)` we have
225	`Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(f))) = Γ(P, q⁻¹U)[1/q.app f]`, i.e. the basic-open restriction
226	of the pushforward is the relevant `IsLocalization.Away`. iter-180 Lane C
227	factors this into two named helpers
228	(`QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`,
229	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`) declared after the universal
230	property has landed; the definition itself is also placed after the
231	namespace so it can consume those helpers. -/
232	
233	namespace RelativeSpec
234	
235	/-! ## §3. Universal property — affine structure morphism
236	
237	The Stacks 01LQ universal property says `Spec_X(𝒜)` represents the functor
238	sending an `X`-scheme `g : T → X` to the set of `O_X`-algebra maps
239	`𝒜 → g_* O_T`. A direct structural consequence of representability is that the
240	structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is *affine* (Stacks 01LR
241	lemma-spec-properties, immediate corollary). For the iter-173 file-skeleton we
242	encode the universal property by this affine-morphism consequence — the
243	substantive content is the same up to body unfolding, and the type is
244	non-tautological.
245	
246	iter-174+: refine the signature to a `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy`
247	witness against the functor of `O_X`-algebra maps once `QcohAlgebra` is
248	unpacked and the Hom-set on algebras is available.
249	
250	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_univ` (Stacks 01LQ lemma-spec). -/
251	
252	/-- **Universal property of the relative spectrum (affine-structure form).**
253	
254	The structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum is affine.
255	This is the substantive consequence of the Stacks 01LQ representability
256	statement (an `X`-scheme is the relative spectrum of some quasi-coherent
257	algebra iff its structure morphism is affine).
258	
259	iter-174+: refine the type signature to the full Yoneda-bijection statement
260	`Hom_X(T, Spec_X(𝒜)) ≃ Hom_{O_X-alg}(𝒜, g_* O_T)` once `QcohAlgebra` is
261	unpacked and an `O_X`-algebra Hom-set is in scope. The current type is the
262	non-tautological structural consequence used downstream by `affine_base_iff`
263	and `base_change`. -/
264	theorem UniversalProperty {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
265	    IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := by
266	  -- Mathlib `relativeGluingData` builder; per `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
267	  -- Block B. We invoke `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`: for each `x : X`
268	  -- pick an affine open `U ∋ x` (every `X` has such by `exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`),
269	  -- and identify the structure-morphism preimage of `U` with the range of the colimit
270	  -- inclusion of the affine fiber `Spec(𝒜(U))` (via
271	  -- `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`); affineness of the
272	  -- opens-range then follows from `isAffineOpen_opensRange` since the source is
273	  -- `Scheme.Spec` of `𝒜.sheaf.val.obj (op U)`, an affine scheme.
274	  apply isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen
275	  intro x
276	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hxU, _⟩ :=
277	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (U := ⊤) (Set.mem_univ x)
278	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU, ?_⟩
279	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered
280	  let i : X.AffineZariskiSite := ⟨U, hU⟩
281	  change IsAffineOpen (d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U)
282	  have key : d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U = (d.cover.f i).opensRange := by
283	    have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι i
284	    simpa [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
285	  rw [key]
286	  have : IsAffine (d.cover.X i) := by
287	    change IsAffine (Scheme.Spec.obj _)
288	    infer_instance
289	  exact isAffineOpen_opensRange _
290	
291	/-! ## §4. Affine base case
292	
293	When the base `X = Spec R` is affine, `Spec_X(𝒜)` reduces to the absolute
294	spectrum of the global sections: `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`. The substantive
295	content for the iter-173 scaffold is that the relative spectrum is itself
296	affine. This is Stacks 01LO lemma-spec-affine.
297	
298	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_affine_base`. -/
299	
300	/-- **Affine-base reduction of the relative spectrum.**
301	
302	For an affine scheme `X = Spec R` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`,
303	the relative spectrum `Spec_X(𝒜)` is itself an affine scheme. (More precisely,
304	there is a canonical isomorphism `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`, but extracting
305	`Γ(X, 𝒜) : CommRingCat` requires the unpacked structure of `QcohAlgebra`,
306	which is iter-174+ work.)
307	
308	iter-174+: refine to the full statement
309	`Nonempty ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜 ≅ Spec (Γ((Spec R), 𝒜)))`
310	once `Γ` for `QcohAlgebra` is in scope. -/
311	theorem affine_base_iff {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (𝒜 : (Spec R).QcohAlgebra) :
312	    IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜) := by
313	  -- Affineness of the relative-spec total space when the base is affine is the
314	  -- standard "affine over affine is affine" consequence: by
315	  -- `UniversalProperty` the structure morphism is `IsAffineHom`, and `Spec R`
316	  -- is itself `IsAffine`, so `isAffine_of_isAffineHom` closes the goal.
317	  have h : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := UniversalProperty 𝒜
318	  exact isAffine_of_isAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
319	
320	end RelativeSpec
321	
322	/-! ## §4.5 `QcohAlgebra.pullback` helpers (iter-180 Lane C)
323	
324	Two named helpers feeding the `coequifibered` field of the `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
325	constructor below. Helper budget = 2 per iter-180 plan. -/
326	
327	/-- **Affineness of the structural pullback projection.**
328	
329	Given `g : T ⟶ X` and `𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra`, the projection
330	`q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜) : (T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜)) ⟶ T` is an affine
331	morphism. Combined with `RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜` (which exhibits the
332	relative-spec structure morphism as affine) and the fact that affineness is
333	stable under base change (`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` on `@isAffineHom`),
334	this is purely an instance derivation. -/
335	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
336	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
337	    IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
338	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) := by
339	  haveI : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
340	    RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜
341	  exact MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ inferInstance
342	
343	/-- **Stacks 01LL Coequifibered overlay for the pushforward of `O_P` along an
344	affine `q : P ⟶ T`** (iter-180 Lane C, Stacks 01LR pushforward compatibility).
345	
346	Per the Mathlib characterization
347	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`, the
348	claim reduces to: for every affine open `U ⊆ T` and section `r : Γ(T, U.1)`,
349	the basic-open restriction `q_* O_P (D(r)) = Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(r)))` is the
350	`IsLocalization.Away` of `q.app r` over `Γ(P, q⁻¹U)`. The named helper
351	`pullback_fst_isAffineHom` exhibits `q` as affine, so every preimage
352	`q⁻¹U` of an affine open `U` is itself affine; combined with
353	`Scheme.Hom.preimage_basicOpen` (which identifies `q⁻¹(T.basicOpen r)` with
354	`P.basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)`), `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`
355	supplies the localization with the desired algebra structure (restriction
356	along the inclusion `q⁻¹(U.basicOpen r) ⊆ q⁻¹U` induced by
357	`q.preimage_mono` on the AffineZariski-site inclusion). -/
358	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
359	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
360	    NatTrans.Coequifibered ((AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor T).op.whiskerLeft
361	      ({ hom := (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
362	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).c } :
363	        T.sheaf ⟶ (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u}
364	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
365	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).base).obj
366	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g
367	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf).hom) := by
368	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
369	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
370	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
371	  refine AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway.mpr
372	    fun U r => ?_
373	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
374	  have hqU : IsAffineOpen (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) := U.2.preimage q
375	  have hle : (U.basicOpen r).toOpens ≤ U.toOpens := T.basicOpen_le r
376	  exact hqU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)
377	    (homOfLE (q.preimage_mono hle)) (q.preimage_basicOpen r)
378	
379	/-- **Base change of a quasi-coherent algebra (pushforward formulation).**
380	
381	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
382	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
383	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
384	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
385	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
386	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
387	`coequifibered` field is supplied by the named helper
388	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`, which packages the Stacks-01LR
389	pushforward-along-affine-morphism compatibility. -/
390	noncomputable def QcohAlgebra.pullback {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
391	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : T.QcohAlgebra :=
392	  let q : CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ⟶ T :=
393	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
394	  { sheaf := (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u} q.base).obj
395	      (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf
396	    unit := ⟨q.c⟩
397	    coequifibered := QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered g 𝒜 }
398	
399	namespace RelativeSpec
400	
401	/-! ## §5. Base change
402	
403	The relative spectrum commutes with base change: for a morphism `g : T → X`
404	and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the pullback `g^* 𝒜` is a
405	quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra and
406	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
407	
408	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_base_change` (Stacks 01LS
409	lemma-spec-base-change). -/
410	
411	/-! ### Helpers for `base_change` (iter-179 Lane B + iter-181 Lane D)
412	
413	The base-change theorem packages the canonical iso content into named helpers
414	(`pullback_iso_affine_piece`, `pullback_iso_construction`, and `pullback_iso`,
415	below) and consumes the file-level `Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor
416	declared just above the namespace. -/
417	
418	/-- **Per-affine-open identification for the base-change iso (iter-181 Lane D
419	helper 1, axiom-clean).**
420	
421	For an affine open `U : T.AffineZariskiSite`, the preimage
422	`q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1` under `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` is itself affine
423	(since `q` is `IsAffineHom` by `QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`). Its
424	canonical iso with `Spec(Γ(pullback, q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`)
425	matches `Spec((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.val(.op U.1))`
426	**by definitional unfolding** of the pushforward sheaf
427	(`(QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf := (pushforward q.base).obj (pullback _).sheaf`,
428	so its value at `U` is `(pullback _).sheaf.val(.op (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` defeq).
429	
430	This is the per-affine-piece iso feeding into the global `pullback_iso`
431	construction. Body is a single `(U.2.preimage q).isoSpec` invocation. -/
432	noncomputable def pullback_iso_affine_piece {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
433	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) (U : T.AffineZariskiSite) :
434	    ((CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
435	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅
436	      Scheme.Spec.obj (.op ((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.obj.obj (.op U.1))) :=
437	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
438	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
439	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
440	  (U.2.preimage _).isoSpec
441	
442	/-! ### iter-183 Lane D structural decomposition
443	
444	Five helpers feeding `pullback_iso_construction`:
445	
446	* `pullback_iso_affine_piece` (already declared above, axiom-clean) — per-affine
447	  iso `(q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅ Spec((g^*𝒜)(U))`.
448	* `pullback_cocone` — cocone on `(relativeGluingData _).functor` with point
449	  `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` whose components are the `fromSpec` maps
450	  of the pulled-back affine opens.
451	* `pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst` — the cocone descent composed with the
452	  pullback projection equals the relative-gluing-data structure morphism;
453	  the load-bearing identification that makes the open-cover argument work.
454	* `pullback_iso_desc_isIso` — the cocone descent is an iso, via the local
455	  affine-open argument on `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`.
456	* `pullback_iso_construction` — final assembly: `asIso desc |>.symm`.
457	-/
458	
459	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
460	/-- **Cocone on `(relativeGluingData _).functor` with point
461	`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`** (iter-183 Lane D helper 3,
462	axiom-clean modulo naturality unfolding).
463	
464	The components are the `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec` maps of the pulled-back affine
465	opens `q⁻¹U.1`. Naturality follows from `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` once
466	the relative-gluing-data functor's `map` action is unfolded as
467	`Spec.map (P.presheaf.map ((q.preimage_mono ...).op))`. The unfolding chase
468	is intricate (deep definitional unfolding of `pushforward` and `rightOp`);
469	deferred to iter-184+ as the only remaining work for axiom-clean closure
470	of the entire base-change iso. -/
471	noncomputable def pullback_cocone {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
472	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
473	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
474	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
475	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
476	    Limits.Cocone (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
477	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor :=
478	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
479	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
480	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
481	  { pt := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
482	    ι :=
483	    { app := fun U => (U.2.preimage (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
484	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜))).fromSpec
485	      naturality := fun U V x => by
486	        -- After unfolding `relativeGluingData.functor.map` through its
487	        -- `rightOp ⋙ Spec` template and the pushforward sheaf, the goal
488	        -- becomes `Spec.map ((pullback _ _).presheaf.map _) ≫
489	        --   (V.2.preimage q).fromSpec = (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec ≫ 𝟙 _`,
490	        -- which is `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` + `Category.comp_id`.
491	        set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
492	          (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
493	        simp only [AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData, Functor.comp_obj,
494	          Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map, Functor.const_obj_obj,
495	          Functor.const_obj_map]
496	        rw [Category.comp_id]
497	        exact (V.2.preimage q).map_fromSpec (U.2.preimage q) _ } }
498	
499	/-- **The descent composed with `q` equals `d.toBase`** (iter-183 Lane D
500	helper 4, axiom-clean).
501	
502	The pullback-cocone descent `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) ⟶ pullback g _`, composed
503	with the pullback projection `q := pullback.fst _ _`, equals the relative-
504	gluing-data's structure morphism `d.toBase`. Proof: `colimit.hom_ext`, then
505	the per-component identity uses `SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec`. -/
506	lemma pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
507	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
508	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
509	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
510	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
511	    haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
512	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
513	        Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
514	      Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
515	    Limits.colimit.desc _ (pullback_cocone g 𝒜) ≫
516	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) =
517	      (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
518	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).toBase := by
519	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
520	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
521	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
522	  haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
523	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
524	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
525	    Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
526	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
527	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
528	  refine Limits.colimit.hom_ext fun U => ?_
529	  rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.colimit.ι_desc, d.ι_toBase U]
530	  change (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec ≫ q = _
531	  rw [← U.2.SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec q (U.2.preimage q) le_rfl,
532	    Scheme.Hom.appLE_eq_app, ← IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_inv_ι, ← Category.assoc]
533	  rfl
534	
535	/-- **The descent map is an isomorphism** (iter-183 Lane D helper 5,
536	substantive — axiom-clean modulo upstream `pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst`
537	and per-piece restrict-iso identification).
538	
539	The canonical descent map `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) ⟶ pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`
540	defined by the cocone `pullback_cocone g 𝒜` is an isomorphism. Proof:
541	`IsZariskiLocalAtTarget` for the isomorphism property applied to the
542	affine open cover `{q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1}` of `pullback g _`. The preimage of each
543	piece under `desc` matches `(colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange` by
544	`pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst` + `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`,
545	and the local restriction is then a composition of three explicit isos. -/
546	lemma pullback_iso_desc_isIso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
547	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
548	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
549	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
550	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
551	    haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
552	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
553	        Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
554	      Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
555	    IsIso (Limits.colimit.desc _ (pullback_cocone g 𝒜)) := by
556	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
557	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
558	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
559	  haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
560	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
561	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
562	    Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
563	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
564	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
565	  set desc := Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜) with desc_def
566	  refine (IsZariskiLocalAtTarget.iff_of_iSup_eq_top
567	    (P := MorphismProperty.isomorphisms Scheme)
568	    (fun U : T.AffineZariskiSite => q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) ?_).mpr fun U ↦ ?_
569	  · -- iSup of preimages = preimage of iSup = preimage of ⊤ = ⊤.
570	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.preimage_iSup]
571	    convert Scheme.Hom.preimage_top q using 2
572	    exact iSup_affineOpens_eq_top T
573	  · -- Per-piece: `desc ∣_ q⁻¹U.1` is iso. Following the template at
574	    -- `SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone` (file
575	    -- `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:343`), reduce
576	    -- to `IsIso (pullback.snd desc (q⁻¹U.1).ι)` and build an iso
577	    -- `pullback desc (q⁻¹U.1).ι ≅ d.functor.obj U` via range matching using
578	    -- the key fact `hPre`.
579	    have hPre : desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 = (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange := by
580	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage, pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst g 𝒜]
581	      have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι U
582	      simpa [AffineZariskiSite.directedCover, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
583	    -- The per-piece factorisation: `desc ∣_ q⁻¹U.1` factors as three isos:
584	    --   (1) `Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre`:
585	    --       `(desc⁻¹q⁻¹U.1).toScheme ≅ (colim.ι U).opensRange.toScheme`
586	    --   (2) `colim.ι U .isoOpensRange.symm`:
587	    --       `(colim.ι U).opensRange.toScheme ≅ d.functor.obj U`
588	    --   (3) `(pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).symm`:
589	    --       `d.functor.obj U ≅ Spec((g^*𝒜)(U)) ≅ (q⁻¹U.1).toScheme`
590	    --       (the inverse via `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec.inv ≫ ι`).
591	    -- Commute the chain through `(q⁻¹U.1).ι` by canceling the mono ι.
592	    let iso_chain : (desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme :=
593	      Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre ≪≫
594	        (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.symm ≪≫
595	        (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).symm
596	    suffices h : desc ∣_ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 = iso_chain.hom by
597	      change IsIso (desc ∣_ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1)
598	      rw [h]; exact iso_chain.isIso_hom
599	    -- Verify equality by post-composing with `(q⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι` (which is mono).
600	    have h_paff_inv : (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).inv ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι =
601	        (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec := (U.2.preimage q).isoSpec_inv_ι
602	    have h_cocone : (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec =
603	        Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U ≫ desc := by
604	      change _ = Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U ≫
605	        Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜)
606	      rw [Limits.colimit.ι_desc]
607	      rfl
608	    have h_post : (desc ∣_ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1)) ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι =
609	        iso_chain.hom ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι := by
610	      refine (morphismRestrict_ι desc (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1)).trans ?_
611	      calc (desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι ≫ desc
612	          = ((Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
613	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange.ι) ≫ desc := by
614	            rw [Scheme.isoOfEq_hom_ι]
615	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
616	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange.ι ≫ desc := by
617	            rw [Category.assoc]
618	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
619	              ((Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.inv ≫
620	              Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U) ≫ desc := by
621	            rw [(Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange_inv_comp]
622	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
623	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.inv ≫
624	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U ≫ desc) := by
625	            rw [Category.assoc]
626	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
627	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.inv ≫
628	              (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).inv ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι := by
629	            rw [← h_cocone, ← h_paff_inv]; rfl
630	        _ = iso_chain.hom ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι := by
631	            simp [iso_chain, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Category.assoc]
632	    exact (cancel_mono (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι).mp h_post
633	
634	/-- **Canonical base-change iso construction** (iter-181 Lane D helper 2,
635	iter-183 Lane D structural close).
636	
637	Builds the iso `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅ T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜)`.
638	
639	**Construction**: the cocone `pullback_cocone g 𝒜` on the relative-gluing-data
640	functor of `g^*𝒜` has point `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The colimit
641	descent map `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) ⟶ pullback g _` from this cocone is an
642	isomorphism by `pullback_iso_desc_isIso`; the result is the inverse iso. -/
643	noncomputable def pullback_iso_construction {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
644	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
645	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
646	      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) := by
647	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
648	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
649	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
650	  haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
651	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
652	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
653	    Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
654	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
655	  let desc : T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) ⟶
656	      CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
657	    Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜)
658	  haveI : IsIso desc := pullback_iso_desc_isIso g 𝒜
659	  exact (asIso desc).symm
660	
661	/-- **Canonical base-change isomorphism (Nonempty form).** The pullback
662	`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` (formed in `Scheme`) is canonically
663	isomorphic to the relative spectrum of the pulled-back qcoh algebra
664	`QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜`. Witnessed by `pullback_iso_construction`. -/
665	theorem pullback_iso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
666	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
667	    Nonempty (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
668	              T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜)) :=
669	  ⟨pullback_iso_construction g 𝒜⟩
670	
671	/-- **Base change of the relative spectrum.**
672	
673	For a morphism `g : T → X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, there
674	exists a quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra `𝒜' = g^* 𝒜` and a canonical isomorphism
675	of `T`-schemes `T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
676	
677	iter-179 body (Block B): witnessed by the named helpers `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
678	and `pullback_iso`. -/
679	theorem base_change {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X) (_𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
680	    ∃ (𝒜' : T.QcohAlgebra),
681	      Nonempty (pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism _𝒜) ≅
682	                  T.RelativeSpec 𝒜') :=
683	  ⟨QcohAlgebra.pullback g _𝒜, pullback_iso g _𝒜⟩
684	
685	/-! ## §6. Functoriality
686	
687	The construction `𝒜 ↦ Spec_X(𝒜)` extends to a contravariant functor
688	`Spec_X : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Over X`. The iter-173 file-skeleton encodes the
689	object-level functorial assignment as `X.QcohAlgebra → Over X`; the
690	morphism-level action and full `Functor` packaging are iter-174+ work after
691	`QcohAlgebra` is unpacked to a category.
692	
693	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_functorial` (Stacks 01LR
694	definition-relative-spec + lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec). -/
695	
696	/-- **The relative-spectrum functor (object level).**
697	
698	The object-level functorial action `𝒜 ↦ Over.mk (π_𝒜) : Over X`, packaging
699	the relative spectrum together with its structure morphism over `X`.
700	
701	iter-174+: the body is concrete via `Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)`
702	but is left as `sorry` here because `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` is itself
703	a typed `sorry`; once the structure morphism lands the body collapses to
704	`fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The full categorical functor
705	`X.QcohAlgebra ⥤ Over X` (with `map` action induced by the universal property)
706	becomes expressible once `QcohAlgebra` carries its category structure. -/
707	noncomputable def functor (X : Scheme.{u}) :
708	    X.QcohAlgebra → Over X :=
709	  fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
710	
711	end RelativeSpec
712	
713	end Scheme
714	
715	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme (A.2.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` — a projective `S`-scheme representing the functor of
15	`T`-flat coherent quotients of `E_T` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with Hilbert
16	polynomial `Φ` on every fiber — together with the in-project sub-build for
17	the Grassmannian *scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a
18	linear-algebra Grassmannian).
19	
20	## Status (iter-176 Lane H file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
21	
22	iter-175 Lane H died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
23	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
24	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations
25	carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
26	`\lean{...}` pin in `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`) with a
27	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
28	deep (Nitsure §5: boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening
29	stratification ⟶ valuative criterion).
30	
31	The 6 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
34	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
35	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
36	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
37	
38	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def, ~6 LOC) — the **Quot
39	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
40	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
41	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
42	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
43	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
46	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
47	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
48	
49	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
50	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
51	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
52	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
53	
54	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
55	   **Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman representability theorem** for the Quot
56	   functor: for a noetherian `S`, a projective `π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively
57	   very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent `E`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the functor
58	   `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is representable by a projective `S`-scheme.
59	
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
61	   **flat base-change theorem of cohomology** (Stacks tag 02KH): for a
62	   cartesian square with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact quasi-separated,
63	   the canonical base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an
64	   isomorphism. The current scaffold encodes the `i = 0` direct-image
65	   form (substantive content of (ii) of the Stacks 02KH statement); the
66	   `R^i f_*` form for `i ≥ 1` requires the higher-direct-image
67	   infrastructure not present at the pinned commit.
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
72	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
75	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
76	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
77	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
78	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
79	- `Grassmannian.representable` and `QuotScheme` package the
80	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
81	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
82	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
83	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
84	  that isomorphism family).
85	- `flatBaseChangeCohomology` produces a `Nonempty (... ≅ ...)` of an
86	  isomorphism between two `S'`-modules built via the pullback/pushforward
87	  bifunctor; the iso is non-trivial (it is `Stacks 02KH` content, not
88	  the identity-on-the-same-object iso `Iso.refl _`).

9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.

46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
47	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
48	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
49	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
50	
51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
58	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
59	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
60	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).
61	
62	## Abelian-variety conventions
63	
64	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
65	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
66	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
67	
68	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
69	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
70	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
71	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
72	
73	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
75	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
76	
77	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
78	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
79	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
80	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
81	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
82	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
83	
84	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
85	the call site.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
90	(250 LOC, 1 pin). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.2,
91	p. 17 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, PDF page 23).
92	-/

99

9	/-!
10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
29	follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
30	consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).
31	
32	## Status (iter-177 Lane 7 file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-177 Lane 7** file-skeleton. Each of the six
35	blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
36	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
38	
39	The 6 pinned declarations are:
40	
41	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the
42	   regular morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
43	   `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`. Encoded against the file-internal
44	   placeholder `Pic0.jacobianScheme` for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (the A.3 upstream
45	   has not yet been split out; see §0 below).
46	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.SymmetricPower` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) —
47	   the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C` of the curve `C`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
48	   Body gated on iter-178+ `SymmetricPower.lean` substrate (Milne III.3
49	   Proposition 3.1; Mumford 1970 II.7 / III.11 affine-and-glue recipe).
50	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerAVMap` (noncomputable def, ~5 LOC) —
51	   the symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` produced by
52	   `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` from an input `φ : C ⟶ A`.
53	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerToJacobian` (noncomputable def,
54	   ~5 LOC) — the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
55	   `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`.
56	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.descentThroughBirationalSigma` (theorem,
57	   ~10 LOC) — the descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism
58	   `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` via Milne Theorem 3.2.
59	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.albanese_universal_property` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
60	   the headline UP theorem (Milne Proposition III.6.1).
61	
62	## File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
63	
64	The A.3 row of Route A (the identity-component refinement of the Picard
65	scheme together with its degree map) has not yet been split out into a
66	dedicated chapter or `.lean` file at the time of this file-skeleton
67	landing. We supply a **single helper** here: a `Pic0.Bundle` structure
68	bundling the underlying scheme together with its four abelian-variety
69	instances (`GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `Smooth`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`),
70	plus a typed-`sorry` carrier `Pic0.bundle C` and a derived definition
71	`Pic0.jacobianScheme C := (bundle C).scheme`. The four instances on
72	`jacobianScheme C` are derived from the bundle's fields (no extra
73	`sorry`). Once the A.3 chapter materialises this placeholder collapses to
74	a one-line re-export.
75	
76	Helper budget = 1: the single helper carrying a `sorry` is `Pic0.bundle`.
77	
78	## Note on type expressivity
79	
80	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
81	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
82	
83	- `abelJacobi C P₀ : C ⟶ jacobianScheme C` — a genuine morphism into the
84	  Pic⁰ scheme (not `𝟙 C` or any tautology).
85	- `SymmetricPower C g : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` — a `k̄`-scheme, not `C`
86	  itself or `𝟙_`.
87	- `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A` — a morphism into
88	  the abelian variety `A`.
89	- `symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C`
90	  — a morphism into Pic⁰.
91	- `descentThroughBirationalSigma` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
92	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying
93	  `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g ≫ ψ`.
94	- `albanese_universal_property` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
95	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ`.
96	
97	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
98	(a morphism into the Pic⁰ scheme, a unique factorisation through the
99	Abel–Jacobi map, …); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` /
100	empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.
